vocabularies.definition: An area outside a window where you can stand or sit.
vocabularies.example:
There are flowers on the balcony.
I like sitting on the balcony in the evening.
Our apartment has a small balcony.
quizzes: I like to sit on the __________ and look at the garden.
Our apartment has a small _________ with many flowers.
Is there a _________ outside your bedroom window?

vocabularies.definition: A room in a house that is under the ground.
vocabularies.example:
We keep our old toys in the basement.
It's dark down in the basement.
The washing machine is in the basement.
quizzes: It is very dark down in the __________, under the house.
We keep our washing machine and old toys in the __________.
Is your bedroom in the __________ or on the first floor?

vocabularies.definition: The door or gate where you go into a place.
vocabularies.example:
Wait for me at the front entrance.
The entrance to the cave is small.
Where is the main entrance?
quizzes: Please wait for me at the front __________ of the school.
The __________ to the big cave was very small.
Which door is the main __________ to the building?

vocabularies.definition: A room at the top of a house under the roof.
vocabularies.example:
He stores his old books in the attic.
It's very dusty in the attic.
The stairs to the attic are narrow.
quizzes: The __________ is a dusty room at the very top of the house.
My grandfather keeps all his old books in the __________.
It is very quiet up in the __________, under the roof.

vocabularies.definition: The top part of a room that you look up at.
vocabularies.example:
The ceiling is painted white.
A large spider is on the ceiling.
There is a lamp hanging from the ceiling.
quizzes: Look up! There is a big spider on the __________.
There is a beautiful lamp hanging from the middle of the __________.
I can't touch the __________ because it is too high.

vocabularies.definition: A pipe on a roof that lets smoke from a fire go out.
vocabularies.example:
Smoke is coming out of the chimney.
Birds often build nests on the chimney.
Santa Claus comes down the chimney.
quizzes: Santa Claus comes down the __________ to bring presents.
Look at the white smoke coming out of the __________.
Birds sometimes build their nests on top of the __________.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of cloth you can pull across a window.
vocabularies.example:
Please close the curtains; it's getting dark.
The curtains match the carpet.
She bought new blue curtains for the bedroom.
quizzes: Please close the __________ because it is getting dark outside.
I have beautiful blue __________ on my bedroom window.
You should pull the __________ across if the sun is too bright.

vocabularies.definition: A soft bag used for sitting or leaning on for comfort.
vocabularies.example:
There are some soft cushions on the sofa.
I like to lean on a cushion when I read.
She bought a colorful cushion for her chair.
quizzes: There are three soft __________ on our living room sofa.
I like to put a __________ behind my back when I read.
Which color do you like for this new __________, red or blue?

vocabularies.definition: A box that slides into a desk or cabinet for keeping things in.
vocabularies.example:
The spoons are in the top drawer.
Keep your socks in the bottom drawer.
I can't open this drawer; it's stuck.
quizzes: Please put your socks away in the bottom __________.
I can't find my pens; are they in the desk __________?
The silver spoons are kept in the top __________ in the kitchen.

vocabularies.definition: A cold machine used to keep food and drinks fresh.
vocabularies.example:
Put the milk back in the fridge.
The fridge is full of vegetables.
Is there any juice in the fridge?
quizzes: Keep the milk and butter cold in the __________.
I am hungry; is there any pizza left in the __________?
Mom put all the fresh vegetables inside the __________.

vocabularies.definition: A large, soft cover used on a bed to keep you warm.
vocabularies.example:
It's cold tonight, so I need an extra blanket.
She wrapped herself in a warm blanket.
Can I have another blanket, please?
quizzes: I need a warm __________ on my bed because it's cold tonight.
The baby is sleeping under a soft blue __________.
Can I have another __________, please? I am shivering.

vocabularies.definition: A thick material that covers the floor in a room.
vocabularies.example:
We have a soft red carpet in the living room.
Please take off your shoes; the carpet is clean.
He spilled some juice on the carpet.
quizzes: Please take off your muddy shoes; the __________ is clean.
We have a very soft red __________ on our living room floor.
Don't spill your orange juice on the new white __________!

vocabularies.definition: A thing that gives light to a room, usually put on a table or desk.
vocabularies.example:
Turn on the lamp so you can see better.
There is a lamp on my bedside table.
She bought a beautiful new lamp.
quizzes: Turn on the __________ on your desk so you can see your book.
There is a small __________ next to my bed for reading at night.
Is the light from that __________ bright enough for you?

vocabularies.definition: A piece of glass where you can see yourself.
vocabularies.example:
She looked at herself in the mirror.
There is a large mirror in the bathroom.
The mirror is broken.
quizzes: I look at my face in the __________ every morning.
There is a large __________ on the bathroom wall.
Don't break the __________; it's made of thin glass.

vocabularies.definition: The top cover of a building or house.
vocabularies.example:
The cat is sitting on the roof.
Heavy rain is hitting the roof.
They are repairing the roof of the house.
quizzes: Look! Our neighbor's cat is sitting on the house __________.
The rain makes a loud noise when it hits the __________.
Workers are fixing the __________ of our school this week.

vocabularies.definition: A flat board on a wall or in a cupboard where you put books or objects.
vocabularies.example:
Put the books back on the shelf.
The shelf is full of old toys.
Can you reach the top shelf?
quizzes: Please put your storybooks back on the wooden __________.
I can't reach that toy because it is on the top __________.
Dad put a new __________ on the wall for my trophies.

vocabularies.definition: Something you use with water to wash your hands and body.
vocabularies.example:
Wash your hands with soap and water.
This soap smells like lemons.
I need to buy some more soap.
quizzes: Always wash your hands with __________ before you eat dinner.
This pink __________ smells like beautiful flowers.
Be careful! The __________ is very slippery in the shower.

vocabularies.definition: A bowl with a seat in the bathroom used for going to the bathroom.
vocabularies.example:
Where is the toilet, please?
The toilet is at the end of the hall.
Don't forget to flush the toilet.
quizzes: Go down the hall and the __________ is the last door on the left.
Don't forget to flush the __________ after you use it.
May I go to the __________, please?

vocabularies.definition: A piece of thick cloth used to dry your body after a shower.
vocabularies.example:
I need a dry towel after my shower.
The towel is hanging behind the door.
Please put the wet towel in the wash.
quizzes: Use this large __________ to dry your hair after swimming.
There is a clean blue __________ hanging behind the bathroom door.
Don't leave your wet __________ on the floor!

grammars.description: Dùng để chỉ vị trí vật dụng trong nhà.
grammars.example:
The keys are in the drawer.
The cat is under the balcony.

vocabularies.definition: A very large animal that lived millions of years ago, before people.
vocabularies.example:
Dinosaurs lived millions of years ago.
I saw a dinosaur skeleton at the museum.
Which is your favorite dinosaur?
quizzes: A __________ is a huge animal that lived a long, long time ago.
I saw a big skeleton of a __________ at the city museum.
Some __________ were as tall as trees!

vocabularies.definition: An insect with large, beautiful, colorful wings.
vocabularies.example:
A colorful butterfly sat on the flower.
Butterflies have beautiful wings.
I saw a butterfly in the garden.
quizzes: Look at that beautiful __________ flying over the flowers!
A __________ has very colorful and soft wings.
Did you see the blue __________ in the garden today?

vocabularies.definition: A sea animal with a soft body and eight long arms.
vocabularies.example:
An octopus has eight arms.
Octopuses live deep in the ocean.
The octopus hid under a rock.
quizzes: An __________ is a sea animal that has eight long arms.
The __________ used its arms to hide under a rock.
Can you draw an __________ with its eight wavy legs?

vocabularies.definition: A large animal with a long neck and one or two humps on its back.
vocabularies.example:
Camels can live in the desert for a long time.
Have you ever ridden a camel?
Camels carry heavy things across the sand.
quizzes: A __________ is a large animal that lives in the hot desert.
Look at the big hump on the back of that __________!
Have you ever tried to ride a __________ in the sand?

vocabularies.definition: An animal that looks like a small horse with long ears.
vocabularies.example:
The donkey carried the heavy baskets.
Donkeys are very strong animals.
I saw a donkey at the farm.
quizzes: A __________ looks a bit like a small horse with long ears.
The gray __________ carried heavy baskets for the farmer.
I heard a __________ making a loud noise at the farm.

vocabularies.definition: A large, strong bird that hunts and eats smaller animals.
vocabularies.example:
The eagle flew high above the mountains.
Eagles have very sharp eyes.
An eagle can catch small animals.
quizzes: The __________ is a large bird that flies high in the sky.
An __________ has very sharp eyes to find its food.
I saw a strong __________ sitting at the top of the mountain.

vocabularies.definition: A small animal with six legs like an ant or a fly.
vocabularies.example:
Bees and ants are types of insects.
There are many insects in the rainforest.
Study the insects with a magnifying glass.
quizzes: An __________ is a tiny animal that always has six legs.
Ants, bees, and flies are all types of __________.
There are many small __________ living in the forest.

vocabularies.definition: A large Australian animal that hops and carries its baby in a pouch.
vocabularies.example:
Kangaroos live in Australia.
A mother kangaroo carries its baby in a pouch.
Kangaroos can jump very high.
quizzes: A __________ is an animal from Australia that can hop very high.
The mother __________ carries its baby in a warm pouch.
I saw a __________ jumping across the grass at the zoo.

vocabularies.definition: A small reptile with short legs, a long tail, and rough skin.
vocabularies.example:
A small lizard is sunbathing on the wall.
Lizards have long tails.
Some lizards can change their color.
quizzes: A tiny green __________ is sitting on the hot stone.
Some types of __________ can change their color to hide.
Look! That __________ just caught a fly with its tongue!

vocabularies.definition: A large, beautiful white bird with a long neck that lives on lakes.
vocabularies.example:
Look at the beautiful white swan on the lake.
Swans are very graceful birds.
The swan protect its nest.
quizzes: A __________ is a beautiful white bird with a very long neck.
Look at the graceful __________ swimming on the lake.
The white __________ is sitting quietly on its nest.

vocabularies.definition: A small animal that looks like a mouse with wings and flies at night.
vocabularies.example:
Bats fly at night to find food.
I saw a bat in the cave.
Bats sleep hanging upside down.
quizzes: A __________ is an animal that can fly but is not a bird.
We saw a small __________ flying in the dark night sky.
Do __________ really sleep while hanging upside down?

vocabularies.definition: A large, strong wild animal with thick fur.
vocabularies.example:
Polar bears live in the cold Arctic.
The bear is looking for honey.
Don't go near the bear.
quizzes: A big brown __________ is looking for honey in the forest.
The white polar __________ loves living in the cold snow.
Don't go too close to the wild __________, it's very strong.

vocabularies.definition: A flying insect that makes honey and can sting you.
vocabularies.example:
Bees make honey from flowers.
A bee sting can be painful.
Bees are very busy insects.
quizzes: A small yellow and black __________ is visiting the flower.
We get sweet honey from a type of insect called a __________.
Be careful! A __________ might sting if you touch it.

vocabularies.definition: A large, dangerous reptile with sharp teeth that lives in rivers.
vocabularies.example:
Crocodiles have very sharp teeth.
Be careful near the river; there are crocodiles.
A crocodile can swim very fast.
quizzes: A __________ is a dangerous animal with very sharp teeth.
Be careful! A __________ is swimming in the muddy river.
That __________ is sunbathing on the river bank.

vocabularies.definition: A very clever sea animal that is friendly and looks like a large fish.
vocabularies.example:
Dolphins are very intelligent animals.
We saw dolphins jumping in the ocean.
Dolphins like to play with people.
quizzes: A __________ is a very clever and friendly sea animal.
We saw a group of __________ jumping out of the blue sea.
A __________ is not a fish, even though it lives in water.

vocabularies.definition: A huge grey animal with large ears and a long trunk.
vocabularies.example:
An elephant has a long trunk.
Elephants are the largest land animals.
We saw an elephant at the zoo.
quizzes: An __________ is the largest land animal and has big ears.
The grey __________ used its long trunk to pick up some grass.
Can we see a real __________ at the city zoo today?

vocabularies.definition: A tall African animal with a very long neck and long legs.
vocabularies.example:
The giraffe has a very long neck.
Giraffes eat leaves from the tops of trees.
I saw a baby giraffe today.
quizzes: A __________ is a very tall animal with a very long neck.
The __________ can reach the highest leaves on the trees.
Look at the orange and brown spots on the _________!

vocabularies.definition: A brightly colored bird that can sometimes copy what people say.
vocabularies.example:
The parrot can repeat the words I say.
Parrots have very colorful feathers.
My grandmother has a pet parrot.
quizzes: A __________ is a bird with many bright and colorful feathers.
My pet __________ can repeat some of the words I say.
What is that green __________ eating in the tree?

vocabularies.definition: A large, dangerous fish with very sharp teeth that lives in the ocean.
vocabularies.example:
Sharks have many rows of teeth.
We went diving and saw a shark.
Some sharks are very dangerous.
quizzes: A __________ is a large fish that has many sharp teeth.
We saw a grey __________ swimming in the deep ocean.
Some types of __________ can be very dangerous to people.

vocabularies.definition: A huge sea animal that looks like a giant fish but breathes air.
vocabularies.example:
The blue whale is the biggest animal in the world.
Whales breathe air through a blowhole.
We saw a whale from the boat.
quizzes: A __________ is a giant animal that lives in the ocean.
The blue __________ is the largest animal in the whole world.
Look! A __________ just blew some water into the air!

grammars.description: Dùng để nói về khả năng của động vật.
grammars.example:
Kangaroos can jump high.
Penguins can't fly.

vocabularies.definition: The natural world of land, air, and water where people and animals live.
vocabularies.example:
We must protect the environment.
Plastic is bad for the environment.
We learn about the environment at school.
quizzes: We must work together to protect our beautiful __________.
We learn how to keep the __________ clean at school.
Throwing trash in the river is bad for the __________.

vocabularies.definition: Dirty or bad things in the air, water, or earth that make it not clean.
vocabularies.example:
Air pollution is a big problem in cities.
There is a lot of water pollution here.
Cars cause a lot of pollution.
quizzes: Air __________ from cars can make the sky look grey.
There is too much water __________ in this dirty river.
We should try to stop __________ to keep our planet clean.

vocabularies.definition: To put used paper, glass, or plastic into special bins so it can be made into new things.
vocabularies.example:
We should recycle glass and paper.
How do you recycle plastic bottles?
Recycling helps the planet.
quizzes: We should __________ our old glass bottles and newspapers.
Do you know how to __________ plastic at your home?
It is easy to __________ your old paper into new notebooks.

vocabularies.definition: A very dry place with lots of sand and not much water or plants.
vocabularies.example:
It is very hot and dry in the desert.
Not many plants grow in the desert.
The Sahara is the largest desert in Africa.
quizzes: A __________ is a very hot place where there is lots of sand.
Not many green plants can grow in the dry __________.
The Sahara is the biggest __________ in the world.

vocabularies.definition: A huge area of salty water that covers a lot of the Earth.
vocabularies.example:
Whales live in the deep ocean.
The ocean is much bigger than a sea.
We shouldn't throw trash into the ocean.
quizzes: A huge blue __________ covers most of our planet Earth.
The __________ is much bigger and deeper than a small sea.
Sharks and whales live deep in the salty __________.

vocabularies.definition: A very large, round object in space that moves around a bright star, like Earth.
vocabularies.example:
Earth is the planet we live on.
There are eight planets in our solar system.
We must keep our planet clean.
quizzes: Earth is the only __________ where we know people live.
There are eight different __________ in our solar system.
We must all work together to keep our __________ clean.

vocabularies.definition: A thick forest in a hot part of the world where it rains very often.
vocabularies.example:
Many animals live in the Amazon rainforest.
Rainforests are important for the Earth's air.
It rains a lot in the rainforest.
quizzes: A __________ is a hot place where it rains almost every day.
Many colorful birds and monkeys live in the __________.
The Amazon is a very famous and large __________.

vocabularies.definition: The huge, dark area outside Earth where the stars and planets are.
vocabularies.example:
Astronauts travel to space.
Stars and planets are in space.
The moon is beautiful in space.
quizzes: Astronauts use big rockets to travel into deep __________.
The stars look very beautiful in the dark __________.
There is no air to breathe in __________, outside of Earth.

vocabularies.definition: Very bad weather with a lot of rain, strong winds, and sometimes thunder.
vocabularies.example:
There was a loud thunder during the storm.
The storm broke some trees in the garden.
Stay inside when there is a storm.
quizzes: We should stay inside the house when there is a big __________.
The loud thunder during the __________ was very scary.
The strong wind from the __________ broke many trees.

vocabularies.definition: A mountain with a hole in the top where hot liquid rock sometimes comes out.
vocabularies.example:
The volcano erupted last night.
Hot lava flows down from the volcano.
Be careful near the volcano.
quizzes: A __________ is a special mountain that can blow out hot fire.
Be very careful if you go near a smoking __________.
Hot red lava flows down from the top of the __________.

vocabularies.definition: Something built over a river or a road so you can cross it.
vocabularies.example:
We drove across the bridge.
This bridge is very old.
The bridge connects the two sides of the river.
quizzes: We walked across the long __________ to cross the river.
This wooden __________ is very old and a bit shaky.
A __________ allows cars to drive over the busy road.

vocabularies.definition: A large hole in the side of a hill, mountain, or under the ground.
vocabularies.example:
The cave was dark and cold.
We explored the deep cave with flashlights.
Bats live inside the cave.
quizzes: The __________ was very dark, so we used our flashlights.
A family of brown bats lives inside the deep __________.
Is there a secret treasure hidden inside this _________?

vocabularies.definition: A large area of land covered with many trees and plants.
vocabularies.example:
There are many trees in the forest.
We went for a walk in the forest.
Many wild animals live in the forest.
quizzes: There are hundreds of tall green trees in the __________.
Many wild animals and birds live in the deep __________.
We went for a long walk in the __________ last Sunday.

vocabularies.definition: An area of high land that is smaller than a mountain.
vocabularies.example:
We climbed to the top of the hill.
The view from the hill is beautiful.
There is a small house on the hill.
quizzes: We climbed up the __________ to see the beautiful sun.
A __________ is like a mountain but it is much smaller.
Look! There is a little white house on top of the __________.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of land that has water all around it.
vocabularies.example:
We stayed on a beautiful tropical island.
You need a boat to get to the island.
The island is surrounded by blue water.
quizzes: An __________ is a piece of land with water all around it.
We need to go by boat to visit that small __________.
The __________ has many beautiful sandy beaches.

vocabularies.definition: A very high and steep piece of land, much taller than a hill.
vocabularies.example:
Mount Everest is the highest mountain.
There is snow on top of the mountain.
We went skiing on the mountain.
quizzes: A __________ is very, very high and often has snow on top.
Mount Everest is the highest __________ in the world.
We went for a walk at the bottom of the tall __________.

vocabularies.definition: A narrow track on the ground for people to walk on in a garden or forest.
vocabularies.example:
Follow the path through the woods.
The path leads to the hidden garden.
Don't run on the muddy path.
quizzes: Follow the narrow __________ to find the secret garden.
There is a small __________ leading through the green forest.
Please stay on the __________, the grass is very wet.

vocabularies.definition: A long, moving stream of fresh water that goes into the sea.
vocabularies.example:
The river flows into the sea.
We went fishing in the river.
The river is very wide here.
quizzes: A __________ is long and has moving water that goes to the sea.
Let's go fishing in the __________ by the old bridge.
The __________ water is cold and very clear today.

vocabularies.definition: A large or small piece of hard stone.
vocabularies.example:
The climbers climbed up the steep rock.
He sat on a large rock by the lake.
There are many rocks on the beach.
quizzes: The climber climbed up the tall and steep __________.
He sat on a big, flat __________ next to the lake.
Be careful not to trip over the __________ on the beach.

vocabularies.definition: A place where a stream or river falls down from a high cliff or rock.
vocabularies.example:
The waterfall is very tall and beautiful.
We took a photo of the waterfall.
You can hear the sound of the waterfall from far away.
quizzes: A __________ is where water falls down from a high rock.
We stood under the small __________ to get cool and wet.
Can you hear the loud noise of the tall __________?

grammars.description: Dùng để giới thiệu các thành phần trong môi trường.
grammars.example:
There is a volcano on the island.
There are many stars in space.

vocabularies.definition: An event where people or teams try to win to be the best.
vocabularies.example:
He won first prize in the art competition.
Are you entering the swimming competition?
The competition was very difficult.
quizzes: Who won the first prize in the school swimming __________?
There is a big art __________ at the city library next week.
The tennis __________ was very difficult, but I had fun.

vocabularies.definition: A game where two teams hit a ball over a high net using their hands.
vocabularies.example:
We play volleyball on the beach.
I am in the school volleyball team.
She hit the volleyball over the net.
quizzes: We like to play __________ on the beach when it's sunny.
I am in the school __________ team this year.
You use your hands to hit the ball over the net in __________.

vocabularies.definition: A person or a team that wins a very important competition or game.
vocabularies.example:
She is the world chess champion.
Our team became the champions.
He wants to be a tennis champion.
quizzes: She is the world chess __________ because she plays so well.
Our team worked very hard to become the school __________.
Do you want to be a tennis __________ when you grow up?

vocabularies.definition: An exciting, unusual, and sometimes dangerous thing to do.
vocabularies.example:
Reading a book is like going on an adventure.
We had a big adventure in the mountains.
Are you ready for an adventure?
quizzes: Reading an exciting book is like going on a big __________.
We had a great __________ while camping in the mountains.
Are you ready for a new __________ in the forest?

vocabularies.definition: To find and keep many things together as a hobby, like stamps or coins.
vocabularies.example:
My brother collects comic books.
I like to collect stickers.
We need to collect wood for the fire.
quizzes: My brother likes to __________ colorful stickers in his album.
I want to __________ all the cards of my favorite players.
Do you __________ old stamps as a hobby?

vocabularies.definition: The place where you kick or hit the ball to score points in games like football.
vocabularies.example:
He scored a fantastic goal in the final minute.
The crowd cheered when we scored a goal.
How many goals did he score?
quizzes: He scored a fantastic __________ in the last minute of the match.
The crowd cheered when the player kicked the ball into the __________.
How many __________ did your team score in the game today?

vocabularies.definition: An activity that you enjoy doing in your free time.
vocabularies.example:
Painting is my favorite hobby.
Do you have any interesting hobbies?
My hobby is taking photos.
quizzes: Painting and drawing are my favorite __________.
Do you have any interesting __________ like collecting rocks?
My brother's __________ is taking photos of wild birds.

vocabularies.definition: A person who joins in playing a game or a sport.
vocabularies.example:
There are eleven players in a football team.
She is the best tennis player in the school.
The players were tired after the match.
quizzes: There are eleven __________ in each football team.
She is the best tennis __________ in our whole school.
The __________ were very tired after the long match.

vocabularies.definition: A large, open building with seats around a playing field for sports.
vocabularies.example:
The stadium was full of excited fans.
We went to the stadium to watch the match.
They build a huge stadium for the Olympics.
quizzes: The __________ was full of excited fans watching the game.
We went to the big __________ to see the football match.
They are building a new __________ for the Olympic Games.

vocabularies.definition: A group of people who play a sport or game together against others.
vocabularies.example:
Our school team won the trophy.
I like working in a team.
Who is the captain of your team?
quizzes: Our school __________ won the trophy for the first time!
I really enjoy working in a __________ with my friends.
Who is the captain of your school basketball __________?

vocabularies.definition: An indoor game played by moving pieces on a special board, like chess.
vocabularies.example:
We like playing board games on rainy days.
My favorite board game is Monopoly.
Let's play a board game tonight.
quizzes: We love playing __________ together on cold, rainy days.
My favorite __________ is chess, but I also like Monopoly.
Let's play a __________ tonight before we go to bed.

vocabularies.definition: A board game for two players where you move pieces to trap the other's king.
vocabularies.example:
He is very good at playing chess.
I want to learn how to play chess.
My father taught me chess when I was six.
quizzes: He is very good at __________ and can win most games.
I want to learn how to play __________ with my grandfather.
In __________, you move your pieces to trap the other's king.

vocabularies.definition: A word game where you guess words from clues and write them into empty squares.
vocabularies.example:
He enjoys doing the crossword in the newspaper.
This crossword is very difficult.
Can you help me with this crossword clue?
quizzes: My dad enjoys doing the __________ in the newspaper every morning.
This __________ is very difficult; I can't find the answers.
Can you help me with this funny __________ clue about animals?

vocabularies.definition: A person who really likes or supports a sports team or a famous person.
vocabularies.example:
He is a big fan of football.
The fans cheered loudly for their team.
I am a fan of that singer.
quizzes: He is a very big __________ of football and knows all the players.
Thousands of __________ cheered loudly for their favorite team.
I am a huge __________ of that famous pop singer.

vocabularies.definition: A game or sports competition between two people or two teams.
vocabularies.example:
Did you watch the football match last night?
The match was very exciting.
Our team won the final match.
quizzes: Did you watch the exciting football __________ on TV last night?
The tennis __________ lasted for three hours!
Our team won the final __________ of the competition.

vocabularies.definition: Something you are given for winning a game or doing something very well.
vocabularies.example:
She won a prize for her beautiful painting.
The first prize is a new bike.
He was very happy with his prize.
quizzes: She won a beautiful __________ for her amazing painting.
The first __________ for the race is a new blue bicycle.
Was he happy with the __________ he got for the competition?

vocabularies.definition: A fast competition to see who can run, drive, or swim the quickest.
vocabularies.example:
I'm going to run in the school race.
Who won the car race?
The race was very fast and exciting.
quizzes: I'm going to run in the school __________ next Friday.
Who was the winner of the big car __________?
The swimming __________ was very fast and exciting to watch.

vocabularies.definition: To move on ice wearing special shoes with metal blades, or shoes with wheels.
vocabularies.example:
We like to go to the ice rink to skate.
Can you skate on the lake?
He is learning how to skate.
quizzes: We like to go to the park and __________ on our rollerblades.
Can you __________ on the ice in the winter?
He is learning how to __________ and falls down a lot.

vocabularies.definition: The sport of sliding down snow-covered hills on a pair of long, flat boards.
vocabularies.example:
We go skiing in the mountains every winter.
Skiing is a very exciting sport.
Have you ever been skiing.
quizzes: We go __________ in the cold mountains every winter.
__________ is a very fast and exciting winter sport.
Have you ever tried __________ on the white snow?

vocabularies.definition: A single wide board that you stand on to slide down a snow mountain.
vocabularies.example:
He wants to learn how to snowboard.
Snowboarding is more difficult than skiing for me.
He bought a new snowboard.
quizzes: He wants to learn how to __________ on his next winter holiday.
__________ looks more difficult than skiing to me.
He bought a cool new __________ to use on the mountain.

grammars.description: Dùng để kể về các sự kiện thể thao trong quá khứ.
grammars.example:
He won the match yesterday.
We played volleyball last weekend.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling afraid, scared, or nervous because of something bad.
vocabularies.example:
She is frightened of spiders.
Don't be frightened, the dog is friendly.
I was frightened by the loud noise.
quizzes: She is extremely __________ of big, hairy spiders.
Don't be __________, the little dog is very friendly.
The loud thunder during the storm made the baby feel __________.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling sad because something was not as good as you expected.
vocabularies.example:
He was disappointed with his exam results.
We were disappointed that it rained.
She felt disappointed when she lost.
quizzes: He was very __________ with his poor exam results.
We were __________ that it rained on the day of our picnic.
She felt __________ when she lost the game after practicing so hard.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling very happy and enthusiastic because something good is happening.
vocabularies.example:
The children are excited about the trip.
I'm so excited to see you!
He was too excited to sleep.
quizzes: The children are very __________ about their school trip.
I'm so __________ to see my grandparents this weekend!
He was too __________ to sleep on the night before his birthday.

vocabularies.definition: Not afraid to do dangerous or difficult things.
vocabularies.example:
The firefighters were very brave.
Be brave; it won't hurt much.
He is a brave knight.
quizzes: The firefighters were very __________ and saved the cat.
Be __________; the doctor says the needle won't hurt much.
The story is about a __________ knight who fought a dragon.

vocabularies.definition: Smart and quick to learn or understand things.
vocabularies.example:
She is very clever at math.
That was a clever idea!
The dog is clever enough to open the door.
quizzes: She is very __________ at math and can solve any problem.
That was a very __________ idea to fix the broken toy!
The dog is __________ enough to open the gate with its nose.

vocabularies.definition: Known or recognized by many people around the world.
vocabularies.example:
She is a famous singer.
This castle is famous all over the world.
I want to be famous one day.
quizzes: She is a very __________ singer who travels all over the world.
This old castle is __________ for its beautiful gardens.
I want to be a __________ scientist when I grow up.

vocabularies.definition: Kind, pleasant, and acting like a good friend to others.
vocabularies.example:
The local people are very friendly.
He has a friendly face.
Our neighbor is very friendly to us.
quizzes: The people in this small town are very __________ and kind.
He has a very __________ face and a big smile.
Our new neighbor is very __________ to us and always says hello.

vocabularies.definition: Making you smile or laugh.
vocabularies.example:
That was a funny story!
He is the funniest person in our class.
The movie was very funny.
quizzes: That was a really __________ story that made everyone laugh!
He is the __________ person in our class and tells great jokes.
The movie we watched last night was very __________.

vocabularies.definition: Caring about other people and wanting to help them.
vocabularies.example:
Thank you for your kind words.
She is a kind and helpful teacher.
Be kind to animals.
quizzes: Thank you for your __________ words and for helping me.
She is a very __________ and helpful teacher who cares for us.
We should always be __________ to animals and nature.

vocabularies.definition: Unusual or surprising in a way that is hard to understand.
vocabularies.example:
I heard a strange noise in the garden.
That's a strange thing to say.
He has a strange hobby.
quizzes: I heard a very __________ noise coming from the garden.
That's a __________ thing to say; I don't understand it.
He has a __________ hobby of collecting old bottle caps.

vocabularies.definition: To think that something is true or that someone is telling the truth.
vocabularies.example:
I believe that everything will be okay.
Do you believe in ghosts?
I can't believe it's already February.
quizzes: I __________ that everything will be okay in the end.
Do you __________ in ghosts or aliens from space?
I can't __________ that it's already time for summer holiday.

vocabularies.definition: Not interesting or exciting, making you feel tired of it.
vocabularies.example:
The movie was very boring; I nearly fell asleep.
I find history a bit boring.
Doing the same thing every day is boring.
quizzes: The long movie was very __________; I nearly fell asleep.
I find practicing the piano a bit __________ sometimes.
Doing the same thing every single day is very __________.

vocabularies.definition: Giving a lot of attention to what you are doing so you do not make a mistake.
vocabularies.example:
Be careful when you cross the road.
You should be more careful with your toys.
He is a very careful driver.
quizzes: Be very __________ when you cross the busy road.
You should be more __________ with your toys so they don't break.
He is a very __________ driver and never goes too fast.

vocabularies.definition: All through a period of time, or at some point in a period of time.
vocabularies.example:
It rained during the night.
Please be quiet during the exam.
We stayed at home during the storm.
quizzes: It rained heavily __________ the night while we were sleeping.
Please try to stay quiet __________ the important exam.
We stayed at home and played games __________ the storm.

vocabularies.definition: To make something easy to understand by talking clearly about it.
vocabularies.example:
Can you explain this math problem to me?
The teacher explained the rules of the game.
It's difficult to explain how I feel.
quizzes: Can you __________ this difficult math problem to me, please?
The teacher __________ the rules of the new game clearly.
It's difficult for me to __________ exactly how I feel.

vocabularies.definition: To try to give an answer when you are not sure if it is right.
vocabularies.example:
Can you guess what I have in my bag?
I guess he is about ten years old.
Guess who I saw today!
quizzes: Can you __________ what I have inside my surprise bag?
I __________ he is about ten years old, but I'm not sure.
__________ who I saw at the supermarket this afternoon!

vocabularies.definition: To take place or to occur, usually without being planned.
vocabularies.example:
What happened to your leg?
A funny thing happened at school today.
When did the accident happen?
quizzes: Oh no! What __________ to your leg? It looks hurt.
A very funny thing __________ at school during lunch today.
When did the small accident __________ in the playground?

vocabularies.definition: To want something good to happen and think that it might happen.
vocabularies.example:
I hope you have a great birthday.
I hope the weather will be sunny tomorrow.
We hope to see you again soon.
quizzes: I __________ you have a wonderful birthday party today!
I __________ the weather will be sunny for our trip tomorrow.
We __________ to see you again very soon at the museum.

vocabularies.definition: To have a certain meaning. Also, not kind or nice to other people.
vocabularies.example:
What does this word mean?
Don't be mean to your little sister.
I didn't mean to hurt your feelings.
quizzes: What does this new English word __________ in Vietnamese?
Don't be __________ to your little sister; you should be kind.
I'm sorry; I didn't __________ to hurt your feelings.

vocabularies.definition: To like one thing or person better than another.
vocabularies.example:
I prefer tea to coffee.
Which do you prefer, apples or oranges?
I prefer staying at home on rainy days.
quizzes: I __________ drinking cold milk to drinking hot tea.
Which do you __________, red apples or green apples?
I __________ staying at home to going out when it's raining.

grammars.description: Một số tính từ đi kèm giới từ cố định.
grammars.example:
I am frightened of snakes.
She is excited about the party.

vocabularies.definition: The sister of your mother or father, or the wife of your uncle.
vocabularies.example:
My aunt lives in London.
My aunt bought me a new dress.
Is your aunt coming to the party?
quizzes: My mother's sister is my __________, and she lives in London.
My __________ bought me a beautiful new dress for my birthday.
Is your __________ coming to the family dinner party tonight?

vocabularies.definition: The brother of your mother or father, or the husband of your aunt.
vocabularies.example:
My uncle is a pilot.
My uncle takes us fishing.
I visited my uncle last summer.
quizzes: My __________ is a very talented pilot who flies big planes.
My __________ often takes us fishing at the lake on weekends.
I visited my __________ and his family last summer in Spain.

vocabularies.definition: The child of your aunt and uncle.
vocabularies.example:
I have many cousins.
My cousin is the same age as me.
We visited our cousins in the city.
quizzes: My __________ is the same age as me, and we play together often.
We visited our __________ in the city during the holidays.
I have many __________, but Alex is my favorite one.

vocabularies.definition: The man that a woman is married to.
vocabularies.example:
Her husband is a doctor.
My aunt's husband is my uncle.
Is he your husband?
quizzes: Her __________ is a friendly doctor who works at the local hospital.
My aunt's __________ is my uncle, and he tells great jokes.
Is that tall man over there your __________?

vocabularies.definition: The woman that a man is married to.
vocabularies.example:
His wife is a teacher.
My uncle's wife is my aunt.
They have been husband and wife for ten years.
quizzes: His __________ is a kind teacher at our school.
My uncle's __________ is my aunt, and she cooks delicious food.
They have been __________ and husband for over ten years now.

vocabularies.definition: A boy or man in relation to his parents.
vocabularies.example:
They have two sons and one daughter.
Their son is very good at football.
Is he your son?
quizzes: They have two __________, but they don't have any daughters.
Their __________ is very good at football and scores many goals.
Is he your __________, or is he a family friend?

vocabularies.definition: A girl or woman in relation to her parents.
vocabularies.example:
My daughter is six years old.
She is the youngest daughter in the family.
Their daughter loves drawing.
quizzes: My __________ is six years old and loves to dance and sing.
She is the youngest __________ in her big family.
Their __________ loves drawing pictures of animals and flowers.

vocabularies.definition: The child of your son or daughter.
vocabularies.example:
They have one grandchild.
She loves spending time with her grandchildren.
Who is your grandchild?
quizzes: My grandparents have one __________ , and that person is me!
She loves spending time with her __________ in the garden.
Who is your youngest __________ , Mr. Green?

vocabularies.definition: The parents of your mother or father.
vocabularies.example:
I visit my grandparents every weekend.
My grandparents live in a small house.
They are very kind grandparents.
quizzes: I visit my __________ every weekend and stay at their house.
My __________ live in a small, cozy house near the beach.
They are very kind and loving __________ to all of us.

vocabularies.definition: A person who lives next to you or near you in your street.
vocabularies.example:
Our neighbors are very noisy.
She is a very friendly neighbor.
I borrowed some sugar from my neighbor.
quizzes: Our __________ are very noisy when they listen to music.
She is a very friendly __________ who always smiles at us.
I borrowed some sugar from my __________ this morning.

vocabularies.definition: The one friend that you like the most out of all your friends.
vocabularies.example:
Sarah has been my best friend since kindergarten.
We tell each other everything.
My best friend lives just across the street.
quizzes: Sarah has been my __________ since we were in kindergarten.
My __________ and I tell each other everything.
My __________ lives just across the street from my house.

vocabularies.definition: The daughter of your son or daughter.
vocabularies.example:
They have a beautiful three-year-old granddaughter.
Her granddaughter visits her every Sunday.
She is very proud of her granddaughter.
quizzes: They have a beautiful three-year-old __________ called Lily.
Her __________ visits her every Sunday to have tea.
She is very proud of her __________, who is a great student.

vocabularies.definition: The son of your son or daughter.
vocabularies.example:
My grandson is starting school next week.
He loves playing football with his grandson.
Their grandson is very tall.
quizzes: My __________ is starting school for the first time next week.
He loves playing football in the park with his young __________.
Their __________ is very tall for his age.

vocabularies.definition: Having a wife or husband.
vocabularies.example:
My aunt and uncle have been married for twenty years.
When did they get married?
They are a very happy married couple.
quizzes: My aunt and uncle have been __________ for twenty years.
When did your parents get __________, do you know?
They are a very happy __________ couple who love traveling.

vocabularies.definition: A person you play a game with, work with, or live with.
vocabularies.example:
Who is your dancing partner for the competition?
He is my business partner.
Find a partner for the next activity.
quizzes: Who is your dancing __________ for the competition?
He is my business __________, and we own a small shop.
Find a __________ for the next classroom activity, please.

vocabularies.definition: A man, woman, or child (plural is people).
vocabularies.example:
Who is that person over there?
She is a very kind and helpful person.
Only one person is allowed in the room at a time.
quizzes: Who is that __________ over there with the red hat?
She is a very kind and helpful __________ to everyone she meets.
Only one __________ is allowed to enter the room at a time.

vocabularies.definition: Not married or not having a romantic partner.
vocabularies.example:
Is your brother married or single?
He is single and lives in a small apartment.
She enjoys being single.
quizzes: Is your older brother __________ or is he married?
He is __________ and lives in a small apartment by himself.
She enjoys being __________ and traveling the world.

vocabularies.definition: Your family name that comes after your first name.
vocabularies.example:
What is your surname, please?
My surname is Smith.
Write your first name and your surname here.
quizzes: What is your __________, please? My first name is John.
My __________ is Smith, which is very common.
Write your first name and your __________ on this line.

vocabularies.definition: A young person between 13 and 19 years old.
vocabularies.example:
The club is for teenagers aged 13 to 18.
He is a typical teenager who loves music.
There were many teenagers at the concert.
quizzes: The sports club is for __________ aged 13 to 18 years old.
He is a typical __________ who loves listening to loud music.
There were many __________ dancing at the school concert.

vocabularies.definition: One of two children born to the same mother at the same time.
vocabularies.example:
My sister has twin boys.
They are identical twins.
He has a twin brother.
quizzes: My sister has __________ boys, and they look exactly alike!
They are identical __________, so it's hard to tell who is who.
He has a __________ brother who is just one minute older.

grammars.description: Dùng để chỉ sự sở hữu giữa các thành viên gia đình.
grammars.example:
This is my aunt's house.
Those are my cousin's toys.

vocabularies.definition: A pain in your back.
vocabularies.example:
He has a terrible backache from lifting heavy boxes.
Sit straight or you will get a backache.
My father has a backache today.
quizzes: He has a terrible __________ from lifting those heavy boxes all day.
Remember to sit straight or you will get a __________.
My father has a bad __________ today and needs to rest on the sofa.

vocabularies.definition: An illness where you make loud, harsh noises from your throat.
vocabularies.example:
She has a bad cough.
Cover your mouth when you cough.
He drank some tea for his cough.
quizzes: She has a very bad __________ and a sore throat.
Please cover your mouth with your hand when you __________.
He drank some warm honey and lemon tea for his __________.

vocabularies.definition: A pain inside your ear.
vocabularies.example:
I can't go swimming because I have an earache.
The baby has an earache and is crying.
Do you have an earache?
quizzes: I can't go swimming today because I have a painful __________.
The baby has a bad __________ and has been crying all morning.
Do you have an __________? You should tell your mother.

vocabularies.definition: A continuous pain in your head.
vocabularies.example:
Be quiet! I have a bad headache.
She needs to rest because she has a headache.
Did you take medicine for your headache?
quizzes: Please be quiet! I have a very bad __________ and need rest.
She needs to lie down because she has a painful __________.
Did you take any medicine for your __________ yet?

vocabularies.definition: A pain in your tummy or belly.
vocabularies.example:
He ate too much candy and got a stomach-ache.
I have a stomach-ache since morning.
Drink some warm water for your stomach-ache.
quizzes: He ate too much chocolate candy and got a __________.
I have a painful __________ and haven't felt well since morning.
Drink some warm water and rest for your __________.

vocabularies.definition: A pain in one of your teeth.
vocabularies.example:
Go to the dentist if you have a toothache.
I have a toothache; I can't eat anything.
Eating too much sugar causes toothache.
quizzes: You should go to the dentist if you have a painful __________.
I have a bad __________ , so I can't eat anything crunchy today.
Eating too much sugar and not brushing often causes __________.

vocabularies.definition: When you are sick and your body is hotter than normal; a fever.
vocabularies.example:
The nurse took my temperature.
You have a high temperature; you should stay in bed.
What is his temperature?
quizzes: The school nurse took my __________ because I felt very hot.
You have a high __________; you should stay in bed today.
What is his __________? He looks a bit sick.

vocabularies.definition: Something bad-tasting that you drink or eat to help you feel better when sick.
vocabularies.example:
Take this medicine after meals.
The doctor gave her some medicine for her cold.
Is this medicine sweet?
quizzes: You must take this __________ after you finish your meals.
The doctor gave her some __________ for her cold and cough.
Is this pink __________ sweet or does it taste bad?

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is caring for people who are ill or hurt in a hospital.
vocabularies.example:
The nurse is very kind to the patients.
A nurse works in a hospital.
She wants to be a nurse when she grows up.
quizzes: The kind __________ is checking the little boy's height and weight.
A __________ usually wears a clean white or blue uniform.
She wants to be a __________ so she can help sick children.

vocabularies.definition: A large place where doctors and nurses take care of sick or injured people.
vocabularies.example:
My grandfather is in the hospital.
The city has a very large hospital.
They are building a new hospital here.
quizzes: My grandfather is feeling better now, but he is still in the __________.
The big city has a very modern __________ for sick people.
They are building a brand new children's __________ near our school.

vocabularies.definition: An arrangement to meet someone like a doctor at a certain time.
vocabularies.example:
I have a dentist appointment at 3 o'clock.
Don't be late for your doctor's appointment.
She made an appointment to see the nurse.
quizzes: I have a dentist __________ at 3 o'clock to check my teeth.
Don't be late for your important doctor's __________ this morning.
She made an __________ to see the school nurse about her sore leg.

vocabularies.definition: A long strip of cloth used to cover and protect a cut or hurt body part.
vocabularies.example:
The nurse put a bandage on my cut finger.
You should change the bandage every day.
Wait, I'll go and get a bandage.
quizzes: The kind nurse put a white __________ on my cut finger.
You should change the __________ on your knee every single day.
Wait here, I'll go and find a __________ for that small scratch.

vocabularies.definition: A shop where you can buy medicines, soap, and other health things.
vocabularies.example:
I need to go to the chemist to buy some aspirin.
The chemist gave me some advice about my cold.
Is there a chemist near the supermarket?
quizzes: I need to go to the __________ to buy some aspirin for my dad.
The __________ gave me some good advice about my seasonal cold.
Is there a __________ near the supermarket where I can buy soap?

vocabularies.definition: To experience a physical or emotional state, like feeling sick or happy.
vocabularies.example:
I don't feel very well today.
How do you feel after the medicine?
I feel much better now, thank you.
quizzes: I'm sorry, but I don't __________ very well today and need to rest.
How do you __________ now that you have taken the medicine?
I __________ much better now than I did this morning, thank you!

vocabularies.definition: The condition of your body, usually how well or how sick you are.
vocabularies.example:
Eating fruit is good for your health.
He is in very good health.
Health is more important than money.
quizzes: Eating fresh fruit and vegetables is very good for your __________.
My grandmother is in very good __________ because she exercises.
Taking care of your __________ is more important than anything else.

vocabularies.definition: Having a strong body that does not get sick very often.
vocabularies.example:
They have a very healthy lifestyle.
You look very healthy and happy.
It's important to eat healthy food.
quizzes: They have a very __________ lifestyle and eat lots of salad.
You look very __________ and happy after your summer holiday!
It's important to choose __________ food like nuts and fish.

vocabularies.definition: Not feeling well; sick.
vocabularies.example:
She stayed at home because she was ill.
He has been ill for a week.
I hope you don't get ill.
quizzes: She stayed at home from school today because she was __________.
He has been __________ for a whole week with a terrible flu.
I really hope you don't get __________ before the big party.

vocabularies.definition: The bad feeling you have in your body when you are damaged or ill.
vocabularies.example:
I have a sharp pain in my knee.
Does the medicine take away the pain?
He was in a lot of pain after the fall.
quizzes: I have a sharp __________ in my knee when I try to run.
Does taking that medicine help to take away the __________?
He was in a lot of __________ after falling off his bicycle.

vocabularies.definition: To relax, sleep, or stop working when you are tired.
vocabularies.example:
The doctor said I need to have plenty of rest.
Let's have a short rest before we continue.
You look tired; you should go and rest.
quizzes: The doctor said you should get plenty of __________ in bed.
I need to take a short __________ after that long walk.
You look very tired; why don't you have a __________ now?

vocabularies.definition: Not healthy, sick or a bit ill.
vocabularies.example:
If you feel unwell, please tell the teacher.
He looked a bit unwell this morning.
She stayed in bed because she felt unwell.
quizzes: If you feel __________ , please tell the teacher immediately.
He looked a bit __________ this morning and was very pale.
She stayed in bed all day because she felt quite __________.

grammars.description: Dùng để đưa ra lời khuyên về sức khỏe.
grammars.example:
You should stay in bed.
You shouldn't eat too many sweets.

vocabularies.definition: A long, thin piece of leather or cloth that you wear around your waist.
vocabularies.example:
He wore a black leather belt.
Your belt is too tight.
I need a new belt for my trousers.
quizzes: He wore a black leather __________ to keep his new trousers up.
Your __________ is too tight; you should loosen it a little bit.
I need to buy a nice new __________ for my school trousers.

vocabularies.definition: Things you wear on your hands to keep your fingers warm in winter.
vocabularies.example:
Wear your gloves; it's cold outside.
She lost one of her woolly gloves.
I have a pair of warm gloves.
quizzes: You should wear your warm __________ because it's snowy outside today.
She was sad because she lost one of her favorite woolly __________.
I have a pair of soft, warm __________ to protect my hands from the cold.

vocabularies.definition: A small bag carried by women to hold money, keys and makeup.
vocabularies.example:
She carries her keys in her handbag.
My mother bought a beautiful handbag.
Is this your handbag?
quizzes: She carries her house keys and some money in her small __________.
My mother bought a beautiful red __________ to go with her new dress.
Is this your floral __________? You left it on the chair.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of jewelry worn around the neck.
vocabularies.example:
She is wearing a gold necklace.
I bought a necklace for my sister's birthday.
The necklace is very expensive.
quizzes: She is wearing a beautiful gold __________ that her grandmother gave her.
I bought a colorful __________ for my sister's tenth birthday.
That shiny diamond __________ looks very expensive and pretty.

vocabularies.definition: Soft and loose clothes that you wear in bed while sleeping.
vocabularies.example:
Put on your pajamas and go to bed.
I have new blue pajamas.
Are these pajamas comfortable?
quizzes: Put on your warm __________ and get into bed; it's getting late.
I have brand new blue __________ with white stars on them.
Are these soft cotton __________ comfortable to sleep in?

vocabularies.definition: A small bag sewn into your trousers or jacket for carrying small things.
vocabularies.example:
I have some coins in my pocket.
Put the keys in your pocket.
My jacket has four pockets.
quizzes: I have some silver coins and a small toy in my jacket __________.
Put your house keys safely in your __________ so you don't lose them.
My new winter jacket has four big __________ for carrying things.

vocabularies.definition: A small circle of metal, sometimes with stones, worn on a finger as jewelry.
vocabularies.example:
She is wearing a diamond ring.
I lost my ring at the beach.
Is that a wedding ring?
quizzes: She is wearing a beautiful diamond __________ on her finger.
I am so sad because I lost my favorite silver __________ at the beach.
Is that a gold wedding __________ that your mother is wearing?

vocabularies.definition: A long piece of cloth that you wrap around your neck to stay warm.
vocabularies.example:
Wear a scarf to keep your neck warm.
She has a colorful silk scarf.
I bought a warm scarf for winter.
quizzes: Wear a woolly __________ to keep your neck warm in the wind.
She has a very colorful silk __________ that she wears with her coat.
I bought a long, warm __________ for the cold winter months.

vocabularies.definition: Trousers that end above the knee, worn when it is hot or for sports.
vocabularies.example:
I wear shorts in the summer.
He is wearing blue sports shorts.
It's too cold to wear shorts today.
quizzes: I love to wear denim __________ and a t-shirt in the hot summer.
He is wearing blue sports __________ for the football match today.
It's much too cold to wear __________ today; you should wear trousers.

vocabularies.definition: A special set of clothes that all members of a school or workers wear.
vocabularies.example:
We have to wear a school uniform.
The police officer is in uniform.
Do you like your school uniform?
quizzes: All the students at my school have to wear a dark blue __________.
Look! That police officer is wearing a very smart __________.
Do you like the color of your new school __________?

vocabularies.definition: The beautiful styles of clothes and hair that are popular at a certain time.
vocabularies.example:
She is interested in fashion and clothes.
This style is very much in fashion now.
He works in the fashion industry.
quizzes: She is very interested in __________ and always follows the latest styles.
This style of wide trousers is very much in __________ right now.
He works in the __________ industry, designing clothes for teenagers.

vocabularies.definition: A valuable shiny grey metal used to make jewelry and coins.
vocabularies.example:
This ring is made of real silver.
She bought a silver necklace.
Silver is a beautiful metal.
quizzes: This shiny ring and bracelet are made of real __________ metal.
She bought a beautiful __________ necklace to wear to the school party.
__________ is a very popular metal for making jewelry and coins.

vocabularies.definition: Having a pattern of small round dots on clothes.
vocabularies.example:
She is wearing a red and white spotted dress.
I have a spotted tie.
Look at that cute spotted dog.
quizzes: She is wearing a very cute red and white __________ dress today.
I have a blue __________ tie that matches my school shirt.
Look at that cute __________ dog; it has black dots on its white fur!

vocabularies.definition: Having a pattern of straight lines of different colors side by side.
vocabularies.example:
He is wearing a blue and white striped shirt.
I like striped socks.
The zebra has black and white striped fur.
quizzes: He is wearing a blue and white __________ shirt for the photo.
I like wearing long __________ socks with my sports shoes.
The zebra is a wild animal with black and white __________ fur.

vocabularies.definition: A round thing with a handle that you hold over your head to stay dry.
vocabularies.example:
Don't forget your umbrella; it might rain.
We shared an umbrella in the storm.
She has a bright yellow umbrella.
quizzes: Don't forget to take your __________ because it might rain later.
We shared a large __________ to stay dry in the heavy rain storm.
She has a bright yellow __________ so people can see her in the rain.

grammars.description: Dùng để mô tả một người đang mặc gì.
grammars.example:
She is wearing a beautiful necklace.
He is putting on his uniform.

vocabularies.definition: A person who performs in plays, movies, or on television.
vocabularies.example:
He is a famous Hollywood actor.
She wants to be an actress one day.
The actors practiced their lines.
quizzes: He is a very famous Hollywood __________ who has been in many movies.
She wants to be a talented __________ and perform on a big stage one day.
The __________ practiced their lines for several hours before the play.

vocabularies.definition: A person who creates art, especially paintings and drawings.
vocabularies.example:
The artist is painting a beautiful landscape.
Do you want to be an artist?
He is a very talented artist.
quizzes: The talented __________ is painting a beautiful landscape of the mountains.
Do you want to be a professional __________ when you grow up?
He is a very creative __________ who loves to draw colorful animals.

vocabularies.definition: The work of buying and selling things to make money.
vocabularies.example:
My father has a small business.
He travels for business every month.
They are in the clothing business.
quizzes: My father owns a small __________ that sells delicious Japanese food.
He often travels to different cities for __________ every single month.
They are in the clothing __________ and make shirts for children.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to prepare and cook food in a restaurant.
vocabularies.example:
My mother is a great cook.
She works as a cook in a hotel.
Who is going to cook dinner tonight?
quizzes: My mother is a great __________ and makes the best chocolate cake.
She works as a professional __________ in a famous city hotel.
Who is going to __________ a delicious dinner for us tonight?

vocabularies.definition: A doctor who checks your teeth and helps keep them clean and healthy.
vocabularies.example:
I have an appointment with the dentist.
The dentist checked my teeth.
You should visit the dentist twice a year.
quizzes: I have an __________ with the dentist at four o'clock this afternoon.
The __________ checked all my teeth and said they look very healthy.
You should visit the __________ twice a year to keep your smile bright.

vocabularies.definition: A person who designs and builds machines, bridges, or roads.
vocabularies.example:
My brother is a civil engineer.
Engineers build bridges and roads.
She wants to study to be an engineer.
quizzes: My older brother is a civil __________ who helps to build big roads.
__________ design and build amazing things like bridges and machines.
She is studying very hard because she wants to be an __________.

vocabularies.definition: A brave person whose job is to put out dangerous fires and save people.
vocabularies.example:
Firefighters are very brave.
The firefighters put out the fire quickly.
I saw a fire engine at the station.
quizzes: __________ are very brave people who help us during emergencies.
The __________ put out the dangerous fire in the building very quickly.
I saw a big red fire __________ at the fire station yesterday.

vocabularies.definition: A person who writes news reports for newspapers, magazines, or television.
vocabularies.example:
The journalist interviewed the famous actor.
She works as a journalist for a newspaper.
Journalists report the news.
quizzes: The __________ interviewed the famous actor about his new movie.
She works as a __________ and writes interesting reports for a newspaper.
__________ report the news and tell us what is happening in the world.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to control an office, shop, or a team of workers.
vocabularies.example:
He is the manager of the local bank.
I want to speak to the manager.
She is a very successful manager.
quizzes: He is the __________ of the local bank and looks after all the money.
I want to speak to the __________ of the shop about this broken toy.
She is a very successful __________ who leads a team of twenty people.

vocabularies.definition: A person who flies an airplane or helicopter.
vocabularies.example:
The pilot flew the plane across the ocean.
He wants to be a pilot when he grows up.
The pilot is in the cockpit.
quizzes: The brave __________ flew the large airplane safely across the ocean.
He wants to be a __________ and fly helicopters when he grows up.
The __________ is sitting in the cockpit, getting ready for the flight.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to catch thieves and make sure people follow the law.
vocabularies.example:
The police officer stopped the car.
My sister wants to be a police officer.
Police officers help to keep us safe.
quizzes: The __________ stopped the car because the driver was going too fast.
My older sister wants to be a __________ and help people in our city.
__________ help to keep our neighborhoods safe and catch thieves.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to collect and deliver letters and parcels to houses.
vocabularies.example:
The postman delivered a letter this morning.
Our postman is very friendly.
Does the postman come on Saturdays?
quizzes: The __________ delivered a large parcel to our house early this morning.
Our __________ is very friendly and always says hello to my dog.
Does the __________ deliver letters and magazines on Saturdays?

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to write instructions and code for computers.
vocabularies.example:
He works as a computer programmer.
Programmers write software and apps.
She is studying to be a programmer.
quizzes: He works as a computer __________ and makes fun games for kids.
__________ write the code and instructions that make apps work.
She is studying computer science to be a __________ in the future.

vocabularies.definition: A person who studies the natural world, does experiments, and learns how things work.
vocabularies.example:
Scientists study the world around us.
She wants to be a scientist when she grows up.
The scientist is working in a lab.
quizzes: __________ study the natural world and how everything around us works.
She wants to be a __________ and work in a laboratory doing experiments.
The __________ is carefully working in a lab with a microscope.

vocabularies.definition: A person who works in an office answering phones, typing letters, and organizing meetings.
vocabularies.example:
The secretary is typing an email.
He works as a secretary in a lawyer's office.
The secretary answered the phone.
quizzes: The __________ is typing an important email to a business partner.
He works as a __________ in an office and organizes many meetings.
The __________ answered the telephone and took a message for the boss.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to sing songs, often with a band or on stage.
vocabularies.example:
She is a very famous pop singer.
He wants to be a professional singer.
The singer has a beautiful voice.
quizzes: She is a very famous pop __________ who has lots of fans.
He wants to be a professional __________ and perform in a band.
The __________ has a beautiful, clear voice that sounds like an angel.

vocabularies.definition: A person who serves in an army and wears a military uniform.
vocabularies.example:
He is a soldier in the army.
Soldiers wear uniforms and carry heavy packs.
My grandfather was a soldier.
quizzes: He is a brave __________ in the army and travels all over the world.
__________ wear camouflage uniforms and carry heavy packs on their backs.
My grandfather was a decorated __________ in the Second World War.

vocabularies.definition: A man who brings food and drinks to customers' tables in a restaurant.
vocabularies.example:
The waiter brought our drinks to the table.
He works as a waiter in a French restaurant.
The waiter was very polite.
quizzes: The polite __________ brought our cold drinks to our table very quickly.
He works as a __________ in a fancy French restaurant in the city center.
The __________ was very friendly and recommended a delicious pasta dish.

vocabularies.definition: A woman who brings food and drinks to customers' tables in a restaurant.
vocabularies.example:
The waitress took our order.
She is a very efficient waitress.
Is the waitress coming to our table?
quizzes: Our __________ took our food order and brought us some bread to eat.
She is a very fast and efficient __________ at a busy Italian cafe.
Is the __________ coming to our table with the dessert menu now?

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to write books or stories.
vocabularies.example:
J.K. Rowling is a very famous writer.
He wants to be a travel writer.
The writer is signing his new book.
quizzes: J.K. Rowling is a very famous __________ who wrote the Harry Potter books.
He wants to be a travel __________ and write about exotic countries.
The __________ is signing copies of his new book at the shop today.

grammars.description: Dùng trước danh từ chỉ nghề nghiệp.
grammars.example:
He is an engineer.
She is a journalist.

vocabularies.example:
The airport is far from the city center.
We went to the airport to meet my uncle.
There are many planes at the airport.
quizzes: We went to the _________ to meet my uncle who just flew from London.
The big __________ is far away from the city center.
Look at all the large airplanes taking off at the __________!

vocabularies.example:
I need to go to the bank to get some money.
The bank is closed on Sundays.
Is there a bank near here?
quizzes: I need to go to the __________ to get some money for our shopping.
Is the __________ open today, or is it closed on Sundays?
There is a small __________ next to the supermarket.

vocabularies.example:
We drove across the bridge.
This bridge is very old.
The bridge connects the two sides of the river.
quizzes: We carefully drove our car across the long __________ to cross the water.
This wooden __________ is very old and a bit shaky.
The __________ connects the two different sides of the wide river.

vocabularies.example:
The king lives in a huge castle.
We visited an ancient castle in Scotland.
The castle has high walls.
quizzes: The king and queen live in a huge stone __________.
We visited an ancient __________ with very high walls last summer.
Is there a secret room hidden inside the old __________?

vocabularies.example:
Let's go to the cinema tonight.
The cinema is next to the bank.
What's on at the cinema?
quizzes: Let's go to the __________ tonight to watch the new cartoon.
The __________ is located right next to the city bank.
What is the name of the movie playing at the __________ today?

vocabularies.example:
My uncle works in a car factory.
The factory produces thousands of toys.
The factory is very large.
quizzes: My uncle works in a busy __________ that makes fast cars.
This large __________ produces thousands of colorful toys every day.
The __________ is much bigger than our school building.

vocabularies.example:
We saw dinosaur skeletons at the museum.
The history museum is very interesting.
Admission to the museum is free.
quizzes: We saw a giant dinosaur skeleton at the city __________.
The history __________ is a very interesting place to visit.
You don't have to pay because the __________ is free today.

vocabularies.example:
The police station is on High Street.
You should report the lost bag to the police station.
I saw a police car near the station.
quizzes: The local __________ is located on High Street, near the park.
You should go to the __________ to report your lost bag.
I saw a blue and white police car parked outside the __________.

vocabularies.example:
I need to go to the post office to buy stamps.
The post office is near the market.
Is the post office open now?
quizzes: I need to go to the __________ to buy some stamps for my letters.
The __________ is very close to the fruit market.
Is the __________ open now? I need to send this parcel.

vocabularies.example:

I will meet you at the railway station.
The railway station is very busy.
quizzes: Our train leaves the __________ at exactly 9 o'clock.
I will meet you at the __________ entrance this afternoon.
The __________ is always very busy with people traveling.

vocabularies.example:
The shop is on the corner of the street.

Turn right at the next corner.
quizzes: The little sweet shop is right on the __________ of the street.

vocabularies.example:
The fire station is near the school.
I saw the fire engines at the fire station.
Firefighters work in a fire station.
quizzes: The municipal __________ is located very near to our school.
I saw two big red fire engines parked at the __________.

vocabularies.example:
We buy fresh vegetables at the market.
The market is very crowded on Saturdays.
I like the smell of the flower market.
quizzes: My mom buys fresh vegetables and fruit at the local __________.
The Saturday __________ is always very crowded with people.
I really like the beautiful smell of the flower __________.

vocabularies.example:
Please walk on the pavement, not the road.
The pavement is wet from the rain.
He is sweeping the pavement.
quizzes: Please stay safe and walk on the __________, not on the road.
The concrete __________ is very wet and slippery from the rain.
The worker is carefully sweeping the leaves off the __________.

vocabularies.example:
A police car drove past with its lights flashing.
I saw two police cars near the bank.
The police car chased the thief.
quizzes: A blue __________ drove past us with its lights flashing brightly.
I saw two __________ parked near the entrance of the bank.
The fast __________ chased the thief down the street.

vocabularies.example:

He works in a tall skyscraper.
quizzes: There are many extremely tall __________ in New York City.
The view from the top floor of the __________ is amazing.
My father works in a very tall __________ in the city center.

vocabularies.example:
We are going to the theater to see a play.
The theater was full of people.
She loves acting in the theater.
quizzes: We are going to the __________ tonight to see a funny play.
The __________ was completely full of excited people.
She is a great actress and she loves acting in the __________.

vocabularies.example:
There is a lot of traffic in the morning.
The traffic was very slow today.
Be careful of the heavy traffic.
quizzes: There is always a lot of __________ on the roads in the morning.
The __________ was very slow today because of the rain.
Please be careful of the heavy __________ when you cross the street.

vocabularies.example:
Can you show me the way to the park?
This is the fastest way to get home.
Which way should we go?
quizzes: Excuse me, can you show me the __________ to the nearest park?
This is the fastest and easiest __________ to get back home.
Which __________ should we go at the next corner, left or right?

grammars.description: Dùng để chỉ đường đến các địa điểm.
grammars.example:

The museum is opposite the cinema.

vocabularies.example:
I use a dictionary to look up new words.
Don't forget to bring your dictionary to class.
This is a very good English dictionary.
quizzes: I use a heavy __________ to look up the meaning of new words.

vocabularies.example:
We learn about rivers and mountains in geography.
My favorite subject is geography.
He has a geography test tomorrow.
quizzes: We learn about different countries, rivers, and mountains in our __________ lesson.

vocabularies.example:
I love learning about the past in history.
History is a very interesting subject.
Our history teacher is very kind.
quizzes: I love learning about kings, queens, and the past in my __________ class.

How many languages can you speak?
English is an international language.

quizzes: How many different __________ can you speak? I can speak two.

Mathematics is quite difficult for me.
We use a calculator in mathematics.
He is very good at mathematics.
quizzes: Solving long number problems in __________ can be quite difficult for me.

vocabularies.example:
What is your favorite school subject?
We study ten subjects at school.
History is my least favorite subject.
quizzes: What is your favorite school __________? I like art the most.
We study ten different __________ at school every single week.
History is my least favorite __________ because I find it a bit boring.

vocabularies.example:
I am studying hard for my English exam.
Did you pass your math exam?
The exam was very long.
quizzes: I am studying very hard for my big English __________ next week.

The science __________ was very long and had many difficult questions.

vocabularies.example:
He got a good grade in his history project.
I'm in the fifth grade.
She always gets high grades.
quizzes: He got a very good __________ in his interesting history project.
I'm currently in the fifth __________ at my primary school.
She is a top student and she always gets high __________ in her tests.

vocabularies.example:
My brother is studying at university.
He wants to go to university next year.
The university is very old and famous.
quizzes: My older brother is currently studying engineering at ___________.
He wants to go to __________ next year after he finishes high school.
The __________ building is very old, big, and famous in our city.

I have piano practice after school.
Practice makes perfect.
quizzes: You really need to __________ your English speaking every day.
