quizzes: Do you know what the secret __________ password for this house is?
I can't connect to the internet because the __________ signal is very weak.
Is there any free __________ for customers to use in this small cafe?

grammars.description: Dùng để nói về khả năng sử dụng công nghệ.
grammars.example:
I can use a computer.
He can't find the website.

vocabularies.definition: Not costing much money.
vocabularies.example:
This watch is very cheap.
I'm looking for a cheap flight.
The tickets were cheap.
quizzes: This notebook is very __________ ; it only costs one dollar at the market today.
I bought a __________ toy today; it's not very strong but it is fun to play with.
Is there anything __________ that I can buy for my friend with my small pocket money?

vocabularies.definition: Costing a lot of money.
vocabularies.example:
This car is very expensive.
Healthy food can be expensive.
That's an expensive dress.
quizzes: That gold watch is way too __________ for me! It costs over a thousand dollars.
The large Italian restaurant is quite __________ but the food is absolutely delicious.
I don't have enough money to buy that __________ doll; let's find a cheaper one.

vocabularies.definition: Firm and strong, or difficult to do.
vocabularies.example:
The bed is too hard.
This exercise is very hard.
Diamond is a hard material.
quizzes: The stone floor is very __________ and cold; please don't sit on it for too long now.
This math homework is really __________ today! I need some help to solve the problem.
The old bread is too __________ to eat; let's throw it away and get some fresh bread.

vocabularies.definition: Not hard or firm, and easy to press.
vocabularies.example:
The pillow is nice and soft.
Her skin is very soft.
I like soft music.
quizzes: The little kitten's fur is extremely __________ and warm when you touch it gently.
My new pillow is very __________ and cozy; I think I will sleep very well tonight.
I really love eating __________ fresh bread with sweet strawberry jam for breakfast.

vocabularies.definition: Not heavy, or having lots of brightness.
vocabularies.example:
This bag is very light.
I love the morning light.
She is wearing a light blue dress.
quizzes: This empty box is very __________ ; even a small child can carry it into the house.
Feathers are extremely __________ and they can fly easily in the soft summer breeze.
The blue bag is much __________ than the red one; you can carry it to the car now.

vocabularies.definition: Weighing a lot.
vocabularies.example:
The box is too heavy to carry.
It's raining heavy outside.
He has a heavy suitcase.
quizzes: This large suitcase is way too __________ for me to carry up to the third floor now.
Elephants are extremely __________ animals that can weigh many thousands of kilos.
Please help me carry this __________ wooden table into the garden for our picnic.

vocabularies.definition: With little or no light.
vocabularies.example:
It's getting dark outside.
She has dark hair.
I like dark chocolate.
quizzes: It is getting extremely __________ outside now; please turn on the bright lights.
She has very __________ hair and beautiful brown eyes that everyone admires today.
I really love eating __________ chocolate because it is a bit bitter and very tasty.

vocabularies.definition: Strong and easy to see; full of light.
vocabularies.example:
The sun is very bright today.
She has a bright future.
I like bright colors.
quizzes: The yellow sun is extremely __________ today; you should wear your dark sunglasses.
She has an extremely __________ future ahead of her because she is so hardworking.
I really like wearing __________ colors like red, yellow, and orange in the summer.

vocabularies.definition: Before the usual or expected time.
vocabularies.example:
I woke up early this morning.
The train arrived early.
He is in his early twenties.
quizzes: I woke up very __________ this morning to watch the beautiful sunrise over the sea.
The fast train arrived exactly ten minutes __________ for our journey to the city.
He is currently in his __________ twenties and he is already a professional pilot.

vocabularies.definition: After the usual or planned time.
vocabularies.example:
Don't be late for school!
The bus was late today.
It's getting very late.
quizzes: Please don't be __________ for school! The first lesson starts exactly at eight o'clock.
The red bus was very __________ today because there was a long traffic jam in city.
It's getting very __________ now; the moon is high in the sky and we must go home.

vocabularies.definition: With nothing inside.
vocabularies.example:
The room was empty.
Her glass is empty.
The box is empty.
quizzes: The classroom was completely __________ when I arrived this morning; everyone's out.
Her glass is currently __________ ; would you like some more fresh orange juice now?
The small wooden box is completely __________ ; I wonder where the gold coins went.

vocabularies.definition: Containing as much as possible.
vocabularies.example:
The cup is full of milk.
The bus was full of people.
I am full; I can't eat any more.
quizzes: The ceramic cup is completely __________ of fresh milk for the little white kitten.
The school bus was absolutely __________ of happy children going on their field trip.
I am extremely __________ ; I really can't eat another single slice of that cake now.

vocabularies.definition: Unusual or surprising.
vocabularies.example:
That's a strange noise.
I had a strange dream last night.
He is a bit strange.
quizzes: That's a truly __________ noise coming from the forest! I wonder what it could be.
I had an extremely __________ and surprising dream about flying in outer space tonight.
He is a bit __________ sometimes, but he is a truly kind and loyal friend to everyone.

vocabularies.definition: Very bad or unpleasant.
vocabularies.example:
The weather was terrible yesterday.
I have a terrible headache.
That's a terrible mistake.
quizzes: The weather was absolutely __________ yesterday with heavy rain and very cold wind.
I have an absolutely __________ headache today; I think I'll stay home from school.
That was a truly __________ mistake to make on your exam; please check your work.

vocabularies.definition: Very good, pleasant, or enjoyable.
vocabularies.example:
We had a wonderful holiday.
The view from the mountain is wonderful.
She is a wonderful person.
quizzes: We all had a truly __________ holiday at the beach with lots of sun and swimming.
The view from the top of the high mountain is absolutely __________ and very clear.
She is a truly __________ person who is always kind and helpful to all her friends.

vocabularies.definition: Not interesting or exciting.
vocabularies.example:
The movie was very boring.
I'm bored with this book.
Do you find history boring?
quizzes: The new movie was unfortunately very __________ and I almost fell asleep in the chair.
I'm already getting a bit __________ with this long book; let's go out and play now.
Do you actually find learning about history __________ , or do you think it's fun?

vocabularies.definition: Making you feel very happy or enthusiastic.
vocabularies.example:
The football match was very exciting.
It's an exciting project.
I'm so excited about the party.
quizzes: The football match last night was extremely __________ and our team finally won it!
It's an extremely __________ project to build a large model of our solar system today.
I'm so extremely __________ about the surprise party! I can't wait to see everyone.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling afraid or scared.
vocabularies.example:
She is frightened of spiders.
I was frightened by the loud noise.
Don't be frightened!
quizzes: She is extremely __________ of small spiders and always runs away when she sees one.
I was truly __________ by the extremely loud noise of the thunder last night today.
Please don't be __________ ! I'm here with you and everything is going to be fine.

vocabularies.definition: Not afraid of dangerous or difficult things.
vocabularies.example:
The firefighters were very brave.
She was brave to speak the truth.
He is a brave boy.
quizzes: The brave firefighters were extremely __________ when they saved the kitten from tree.
She was extremely __________ to speak the whole truth, even when it was very difficult.
He is a truly __________ boy who isn't afraid of the dark room or the big dog now.

vocabularies.definition: Quick to learn and understand things.
vocabularies.example:
She is a very clever student.
He is clever at fixing things.
That was a clever idea.
quizzes: She is an extremely __________ student who always gets the best marks in our class.
He is extremely __________ at fixing broken toys and machines in his small garage.
That was a truly __________ idea to solve the difficult problem we had this morning.

vocabularies.definition: Kind and pleasant towards others.
vocabularies.example:
The neighbors are very friendly.
She has a friendly smile.
It's a friendly dog.
quizzes: Our new neighbors are extremely __________ and they always say hello with a smile.
She has an extremely __________ and kind smile that makes everyone feel happy today.
It's an extremely __________ and gentle dog that loves to play with young children.

vocabularies.definition: Caring and helpful towards other people.
vocabularies.example:
He is very kind to old people.
Thank you for your kind words.
She is a kind teacher.
quizzes: He is always extremely __________ to old people and helps them carry their heavy bags.
Thank you so much for your truly __________ words! They made me feel much better now.
She is a truly __________ teacher who always takes time to help every single student.

vocabularies.definition: Not wanting to work or use any effort.
vocabularies.example:
Don't be lazy; do your homework!
He is a lazy cat.
I feel a bit lazy today.
quizzes: Please don't be so __________ ! You really should finish all your school work today.
He is an extremely __________ cat that sleeps on the sofa all day long in the sun.
I feel a bit __________ today, so I think I'll just stay in bed and read my new book.

vocabularies.definition: Making a lot of loud or unpleasant noise.
vocabularies.example:
The children are being very noisy.
It's a noisy street.
The machine is very noisy.
quizzes: The happy children are being extremely __________ today while they play in the park.
It's an extremely __________ street with many fast cars and loud buses all day long.
The old machine is extremely __________ and it's making a terrible sound right now.

vocabularies.definition: Making very little or no noise.
vocabularies.example:
Please be quiet in the library.
It's a quiet village.
He is a quiet person.
quizzes: Please be extremely __________ in the school library so that everyone can study now.
It's an extremely __________ and peaceful village with very few cars or loud noises.
He is a truly __________ and shy person who doesn't like to speak in front of others.

grammars.description: Dùng để so sánh hai vật hoặc hai người.
grammars.example:
This watch is cheaper than that one.
The red bag is heavier than the blue one.

vocabularies.definition: To reach the end of a journey.
vocabularies.example:
The train arrives at 10 o'clock.
When did you arrive home?
We arrived at the airport early.
quizzes: The fast train usually __________ at exactly six o'clock every evening.
When exactly did you __________ at the hotel after your long journey?
He unfortunately __________ half an hour late for the important school meeting.

vocabularies.definition: To think that something is true.
vocabularies.example:
I believe you.
Do you believe in ghosts?
I can't believe it's already June.
quizzes: I truly __________ you and I know that you are telling me the whole truth.
Do you actually __________ in real magic, or do you think they are just tricks?
I simply can't __________ it! You actually won the first prize in the race!

vocabularies.definition: To choose something after thinking about several options.
vocabularies.example:
I can't decide which dress to wear.
They decided to go to the beach.
Have you decided on a name for the baby?
quizzes: I simply can't __________ yet which movie I want to see this afternoon.
He finally __________ to go home early because he was feeling a bit tired.
Have you finally __________ what you want to eat for your birthday dinner?

vocabularies.definition: To make something clear or easy to understand by describing it.
vocabularies.example:
Can you explain the rules of the game?
The teacher explained the lesson.
He explained why he was late.
quizzes: Could you please __________ the rules of the new game to everyone again?
Can you actually __________ why you were so late for class this morning?
He __________ the difficult science lesson very clearly to all the students.

vocabularies.definition: To try to give an answer without being sure it is right.
vocabularies.example:
Can you guess what's in the box?
I guess he is about thirty years old.
Guess who I saw today!
quizzes: Can you actually __________ what exactly is inside the large wooden box today?
I __________ he is about thirty years old, but I could be completely wrong.
__________ who I saw at the park today! It was our old teacher from last year!

vocabularies.definition: To take place, often without being planned.
vocabularies.example:
What happened to your arm?
Accidents happen.
I don't know what will happen next.
quizzes: What exactly __________ to your poor arm? It looks a bit red and sore today.
Accidents unfortunately __________ sometimes, so please be more careful next time.
I truly don't know what will __________ next in this exciting adventure story.

vocabularies.definition: To want something to happen and think that it could happen.
vocabularies.example:
I hope you have a great time.
I hope it doesn't rain.
We hope to see you soon.
quizzes: I truly __________ you have a truly great and wonderful time on your holiday!
I truly __________ it doesn't rain heavily tomorrow for our school picnic.
We truly __________ to see you again very soon; please come and visit us!

vocabularies.definition: To have a particular meaning or intention.
vocabularies.example:
What does this word mean?
I didn't mean to hurt you.
What do you mean by that?
quizzes: What exactly does this difficult word __________ ? I need to look it up now.
I'm extremely sorry, I strictly didn't __________ to hurt your feelings today.
He says exactly what he __________ and he is always very honest with everyone.

vocabularies.definition: To like one thing or person better than another.
vocabularies.example:
I prefer tea to coffee.
Which do you prefer, apples or oranges?
I prefer staying at home tonight.
quizzes: I truly __________ having some hot tea to coffee in the early morning now.
Which exactly do you __________ eating, sweet juicy apples or sour oranges?
I truly __________ staying at home and reading tonight than going to the park.

vocabularies.definition: To keep a piece of information in your mind.
vocabularies.example:
Do you remember me?
I can't remember his name.
Remember to lock the door.
quizzes: Do you actually __________ me from when we met at the park last summer today?
I'm extremely sorry, I strictly can't __________ his name at this moment now.
Please __________ to lock the front door very carefully before you go to bed.

vocabularies.definition: To mention an idea or a plan for someone else to think about.
vocabularies.example:
I suggest we go for a walk.
What do you suggest I do?
He suggested a new plan.
quizzes: I truly __________ we go for a long walk in the park while the sun is shining.
What exactly do you __________ I do for my upcoming school science project now?
He very kindly __________ a brand new and exciting plan for our summer holiday.

vocabularies.definition: To know the meaning of something that someone says.
vocabularies.example:
Do you understand the question?
I don't understand French.
She understands how I feel.
quizzes: Do you actually __________ the difficult math question that the teacher asked now?
I'm sorry, I strictly don't __________ any French words at all right now.
She truly __________ exactly how I feel when I am sad or happy about something.

vocabularies.definition: To have the same opinion as someone else.
vocabularies.example:
I agree with you.
Do you agree to the plan?
They finally agreed on a price.
quizzes: I truly __________ with you and I think that's an absolutely great idea today.
Do you actually __________ to follow the new plan for our school project now?
They finally and luckily __________ on a fair price for the old wooden table.

vocabularies.definition: To get things together and keep them.
vocabularies.example:
She collects stamps.
I need to collect my mail.
The children are collecting shells.
quizzes: She really likes to __________ rare and colorful stamps from all over the world.
I really need to go and __________ my mail from the mailbox near the station.
The happy children are currently __________ pretty shells on the sandy beach.

vocabularies.definition: To say that you are not happy about something.
vocabularies.example:
He's always complaining about the weather.
She complained to the manager.
I have nothing to complain about.
quizzes: He is unfortunately always __________ about the cold weather during the winter.
She very politely __________ to the manager about the slow service today now.
I truly have strictly nothing at all to __________ about my new school today.

vocabularies.definition: To say what someone or something is like.
vocabularies.example:
Can you describe the man you saw?
Describe your favorite animal.
Words cannot describe how happy I am.
quizzes: Can you actually __________ the man you saw at the park earlier this morning?
Please try to __________ your favorite wild animal in as much detail as you can.
Words strictly cannot __________ just how happy I am that you came to visit me.

vocabularies.definition: To not remember something.
vocabularies.example:
Don't forget your umbrella.
I forgot to call him.
I'll never forget that day.
quizzes: Please don't __________ to take your blue umbrella with you! It looks like rain.
I'm extremely sorry, I strictly __________ to call him back yesterday evening.
I will truly and strictly never __________ that wonderful day at the sandy beach.

vocabularies.definition: To form a picture or idea in your mind.
vocabularies.example:
Can you imagine living on the moon?
Imagine you are a bird.
I can't imagine what it's like.
quizzes: Can you actually __________ living on the moon in a giant white space station?
Just __________ you are a beautiful colorful bird flying high in the blue sky.
I truly strictly can't __________ what it's like to live in such a cold place.

vocabularies.definition: To make something better.
vocabularies.example:
I want to improve my English.
His health is improving.
The weather is starting to improve.
quizzes: I truly want to __________ my English speaking skills during this summer camp.
His health is slowly __________ after he stayed in the hospital for two weeks.
The weather is finally starting to __________ after the long and heavy rain now.

vocabularies.definition: To go away from a place.
vocabularies.example:
What time does the train leave?
Don't leave your bag here.
He left his keys on the table.
quizzes: What exactly time does the fast train __________ the city station this evening?
Please don't __________ your heavy bag here on the floor; someone might trip.
He __________ his silver house keys on the wooden table this morning by mistake.

vocabularies.definition: To speak or write about something briefly.
vocabularies.example:
He didn't mention the news to me.
Did I mention that I bought a new car?
Don't mention it!
quizzes: He strictly didn't __________ the exciting news to me when I saw him earlier.
Did I actually __________ that I proudly bought a brand new red car recently?
Don't __________ it! I was very happy to help you with your school project now.

vocabularies.definition: To ask someone if they would like something.
vocabularies.example:
He offered me a drink.
She offered to help me.
What should I offer them?
quizzes: He very kindly __________ me a glass of cold water when I arrived at his house.
She very kindly __________ to help me with my heavy bags after school today now.
What exactly should I __________ them to eat when they visit us this afternoon?

vocabularies.definition: To do something regularly to become better at it.
vocabularies.example:
You need to practise your piano every day.
Practise speaking English with your friends.
She is practising her dance.
quizzes: You really need to __________ your piano lessons every single day after school.
Try to __________ speaking English with your friends at the park every weekend.
She is currently __________ her new dance in her room for the school show now.

vocabularies.definition: To say that you will definitely do something.
vocabularies.example:
I promise I'll be there.
Do you promise to tell the truth?
He broke his promise.
quizzes: I truly __________ I'll be there on time for your fun birthday party tonight!
Do you actually __________ to tell the whole truth and nothing but the truth?
He unfortunately broke his __________ and didn't come to help me with my work.

vocabularies.definition: To feel nervous or unhappy about something that might happen.
vocabularies.example:
Don't worry about it.
I'm worried about the exam.
What are you worrying about?
quizzes: Please don't __________ about it at all; everything is going to be just fine today.
I am extremely __________ about my upcoming school science exam next week now.
What exactly are you __________ about? Tell me and I'll try to help you out now.

grammars.description: Một số động từ theo sau là động từ thêm -ing.
grammars.example:
I prefer staying at home.
I suggest going for a walk.

vocabularies.definition: Coming before all others in time or order.
vocabularies.example:
He was the first to arrive.
This is my first time in London.
January is the first month.
quizzes: He was proudly the very __________ student to arrive at school this morning now.
This is actually my very __________ time visiting the large city of London today.
__________ is the first month of every single new year in the whole world today.

vocabularies.definition: Coming immediately after the first one.
vocabularies.example:
She won the second prize.
Wait a second, please.
This is the second time I've called.
quizzes: She proudly won the __________ prize in the school's exciting art competition now.
Please wait just a __________ ! I forgot to take my heavy bag from the car today.
This is actually the __________ time I have called you on the phone this afternoon.

vocabularies.definition: Coming immediately after the second one.
vocabularies.example:
He lives on the third floor.
It's his third birthday.
March is the third month.
quizzes: He lives on the __________ floor of that very high and modern apartment building.
Today is his happy __________ birthday and he is having a large party with friends.
March is actually the __________ month of the year; it comes after the month of February.

vocabularies.definition: Coming immediately after the third one.
vocabularies.example:
Thursday is the fourth day of the week.
It's the fourth of July.
She finished in fourth place.
quizzes: Thursday is proudly the __________ day of the week in many countries around world.
Today is actually the __________ of July; happy holiday to all our friends today!
She unfortunately finished in __________ place in the very fast swimming race today.

vocabularies.definition: Coming immediately after the fourth one.
vocabularies.example:
May is the fifth month.
He is in the fifth grade.
This is the fifth time.
quizzes: May is actually the __________ month of the year; it's a very beautiful spring month.
He is currently in the __________ grade at school and he loves his new teacher now.
This is actually the __________ time that I have told you to be quiet in the library.

vocabularies.definition: One single time.
vocabularies.example:
I've been to Paris once.
I brush my teeth once a day.
Once upon a time...
quizzes: I only go swimming __________ a week because I am very busy with my school work now.
I have only been to the large city of Paris __________ in my whole life so far today.
__________ upon a time, there was a very brave and kind little princess in a forest.

vocabularies.definition: Two times.
vocabularies.example:
I go to the gym twice a week.
He called me twice today.
Think twice before you act.
quizzes: Please remember to brush your teeth at least __________ a day, morning and night.
I have already seen that exciting movie __________ because I liked it so much today.
You should think __________ before you make an important decision about your future.

vocabularies.definition: A small number of people or things.
vocabularies.example:
I have a few friends in this city.
Wait a few minutes.
Can I have a few cookies?
quizzes: I only have __________ friends coming to my small house for my birthday party now.
I'll be ready in just __________ minutes; please wait for me at the front door now.
There are __________ apples left in the wooden bowl; would you like to eat one now?

vocabularies.definition: A large number of people or things.
vocabularies.example:
How many sisters do you have?
There are many people at the park.
Many students like music.
quizzes: How __________ children are in your class at school this year? Is it a large class?
There are very __________ beautiful colorful flowers in the garden during the spring.
I have visited __________ different countries with my family on our summer holidays.

vocabularies.definition: A large amount of something.
vocabularies.example:
How much milk is left?
I don't have much money.
Thank you very much!
quizzes: How __________ does this new red notebook cost? I want to buy it for my school work.
I don't have very __________ free time today because I have to finish my homework.
Thank you so __________ for your very kind help! I truly appreciate it today now.

vocabularies.definition: A group of twelve things.
vocabularies.example:
I bought a dozen eggs.
There were a dozen people at the meeting.
He has dozens of books.
quizzes: I bought a __________ fresh eggs from the local market this morning for breakfast.
A __________ is exactly the number twelve; please remember this for your math test.
There were a __________ colorful roses in the beautiful vase on the wooden table.

vocabularies.definition: The number 0.
vocabularies.example:
The temperature is zero degrees.
Zero plus zero is zero.
My score was zero.
quizzes: The temperature is currently __________ degrees; it's very cold and might snow today.
The final score of the football match was three-__________ to our favorite team now.
__________ comes before the number one; please write it carefully in your notebook.

vocabularies.definition: The number 100.
vocabularies.example:
There were a hundred people in the room.
One hundred years is a century.
I have a hundred dollars.
quizzes: There are exactly one __________ cents in one dollar; please remember this for test.
I have read over one __________ interesting books this year! I really love reading.
The huge old tree is over one __________ years old and it is truly very beautiful.

vocabularies.definition: The number 1,000.
vocabularies.example:
A thousand people attended the match.
I have a thousand questions.
Ten hundreds make a thousand.
quizzes: There are exactly one __________ meters in one kilometer; please remember this now.
Over one __________ people went to the exciting concert in the park last night now.
This old gold coin is over two __________ years old and it is worth a lot of money.

vocabularies.definition: The number 1,000,000.
vocabularies.example:
The population is ten million.
He won a million dollars.
A million is a very large number.
quizzes: There are over one __________ people living in this large and busy city today now.
I wish I had a __________ dollars! I would buy a large house and a fast red car.
The sun is many __________ kilometers away from our planet, the beautiful Earth.

vocabularies.example:
There are over seven billion people on Earth.
The project cost a billion dollars.
A billion has nine zeros.
quizzes: There are over eight __________ people living on our planet today; that's a lot!
The universe is many __________ years old and it is truly huge and very mysterious.
It costs many __________ dollars to build a large and modern space station today.

vocabularies.definition: One of four equal parts of something.
vocabularies.example:
A quarter of an hour is fifteen minutes.
Cut the apple into quarters.
It's a quarter past three.
quizzes: It's currently a __________ past ten o'clock; let's go to the park for a walk now.
Please cut the large juicy apple into __________ so that we can all share it today.
A __________ is exactly twenty-five cents; please remember this for your math test.

vocabularies.definition: One of two equal parts of something.
vocabularies.example:
Cut it in half.
An hour and a half.
It's half past ten.
quizzes: I ate __________ of the large delicious pizza and I'm already feeling very full now.
It's currently __________ past eight o'clock; it's time for the first school lesson.
Please cut the sandwich in __________ so that my little brother and I can share it.

vocabularies.definition: Twice as much or as many as usual.
vocabularies.example:
I need a double room.
Can I have a double burger?
Prices have doubled.
quizzes: Two is the __________ of one; please remember this for your math homework today now.
I'd like a __________ scoop of chocolate ice cream in a large wafer cone today now.
Please __________ check your work before you give it to the teacher this morning.

vocabularies.definition: Three times as much or as many as usual.
vocabularies.example:
The population tripled in ten years.
I'll take a triple scoop of ice cream.
Triple the number eight is twenty-four.
quizzes: Three is the __________ of one; please remember this for your math homework today.
I had a __________ scoop of delicious fruit ice cream for my dessert last night.
We need to __________ our efforts if we want to finish this large project on time.

vocabularies.definition: More than two but not many.
vocabularies.example:
I have seen that movie several times.
Several people were injured.
Wait for several minutes.
quizzes: I've been to that beautiful park __________ times this year with my whole family.
There are __________ different ways to solve this difficult math problem today now.
He very kindly bought __________ different kinds of fruit for us to eat for lunch.

vocabularies.definition: As much as is needed.
vocabularies.example:
Is there enough food for everyone?
I've had enough, thank you.
He is old enough to drive.
quizzes: Do you have __________ money to buy that expensive new toy at the shop today now?
I have fortunately had __________ to eat; thank you for the delicious dinner today.
There isn't __________ space in this small box for all your toys; find a larger one.

vocabularies.definition: A large amount; as much as you need.
vocabularies.example:
We have plenty of time.
Eat plenty of fruit and vegetables.
There's plenty of room.
quizzes: Don't worry, we have __________ of time to get to the station before the train goes.
There is __________ of fresh water in the large blue bottle; please drink some now.
We fortunately have __________ of food for everyone at the surprise birthday party.

vocabularies.definition: Nearly all of a group or amount.
vocabularies.example:
Most children like chocolate.
Most of the students were late.
What do you like most?
quizzes: __________ children in our class really love playing football during the lunch break.
Which of these beautiful colorful flowers do you like the __________ today, tell me.
__________ of the students have already finished their science homework for today.

vocabularies.definition: The whole number or amount of something.
vocabularies.example:
All the children are playing.
Is that all?
I love all of you.
quizzes: __________ the children in the class were very happy when the school holiday began.
Is that __________ for today, or do you have any more questions for me to answer now?
I truly love __________ my friends and family very much; they are all very kind people.

grammars.description: Phân biệt dùng cho danh từ đếm được và không đếm được.
grammars.example:
How many books?
How much water?

vocabularies.definition: A game for two people played on a board with 64 black and white squares.
vocabularies.example:
He is playing chess with his grandfather.
Chess is a difficult game to learn.
She won the school chess competition.
quizzes: He is currently playing __________ with his kind grandfather on the wooden porch now.
__________ is actually a truly difficult game to learn, but it is very fun to play.
She proudly won the school __________ competition and got a shiny gold trophy today.

vocabularies.definition: A group of things that someone has gathered together, like stamps or coins.
vocabularies.example:
He has a large collection of stamps.
She added a new coin to her collection.
The museum has a beautiful art collection.
quizzes: He has an absolutely huge and impressive __________ of rare stamps from all over world.
She proudly added a brand new shiny silver coin to her growing __________ today now.
The city museum has an absolutely beautiful art __________ that everyone should see.

vocabularies.definition: A musical instrument that you hit with sticks or your hands to make a sound.
vocabularies.example:
He plays the drums in a band.
Don't hit the drum so loudly!
She is learning to play the drums.
quizzes: He plays the __________ in a loud rock band with his best friends every weekend now.
Please don't hit the __________ so loudly! I'm trying to study for my science exam now.
She is currently learning to play the __________ at the local school of music today.

vocabularies.definition: A musical instrument with strings that you play by pulling them with your fingers.
vocabularies.example:
He can play the guitar very well.
I want to buy a new electric guitar.
She sang a song while playing the guitar.
quizzes: He plays the acoustic __________ very beautifully and he even writes his own songs now.
I really want to learn how to play the __________ so I can join the school's music band.
She very kindly bought a brand new shiny black __________ for her son's happy birthday.

vocabularies.definition: An activity that you enjoy doing in your free time.
vocabularies.example:
My hobby is reading books.
What is your favorite hobby?
He has many interesting hobbies.
quizzes: Photography is actually my very favorite __________ and I take my camera everywhere today.
What exactly is your favorite __________ to do when you have some free time after school?
He has many different and interesting __________ , like bird watching and drawing today.

vocabularies.definition: An object used for making music, such as a piano or a drum.
vocabularies.example:
The piano is a beautiful musical instrument.
Can you play any musical instruments?
He is learning to play several instruments.
quizzes: Which musical __________ would you truly like to learn how to play the most today now?
The piano is an absolutely beautiful __________ that can make many wonderful sounds today.
She can play several different __________ , including the violin and the silver flute.

vocabularies.definition: A thin book with a paper cover that comes out every week or month.
vocabularies.example:
I like reading fashion magazines.
She bought a magazine at the airport.
This magazine is published monthly.
quizzes: I really love reading this science __________ because it has many colorful pictures today.
She very kindly bought a new fashion __________ to read on the long train journey today.
There are many different __________ on the wooden table in the quiet waiting room now.

vocabularies.definition: The activity of using paint to create a picture.
vocabularies.example:
Painting is a very relaxing hobby.
That is a beautiful oil painting.
She is taking painting classes.
quizzes: She is currently __________ a truly beautiful picture of the blue sea and the sun now.
That __________ of the colorful flowers is absolutely wonderful; who painted it today?
He very much enjoys __________ with bright oil colors in his small art studio at home.

vocabularies.definition: The hobby or job of taking photographs.
vocabularies.example:
He is interested in nature photography.
Photography is a very expensive hobby.
She takes beautiful photography.
quizzes: __________ is an absolutely great and creative way to capture all your happy memories.
He is very interested in nature __________ and he loves taking photos of wild animals.
She is currently taking a __________ class at the local community center every Tuesday.

vocabularies.definition: A large musical instrument with black and white keys that you press.
vocabularies.example:
She has been playing the piano since she was five.
There is a large piano in the living room.
He is practicing a new song on the piano.
quizzes: She has been playing the __________ since she was only five years old today now.
There is an absolutely large and beautiful __________ in the middle of our living room.
He is currently practicing a brand new and difficult song on the __________ today.

vocabularies.definition: A game or toy that tests your thinking or skill, like a jigsaw puzzle.
vocabularies.example:
I love doing jigsaw puzzles.
This is a very difficult puzzle.
Can you help me solve this puzzle?
quizzes: I'm currently trying to solve this truly difficult jigsaw __________ with my little sister.
This difficult math __________ is really making me think hard today; can you help me now?
We fortunately finished the one thousand piece __________ after working on it for weeks.

vocabularies.definition: A short test to check what you know.
vocabularies.example:
We had a fun quiz at the party.
Did you know the answer to the quiz question?
I won first prize in the school quiz.
quizzes: We had an absolutely fun and exciting __________ at the birthday party last night.
Did you actually know the correct answer to that very difficult __________ question now?
I proudly won the first prize in the school's general knowledge __________ today now.

vocabularies.definition: To move on smooth ice or a flat surface using shoes with blades or wheels.
vocabularies.example:
I like to skate at the park.
Can you skate on ice?
He bought a new pair of skates.
quizzes: I really like to __________ at the local park with all my best friends every Saturday.
Can you actually __________ on real ice, or do you find it very difficult to balance?
He very proudly bought a brand new pair of shiny black __________ for the winter now.

vocabularies.definition: The raised area in a theatre where actors or singers perform.
vocabularies.example:
The actors are performing on the stage.
She was nervous about going on stage.
The stage was decorated with flowers.
quizzes: The talented actors are all standing on the __________ waiting for the play to begin now.
He was a bit nervous when he walked onto the __________ to give his important speech today.
The __________ was beautifully decorated with many colorful flowers for the school show.

vocabularies.definition: A group of people who play a sport or game together against another group.
vocabularies.example:
He plays for the local football team.
We have a great team this year.
Who is the captain of your team?
quizzes: He plays for the school's first football __________ and they are very good indeed today.
Our __________ proudly won the exciting match yesterday with a score of three to zero.
We really need to work together as a __________ if we want to win the school competition.

vocabularies.definition: The person or team that wins a game or competition.
vocabularies.example:
And the winner is... Sarah!
She is a natural winner.
There can only be one winner.
quizzes: The lucky __________ of the race proudly received a large gold trophy and a medal now.
Congratulations! You are the happy __________ of our exciting school art competition!
There can only be one lucky __________ in this game, so please try your very best now.

vocabularies.definition: The sport of swimming underwater using special breathing equipment.
vocabularies.example:
We went scuba diving on holiday.
Scuba diving is an exciting sport.
You need a lot of equipment for scuba diving.
quizzes: We all went __________ on our summer holiday and saw many truly amazing sights now.
__________ is an absolutely exciting and adventurous sport for everyone to try today.
You really need a lot of special equipment if you want to go __________ in the sea.

vocabularies.definition: The sport of swimming near the surface of the water using a tube to breathe.
vocabularies.example:
I saw many colorful fish while snorkeling.
Snorkeling is easier than scuba diving.
We went snorkeling in the coral reef.
quizzes: I saw many beautiful colorful fish while I was __________ in the clear blue sea.
__________ is actually much easier than scuba diving and it is very fun to do now.
We all went __________ in the beautiful coral reef during our tropical holiday now.

vocabularies.definition: The activity of staying in a tent for a holiday.
vocabularies.example:
We go camping every summer.
Do you like sleeping in a tent while camping?
Camping is a great way to enjoy nature.
quizzes: We try to go __________ every summer in the beautiful green and peaceful forest now.
Do you actually like sleeping in a small __________ while you are out camping today?
__________ is an absolutely great way to enjoy nature and see many wild animals now.

vocabularies.definition: The activity of going for long walks in the countryside or mountains.
vocabularies.example:
We went hiking in the mountains.
Wear comfortable shoes for hiking.
Hiking is good exercise.
quizzes: We all went __________ in the high mountains and saw some truly wonderful views today.
Please remember to wear very comfortable shoes if you are going __________ today now.
__________ is an absolutely great exercise for staying very healthy and strong today.

vocabularies.definition: The activity of running at a slow, regular speed for exercise.
vocabularies.example:
I go jogging every morning.
Jogging helps me feel healthy.
He is wearing his jogging suit.
quizzes: I try to go __________ every single morning before I go to school to stay healthy.
__________ truly helps me feel much more healthy and full of energy all day long now.
He is currently wearing his new blue __________ suit for his morning run in the park.

vocabularies.definition: A form of entertainment where people sing along to recorded music.
vocabularies.example:
We love singing karaoke together.
He is a very good karaoke singer.
Let's go to a karaoke bar tonight.
quizzes: We all truly love singing __________ together at our fun family parties every year.
He is actually a truly good __________ singer and everyone loves to hear him sing.
Let's go to a fun __________ bar tonight and sing all of our favorite songs today!

vocabularies.definition: Special powers or tricks that seem to do impossible things.
vocabularies.example:
The magician performed some amazing magic tricks.
Do you believe in magic?
It was a magical evening.
quizzes: The clever magician performed some truly amazing __________ tricks for all of us now.
Do you actually __________ in real magic, or do you think they are just tricks?
It was an absolutely __________ evening that I will truly never forget in my life.

vocabularies.definition: A small copy of something, like a car or a plane.
vocabularies.example:
He builds model airplanes.
She wants to be a professional model.
This is a model of the Taj Mahal.
quizzes: He very much enjoys building __________ airplanes and painting them very carefully now.
She truly wants to be a professional fashion __________ when she grows up one day.
This is an absolutely perfect __________ of the Taj Mahal that I bought on holiday.

vocabularies.definition: The Japanese art of folding paper to create beautiful shapes.
vocabularies.example:
She is very good at origami.
I made a paper crane using origami.
Origami is a traditional Japanese art.
quizzes: She is actually truly good at the art of __________ and can make many paper shapes.
I proudly made a beautiful paper crane using __________ during my art class today.
__________ is a truly beautiful and traditional Japanese art that I want to learn.

grammars.description: Dùng để nói về sở thích.
grammars.example:
I love playing chess.
He enjoys collecting stamps.

vocabularies.definition: A large hairy bee that makes a loud humming sound.
vocabularies.example:
A large bumblebee is flying near the flowers.
Bumblebees are important for pollination.
Don't touch the bumblebee; it might sting!
quizzes: A large hairy __________ is flying near the colorful flowers in the garden now.
__________ are extremely important for pollination of many different plants.
Please don't touch the fuzzy __________ ; it might unfortunately sting you now!

vocabularies.definition: An insect with four large, often brightly colored wings.
vocabularies.example:
A beautiful butterfly landed on my hand.
Butterflies have colorful wings.
Look at that bright yellow butterfly.
quizzes: A truly beautiful and colorful __________ landed softly on my hand today now.
__________ have very large and brightly colored wings that help them to fly.
Look at that absolutely bright yellow __________ flying over the green bushes.

vocabularies.definition: A living thing, such as an animal or an insect.
vocabularies.example:
The ocean is full of strange creatures.
Every living creature is important.
He is a kind creature.
quizzes: The deep ocean is actually full of many strange and mysterious __________ now.
Every single living __________ on our beautiful Earth is truly very important.
He is a truly kind and gentle __________ that loves to help everyone in need.

vocabularies.definition: A type of animal that lived millions of years ago but is now extinct.
vocabularies.example:
Dinosaurs lived millions of years ago.

quizzes: __________ lived many millions of years ago, but they are all now extinct today.
I proudly saw a giant __________ skeleton at the city museum with my family.
My very favorite __________ is the T-Rex because it was so large and strong.

vocabularies.definition: A large bird of prey with very good eyesight.
vocabularies.example:
An eagle is flying high in the sky.
Eagles have very sharp eyes.
The eagle is a symbol of strength.
quizzes: A large __________ is flying very high in the clear blue sky above the mountains.

The __________ is a truly powerful symbol of strength and freedom in many lands.

vocabularies.definition: The natural world around us, including the air, water, and land.
vocabularies.example:
We should protect the environment.
Pollution is bad for the environment.
Environment day is in June.
quizzes: We should all work together to protect the natural __________ for our future.
Pollution is unfortunately very bad for the __________ and the wild animals now.
World __________ Day is celebrated in June every year to help save our planet.

vocabularies.definition: No longer existing; having died out completely.
vocabularies.example:
Dinosaurs are now extinct.
Many animals are in danger of becoming extinct.
An extinct animal no longer exists.
quizzes: Dinosaurs are now __________ , which means they strictly no longer exist today.

An __________ animal is one that we can strictly never see alive in person today.

vocabularies.definition: The thick hair that covers the bodies of some animals.
vocabularies.example:
The cat has very soft fur.
Polar bears have thick white fur.
Don't pull the dog's fur.
quizzes: The little kitten has very soft __________ that feels like a fluffy white cloud.
Polar bears have truly thick white __________ to keep them warm in the cold snow.
Please don't pull the dog's __________ ; it might hurt him and make him very sad.

vocabularies.definition: A small animal with six legs and three body parts.
vocabularies.example:
An ant is a small insect.
Most insects have six legs.
There are many insects in the garden.
quizzes: An __________ has six legs and usually has wings; there are many kinds of them.
How many different kinds of __________ can you find in your garden this morning?
Ants and bees are very busy and interesting __________ that work together today.

vocabularies.definition: A sea creature with a soft body and eight long arms.
vocabularies.example:
An octopus has eight arms.
Octopuses live in the ocean.
I saw an octopus at the aquarium.
quizzes: An __________ has eight long arms and it can change its color to hide today.
__________ live in the deep ocean and they are truly very clever creatures now.
I proudly saw a small __________ at the city aquarium swimming with many fish.

vocabularies.definition: A very large round object in space that goes around a star.
vocabularies.example:

Mars is often called the red planet.
quizzes: The Earth is actually the __________ planet from the sun in our solar system.
There are eight different __________ in our solar system rotating around the sun.
Mars is often called the red __________ because of its truly rusty and red soil.

vocabularies.definition: Damage caused to the environment by harmful substances.
vocabularies.example:
Air pollution is a big problem in cities.
We should try to reduce water pollution.
Pollution kills many animals.
quizzes: Air __________ is unfortunately an absolutely big problem in many large cities today.
We should all truly try to reduce water __________ to help save the sea creatures.
__________ unfortunately kills many wild animals and ruins their natural homes.

vocabularies.definition: An animal that is cold-blooded and has scales, like a snake or lizard.
vocabularies.example:
A lizard is a type of reptile.
Reptiles are cold-blooded animals.
Snake is a common reptile.
quizzes: A green lizard is actually a type of __________ that has scaly skin and four legs.
__________ are cold-blooded animals and they usually lay eggs to have babies.
The snake is a truly common __________ that can be found in many parts of world.

vocabularies.definition: A large sea fish with very sharp teeth.
vocabularies.example:
Sharks have very sharp teeth.
I am afraid of sharks.
There are many different types of sharks.
quizzes: __________ have very large and extremely sharp teeth for catching many fish now.
I am honestly a bit afraid of __________ when I go swimming in the deep blue sea.
There are actually many different types of __________ , like the great white shark.

vocabularies.definition: A large white bird with a long neck that lives on rivers and lakes.
vocabularies.example:
A graceful swan is swimming on the lake.
Swans are usually white.
The young swan is called a cygnet.
quizzes: A graceful white __________ is swimming slowly on the peaceful lake this morning.
__________ are usually purely white and they have truly long and elegant necks.
The young __________ is called a cygnet and it is very fluffy and cute today now.

vocabularies.definition: An animal with a hard shell that moves very slowly on land.
vocabularies.example:

My pet tortoise loves eating lettuce.
quizzes: A brown __________ moves very slowly across the green grass in the park today.

vocabularies.definition: A very large sea animal that looks like a fish but breathes air.
vocabularies.example:
The blue whale is the largest animal on Earth.

I saw a whale during the boat trip.
quizzes: The blue __________ is proudly the very largest animal on our beautiful planet now.
__________ are actually mammals, not fish, because they breathe air with lungs.
I luckily saw a giant __________ jumping out of the water during our boat trip.

vocabularies.definition: Animals and plants that grow or live in the wild.
vocabularies.example:
We should protect African wildlife.
I love watching wildlife documentaries.
He is interested in wildlife photography.
quizzes: We should all work together to protect African __________ and their natural homes.
I truly love watching __________ documentaries about wild animals on the television.
He is very interested in __________ photography and taking photos of wild birds.

vocabularies.definition: A thick forest in a hot and tropical country.
vocabularies.example:
Many wild animals live in the jungle.
The jungle is very hot and humid.
It's easy to get lost in the jungle.
quizzes: Many wild animals, like tigers and monkeys, live in the deep green __________ now.
The __________ is actually a truly hot and humid place with many large green trees.
It's unfortunately very easy to get lost in the thick and dark __________ today now.

vocabularies.definition: A large area of land that is very hot and dry with very little water.
vocabularies.example:
It is very hot and dry in the desert.
Camels can live in the desert.
The desert is covered in sand.
quizzes: A __________ is a truly dry and sandy place where it strictly rarely ever rains now.
Camels are very good at living in the hot __________ for many long days together.
It gets very cold at night in the __________ , even if it was very hot during day.

vocabularies.definition: A large area of land covered with trees.
vocabularies.example:
We went for a walk in the forest.
The forest is full of tall trees.

quizzes: The __________ is full of many tall green trees and many different kinds of birds.
We all went for a long and peaceful walk in the dark __________ yesterday morning.
Please try to be very quiet in the __________ so that you don't scare the animals.

vocabularies.definition: The very large area of salt water that covers most of the Earth.
vocabularies.example:
The ocean covers most of the Earth's surface.
The water in the ocean is salty.
I love listening to the sound of the ocean.
quizzes: The __________ covers most of the Earth's surface and it is full of salt water now.
The salt water in the __________ is home to many truly amazing sea creatures today.
I truly love listening to the peaceful sound of the __________ waves on the beach.

vocabularies.definition: A very high hill, often with rocks or snow on top.
vocabularies.example:
The top of the mountain is covered in snow.
We climbed the mountain yesterday.
The view from the mountain is amazing.
quizzes: The top of the high __________ is often covered in thick white snow even in summer.
We all proudly climbed to the top of the __________ yesterday and saw a great view.
The view from the top of the __________ is absolutely amazing; you can see for miles.

vocabularies.definition: A natural wide flow of water that goes across the land into the sea.
vocabularies.example:
The river flows into the sea.
We went fishing in the river.
The river is very deep here.
quizzes: The wide __________ flows slowly into the deep blue sea at the edge of the city.
We all went fishing in the quiet __________ yesterday, but we didn't catch any fish.
The __________ is actually very deep and wide here, so please be very careful now.

vocabularies.definition: A mountain with a hole at the top through which hot liquid rock can come out.
vocabularies.example:
The volcano erupted last night.

Volcanos are very dangerous.
quizzes: A giant and powerful __________ erupted last night and sent hot smoke into the sky.

grammars.description: Dùng để so sánh một vật với nhiều vật khác.
grammars.example:
The blue whale is the largest animal.
The cheetah is the fastest animal.

vocabularies.definition: A small area outside a window on an upper floor where you can stand.
vocabularies.example:
We have a small balcony with many flowers.
I like sitting on the balcony in the evening.
The view from the balcony is beautiful.
quizzes: We have a small and very colorful __________ with many flowers on its floor now.
I truly like sitting on the __________ in the quiet evening to watch the stars.
The view from our high __________ is absolutely beautiful; you can see the whole city.

vocabularies.definition: A room or set of rooms that are below the ground floor of a building.
vocabularies.example:

quizzes: We keep all our old toys and books in the __________ under our house today now.
The dark __________ is unfortunately a bit cold and scary to go to by myself.

vocabularies.definition: The flat surface that covers the top of a room.
vocabularies.example:

The ceiling is very high in this room.
quizzes: The __________ of my bedroom is painted white and it has some glow stars on it now.
There is an absolutely large and shiny lamp hanging from the __________ in the hall.

quizzes: White smoke is slowly coming out of the red __________ on the roof this morning now.
The old house has a truly tall brick __________ that goes all the way into sky.

vocabularies.definition: A door, gate, or passage used for entering a building or place.

vocabularies.definition: A part of a fence or wall that opens like a door.
vocabularies.example:

The gate is made of iron.
quizzes: Please remember to lock the wooden garden __________ before we go into the house now.
He is currently standing at the front __________ waiting for the school bus to arrive.
The heavy black __________ is made of iron and it is truly very strong and high.

vocabularies.definition: The area just inside the entrance of a house or building.

quizzes: Please leave your heavy coat in the __________ before you come into the living room.
The __________ is actually very long and narrow, with many doors on both sides now.

vocabularies.definition: The surface that covers the top of a building.

quizzes: There is a small black cat sitting on the __________ and watching the birds now.

vocabularies.definition: A flat board attached to a wall or in a cupboard for keeping things on.

quizzes: Please put the heavy book back on the wooden __________ after you finish reading it.

vocabularies.definition: One of a set of steps for going from one level of a building to another.

quizzes: He quickly ran up the __________ to his bedroom to fetch his new school bag now.

vocabularies.definition: A room at the top of a house under the roof.

quizzes: The green garden is surrounded by a very high and strong wooden __________ today now.

vocabularies.definition: A place where cars are repaired or kept.

The garage is full of tools.
quizzes: Please put the new car in the __________ before it starts to rain today now.

vocabularies.definition: An area of short grass in a garden or park.

quizzes: He is currently mowing the green __________ in front of our house to make it look tidy.
The happy kids are playing tag on the soft __________ in the garden this afternoon.
Please strictly keep off the __________ , because the grass is still very fresh and new.

vocabularies.definition: A track or way made for people to walk on.

The path leads to the river.
It's a narrow stone path.
quizzes: Please follow the narrow __________ through the garden to reach the back door now.
The long stone __________ leads directly to the quiet river at the end of the field.
It's an incredibly narrow stone __________ , so please walk carefully in a line today.

vocabularies.definition: A tall, strong post used to support a part of a building.
vocabularies.example:
The building has large white pillars.
The roof is supported by pillars.
He leaned against a pillar.
quizzes: The modern museum building has many large white __________ at its front entrance today.
The heavy roof is correctly supported by four very strong stone __________ in the hall.
He leaned against a tall stone __________ while he was waiting for his friend to arrive.

vocabularies.definition: A covered area at the entrance to a house.
vocabularies.example:
We sat on the porch and watched the rain.

Welcome to our home; come onto the porch.
quizzes: We all sat on the wooden __________ and watched the heavy rain falling down now.
The front __________ has a very fun swing where I like to sit and read my book.
Welcome to our beautiful home; please come onto the __________ and sit with us today.

vocabularies.definition: Flat pieces of baked clay or stone used for covering roofs or floors.

She is cleaning the kitchen tiles.
quizzes: The clean bathroom floor has many small and shiny blue __________ on it today now.

She is currently cleaning the kitchen __________ with a wet cloth to make them shine.

vocabularies.definition: Thick decorative paper used for covering the walls of a room.
vocabularies.example:
The wallpaper has a flower pattern.
They are putting up new wallpaper.
Do you like the color of the wallpaper?
quizzes: The new __________ on my bedroom wall has a truly beautiful flower pattern on it.
They are currently putting up some blue __________ in the living room this morning.
Do you truly like the bright yellow color of the __________ in the hallway now?

vocabularies.definition: A piece of cloth that hangs across a window or door to cover it.
vocabularies.example:

The curtains are made of thick silk.
She drew the curtains to let in the light.
quizzes: Please quickly close the __________ because it's getting dark outside the house now.
The expensive __________ are made of thick and very soft silk from a faraway land.
She drew the __________ to let the bright morning light into the cozy bedroom today.

vocabularies.definition: A soft bag filled with feathers or foam, used for sitting or leaning on.
vocabularies.example:
Put a cushion behind your back.
The sofa has many colorful cushions.
Sit on this soft cushion.
quizzes: Please put a soft blue __________ behind your back to be more comfortable now.
The large sofa in the living room has many truly colorful and soft __________ on it.
You can sit on this extremely soft __________ on the floor while we play our game.

vocabularies.definition: A warm bed cover filled with feathers or soft material.
vocabularies.example:
I love my warm duvet in winter.
Change the duvet cover, please.
The duvet is very soft.
quizzes: I truly love my warm and heavy __________ during the very cold winter nights now.
Please help me change the __________ cover so that my bed is fresh and clean today.
The new __________ is absolutely soft and it makes me feel very cozy in my bed.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of special glass that you can see yourself in.
vocabularies.example:
She looked at herself in the mirror.
The mirror is on the bedroom wall.
Don't break the mirror!
quizzes: She looked at herself in the large __________ while she was brushing her long hair.
The shiny __________ is currently on the bedroom wall next to the tall wardrobe now.
Please be very careful and don't break the __________ because it is made of glass.

vocabularies.definition: A small piece of carpet that covers part of a floor.
vocabularies.example:

The rug is made of wool.
Clean the rug with the vacuum cleaner.
quizzes: There is a small and very soft __________ next to my bed to keep my feet warm.
The colorful __________ in the living room is made of thick and very warm sheep wool.
Please help me clean the dusty __________ with the vacuum cleaner this morning now.

vocabularies.definition: A tall cupboard where you can hang your clothes.
vocabularies.example:
Hang your clothes in the wardrobe.
The wardrobe is full of dresses.
I need a bigger wardrobe.
quizzes: Please hang all your clean school clothes in the __________ after they are dry now.
The tall wooden __________ is currently full of many colorful and pretty dresses.
I truly need a much bigger __________ because I have many new clothes for school.

grammars.description: Dùng để mô tả vị trí các vật trong nhà.
grammars.example:
There is a cat on the roof.
There are many books on the shelf.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people can study after they leave high school.

He teaches at a local college.
The college has a large library.
quizzes: After high school, my older sister wants to go to __________ to study music.

Our city __________ has a very large and quiet library for all the students.

vocabularies.definition: An official document you get when you finish university.
vocabularies.example:
He has a degree in history.
She is studying for a science degree.
A university degree is very important.
quizzes: After four years of hard study at university, he finally got his __________ in history.
My mother is studying very hard every night for her new science __________.
Getting a university __________ is a very important goal for many young people today.

vocabularies.definition: A document showing that you have completed a course of study.
vocabularies.example:
She received her diploma at the ceremony.
He has a diploma in computer science.
Work hard to get your diploma.
quizzes: She was very proud to receive her high school __________ at the graduation ceremony.
The __________ on the wall shows that he has completed his computer science course.
You must work very hard in all your classes to get your __________ at the end.

vocabularies.definition: A high-level teacher at a university or college.
vocabularies.example:
The professor is giving a lecture.
She is a biology professor.
He wants to be a university professor.
quizzes: The __________ stood at the front of the large hall and gave a very interesting lecture.
My aunt is a biology __________ who knows everything about rare plants and animals.

vocabularies.definition: Money given to a student to help pay for their studies.
vocabularies.example:

He applied for a music scholarship.
Scholarships help students with their fees.
quizzes: Because she is such a good student, she won a __________ to study in England for free.
He is practicing his violin every day to try and win a music __________ to the academy.
__________ are very helpful because they give money to students who work very hard.

vocabularies.definition: One of the periods of time that the school year is divided into.

How many terms are there in a year?
quizzes: The first school __________ of the year usually ends just before the Christmas holidays.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of work that a student is asked to do.
vocabularies.example:

The teacher gave us a fun assignment.
Submit your assignment on time.
quizzes: I have a very difficult math __________ to finish before I can go out and play.

vocabularies.definition: The act of being present at a place, like school or a meeting.

quizzes: Good __________ at school is very important if you want to learn everything well.

vocabularies.definition: An official document that says something is true or has been achieved.
vocabularies.example:
She got a certificate for perfect attendance.
He showed his English certificate to the company.
The certificate is framed on the wall.
quizzes: She was very happy to get a __________ for having perfect attendance this year.
He proudly showed his new English __________ to his parents when he got home.
The beautiful gold and blue __________ for winning the race is framed on his bedroom wall.

vocabularies.definition: A series of lessons in a particular subject.
vocabularies.example:
I am taking a cooking course.
The course lasts for six months.
Is the course difficult?
quizzes: I am taking a fun photography __________ on Saturday mornings to learn how to take better photos.
The swimming __________ at the local pool lasts for six weeks during the summer.
Is the new computer __________ at your school difficult, or is it easy to learn?

vocabularies.definition: The subjects that are taught in a school or college.

What's in the history curriculum?
quizzes: Science and music are both important parts of our school __________ this year.
The school __________ is updated every few years to include new and interesting subjects.
What kind of interesting things are in the history __________ for your class right now?

vocabularies.definition: A department or group of related subjects in a university.
vocabularies.example:
He is a member of the science faculty.
The university has several faculties.
She works for the faculty of arts.
quizzes: He is a very famous member of the science __________ at the large city university.
The university has several different __________, including one for art and one for music.
She works as a secretary for the __________ of medicine, helping all the doctors.

vocabularies.definition: To complete a degree or a course of study.
vocabularies.example:
He will graduate from university next summer.
She is a high school graduate.
The graduation ceremony was very special.
quizzes: He will __________ from university next summer and starts his new job in September.
My sister is a high school __________ now, and she is very happy about finishing school.
We all wore special caps and long black gowns at the __________ ceremony last year.

vocabularies.definition: A talk given to a group of people to teach them about a subject.
vocabularies.example:
The professor gave an interesting lecture.
I am attending a lecture on history.
Take notes during the lecture.
quizzes: The professor gave an interesting __________ about the history of space travel today.
I am attending a long __________ on history at the city university this afternoon.
Please remember to take notes in your book during the __________ so you don't forget.

vocabularies.definition: A place where you can borrow books or study quietly.
vocabularies.example:
I like studying in the quiet library.
The library has thousands of books.
Please return the books to the library.
quizzes: I like studying in the quiet school __________ because there are so many books to read.
The city __________ has thousands of books that you can borrow and take home.
Please remember to return the books to the __________ before the end of the week.

vocabularies.definition: A book with empty pages for writing notes in.
vocabularies.example:
Write the lesson in your notebook.
I have a new blue notebook.
Don't lose your notebook!
quizzes: Please write the main points of the lesson in your new blue __________ now.
I have a new __________ with beautiful flowers on the cover for my science class.
Don't lose your __________, because it has all your important school notes inside it!

vocabularies.definition: A small bag or box for keeping pens and pencils in.
vocabularies.example:
Keep your pens and pencils in your pencase.
I have a colorful pencase.
Is this your pencase?
quizzes: You should keep your pens and pencils safely in your __________ so you don't lose them.
I have a colorful pink __________ that matches my new school bag and shoes.
Is this your blue __________? You left it on the table in the classroom today.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of work that involves careful study and planning.
vocabularies.example:
We are working on a group project.
The project is about the solar system.
When is the project due?
quizzes: We are working on a group __________ to build a model of a volcano this week.
The science __________ is about the planets in our solar system and the bright stars.
When is the history __________ due to be finished? I need to know the date.

vocabularies.definition: A short test to check what you know.
vocabularies.example:
We had a surprise math quiz today.
Did you pass the quiz?
I need to study for the science quiz.
quizzes: We had a surprise math __________ today to see if we understood the new lesson.
Did you pass the geography __________? It was quite easy for me to answer.
I need to study very hard for the science __________ that we have next Friday morning.

vocabularies.definition: The act of putting a name on an official list.
vocabularies.example:
Registration for the course starts next week.
Fill out the registration form.
Where is the registration desk?
quizzes: __________ for the new summer art course starts next Monday at the school office.
Please fill out the __________ form with your name and your home address clearly.
Where is the __________ desk located? I need to sign up for the football team.

vocabularies.definition: A detailed study of a subject to find out new information.
vocabularies.example:
He is doing research on ancient Egypt.
Research is important for the project.
Search for information during your research.
quizzes: He is doing a lot of __________ on ancient Egypt for his big history project.
Doing careful __________ is very important if you want to find out new information.
You should search for information on the internet during your __________ for school.

vocabularies.definition: One of the two periods of time that a school year is divided into.
vocabularies.example:
The first semester is almost over.
We have five subjects this semester.
I hope to get good grades this semester.
quizzes: The first __________ of school is almost over, and we will have a holiday soon.
We are studying five different subjects this __________, including art and music.
I hope to get very good grades this __________ because I have studied very hard.

vocabularies.definition: The people who work for an organization, like a school.
vocabularies.example:
The school staff are very helpful.
He is a member of the teaching staff.
Meet the staff in the office.
quizzes: The school __________ are very helpful and always help us find our classrooms.
He is a member of the teaching __________ and teaches science to the older students.
You can meet the school __________ in the main office if you have any questions.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is studying at a school or university.
vocabularies.example:
She is a very hardworking student.
There are thirty students in our class.
Welcome the new student.
quizzes: She is a very hardworking and helpful __________ who always does her homework.
There are thirty __________ in our class this year, and we are all good friends.
Let's all welcome the new __________ who joined our class this morning from London.

vocabularies.definition: A teacher who teaches a small group of students or one student.
vocabularies.example:
He has a private tutor for math.
She works as an English tutor.
The tutor helped him understand the lesson.
quizzes: He has a private __________ for math who comes to his house every Tuesday evening.
She works as an English __________ and helps many children learn how to speak better.
The tutor helped him understand the difficult science lesson very clearly today.

grammars.structure: Prepositions of place: At school, In class
grammars.description: Dùng để chỉ địa điểm trong giáo dục.
grammars.example:
He is at school now.
She is in class.

vocabularies.definition: Used to change the subject or to finish a conversation.
vocabularies.example:
It's raining, but I'm going out anyway.
Thanks for your help anyway.
Anyway, let's get back to work.
quizzes: It's raining heavily outside, but I am going for a walk __________ because I need exercise.
Thanks so much for your kind help __________, even if we didn't find the lost keys today.
__________, let's stop talking and get back to our important school work right now.

vocabularies.definition: In addition to what has already been mentioned; different.
vocabularies.example:
Would you like anything else?
Who else is coming to the party?
Find someone else to help you.
quizzes: Would you like anything __________ to eat, or are you full after that large delicious meal?
Who __________ is coming to the surprise birthday party tonight besides our best friends?
If you are too busy, I will try to find someone __________ to help me with my project now.

vocabularies.definition: As much as is needed or wanted.
vocabularies.example:
Is there enough room for me?
He is tall enough to reach the shelf.
I've had enough of this weather.
quizzes: Is there actually __________ room for me to sit in the car for our long journey today?
He is finally tall __________ to reach the high shelf and get his favorite book now.
I've truly had __________ of this cold and rainy weather; I wish it was sunny and warm.

vocabularies.definition: In place of something or someone else.
vocabularies.example:
I'll have tea instead of coffee.
Can we go tomorrow instead?
He stayed home instead of going to the park.
quizzes: I think I'll have a glass of orange juice __________ of milk for my breakfast today.
The weather is terrible today, so can we go to the city park tomorrow __________ now?
He stayed at home to study __________ of going to the park with his friends yesterday.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say 'yes' or to show that something is obvious.
vocabularies.example:
Of course you can come!
Can I borrow your pen? Of course.
Of course it's true.
quizzes: __________ you can come to my house today! We can play our favorite new video games.
Can I please borrow your blue pen for a second? __________, here you go, my friend.
__________ it's true that there is no school tomorrow! It's a national holiday today.

vocabularies.definition: One time only.
vocabularies.example:
I've been there once before.
I'll call you once I arrive.
Once upon a time, there was a king.
quizzes: I have only been to the large city of London __________ before on a school trip last year.
I'll call you on your mobile phone __________ I arrive at the train station this evening.
__________ upon a time, there was a very brave and kind king who lived in a large castle.

vocabularies.definition: Used when you are not sure if something is true; maybe.
vocabularies.example:
Perhaps it will rain today.
Perhaps he forgot about our meeting.
May I join you? Perhaps.
quizzes: __________ it will rain heavily today, so please don't forget to take your umbrella now.
__________ he simply forgot about our important meeting at the library this afternoon today.
May I please join you for a game of football in the park? __________, come and join us!

vocabularies.definition: Used to show that something is true to a certain level; fairly.
vocabularies.example:
The movie was quite good.
I'm quite tired tonight.
It's quite a long way to the city.
quizzes: The new adventure movie was actually __________ good and I really enjoyed watching it today.
I'm feeling __________ tired tonight, so I think I'll go to bed much earlier than usual.
It's actually __________ a long way to the city center, so we should take the fast bus now.

vocabularies.definition: Used to show a preference or to mean 'fairly/quite'.
vocabularies.example:
It's rather cold today.
I'd rather stay at home than go out.
That's a rather strange question.
quizzes: It's __________ cold outside today, so please wear your warm coat and a fuzzy scarf now.
I'd __________ stay at home and read my new book than go out to the noisy party today.
That's a __________ strange question to ask! I don't really know the answer to it yet.

vocabularies.definition: In a short period of time from now.
vocabularies.example:
I'll be home soon.
See you soon!
The bus is coming soon.
quizzes: I'll be home very __________ after I finish my football practice in the park today now.
Goodbye! See you __________ at the school gates tomorrow morning at eight o'clock now.
The yellow school bus is coming very __________ ; I can see it at the end of the road.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something is continuing to happen.
vocabularies.example:
Are you still here?
It is still raining.
He is still sleeping.
quizzes: Are you __________ here at school? I thought you went home half an hour ago today now.
It is __________ raining heavily outside, so we can't go out and play football today.
He is __________ sleeping in his warm bed; please be quiet so you don't wake him up.

vocabularies.definition: With each other; in one group.
vocabularies.example:
Let's work together.
They went to the cinema together.
We can solve this problem together.
