quizzes: Let's all work __________ on this science project so that we can finish it much faster.
They all went to the city cinema __________ to watch the new comedy movie yesterday now.
We can definitely solve this difficult problem if we work __________ as a great team today.

vocabularies.definition: In a good or successful way.
vocabularies.example:
He plays the piano very well.
I hope you are well.
Well, I should be going now.
quizzes: He plays the beautiful piano very __________ and everyone loves to hear his music now.
I truly hope you are __________ and happy in your brand new house in the large city today.
__________, I should really be going home now because it's getting quite late this evening.

vocabularies.definition: Until now; used in negative sentences and questions.
vocabularies.example:
I haven't seen the movie yet.
Is dinner ready yet?
No, not yet.
quizzes: I haven't seen that exciting new adventure movie __________ ; would you like to see it?
Is the delicious dinner ready __________ ? I am already feeling very hungry this evening.
Have you finished your long math homework __________ ? No, not __________, it's very hard.

vocabularies.definition: Before now or before a particular time.
vocabularies.example:
I've already finished my homework.
Has he left already?
It's already 10 o'clock.
quizzes: I've __________ finished all my difficult school homework for tomorrow; can I go out now?
Has he left for the railway station __________? I really wanted to say goodbye to him today.
Look at the clock! It's __________ ten o'clock at night; it's definitely time for bed now.

vocabularies.definition: In addition; too.
vocabularies.example:
She is a teacher, and also a writer.
I'm also coming to the party.
He also likes football.
quizzes: She is a very kind teacher, and she is __________ a truly famous writer of adventure books.
I'm __________ coming to the surprise birthday party tonight; it's going to be very fun!
He really likes playing the piano, but he __________ loves playing football in the park today.

vocabularies.definition: Used to show that something is surprising or unexpected.
vocabularies.example:
Even a child could understand this.
It's cold, even in the house.
He didn't even say goodbye.
quizzes: That puzzle is so easy that __________ a small child could understand how to solve it today.
It's extremely cold outside tonight, and it's cold __________ inside the house by the fire.
He was so angry with me that he didn't __________ say goodbye when he left the room now.

vocabularies.definition: A short time ago; only; exactly.
vocabularies.example:
I've just arrived home.
It's just a small problem.
He is just a boy.
quizzes: I've __________ arrived home from my long school trip, and I'm feeling quite tired now.
Please don't worry about it; it's __________ a small and easy problem that we can solve.
He is __________ a young boy, but he is already very brave and very clever for his age.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something is possible; perhaps.
vocabularies.example:
Maybe we can go tomorrow.
Maybe she is busy.
Just maybe it will work.
quizzes: __________ we can go to the city museum tomorrow if the weather is better than today now.
__________ she is truly busy with her school work right now, so please don't disturb her.
I truly hope that just __________ it will work, and we can finally finish our science project.

vocabularies.definition: Not at any time; not once.
vocabularies.example:
I have never been to London.
He never tells lies.
Never give up!
quizzes: I have __________ been to the city of London in my whole life, but I really want to go one day.
He is an extremely honest person, and he __________ tells lies to anyone at any time today.
Please __________ give up on your dreams! Work hard and you will surely achieve them today.

vocabularies.definition: Many times; frequently.
vocabularies.example:
I often go for a walk in the evening.
How often do you go to the gym?
She often visits her grandparents.
quizzes: I __________ go for a long walk in the quiet park in the evening when the sun is setting.
How __________ do you actually go to the sports gym to exercise and stay healthy today now?
She __________ visits her kind grandparents at their small house in the peaceful village today.

vocabularies.definition: No more than; just one.
vocabularies.example:
I have only one brother.
It's only five o'clock.
He is the only child.
quizzes: I have __________ one older brother, and his name is David; we play football together today.
Please don't worry, it's __________ five o'clock and we have plenty of time to get home now.
He is the __________ child in his family, so he doesn't have any brothers or sisters today.

vocabularies.definition: Occasionally; not all the time.
vocabularies.example:
I sometimes stay up late.
Sometimes I feel a bit sad.
We sometimes go to the beach.
quizzes: I __________ stay up a bit late to finish reading a truly exciting adventure book at night.
__________ I feel a bit sad for no reason, but I usually feel happy again very quickly.
We __________ go to the sandy beach during the summer holidays to swim in the blue ocean.

vocabularies.definition: Normally; what happens most of the time.
vocabularies.example:
I usually have breakfast at 7 am.
What do you usually do on Sundays?
She usually wears glasses.
quizzes: I __________ have a large breakfast at seven o'clock every single morning before school now.
What exactly do you __________ do on Sundays when you don't have any school work to do today?
She __________ wears her red glasses when she is reading a book or watching the television.

vocabularies.definition: Nearly; not quite.
vocabularies.example:
The movie is almost over.
I've almost finished my work.
He is almost as tall as me.
quizzes: The exciting adventure movie is __________ over now; let's wait to see the very end today.
I've __________ finished all my difficult math homework for tomorrow afternoon; just one more!
He is __________ as tall as me now, even though he is two years younger than I am today.

grammars.description: Vị trí của trạng từ chỉ tần suất trong câu.
grammars.example:
I usually wake up early.
He is never late.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is good at sports and takes part in competitions.
vocabularies.example:
The athletes are training for the Olympics.
She is a very talented young athlete.
He wants to be a professional athlete.
quizzes: The talented __________ are all training very hard every day for the next Olympic games.
She is an extremely talented young __________ who can run much faster than anyone else here.
He truly wants to be a professional __________ when he grows up and wins many gold medals.

vocabularies.definition: A competition to find out who is the best at a sport.
vocabularies.example:
They won the world championship last year.
The championship will be held in London.
Who is your favorite team in the championship?
quizzes: Our national football team proudly won the world __________ last year in a very exciting match.
The next school basketball __________ will be held in the large sports hall in London today.
Who exactly is your favorite team in the national tennis __________ this year? Tell me now.

vocabularies.definition: A person who trains a sports player or a team.
vocabularies.example:
The coach gave the players some advice.
She is an excellent swimming coach.
He was a football coach for twenty years.
quizzes: The kind __________ gave all the players some very good advice before the match started today.
She is an absolutely excellent swimming __________ who has taught many children how to swim.
He was a professional football __________ for twenty years before he decided to retire now.

vocabularies.definition: An event in which people try to win by being better than others.
vocabularies.example:
There is a spelling competition tomorrow.
She won first prize in the competition.
Competition is good for students.
quizzes: There is a very exciting spelling __________ tomorrow morning; I hope I can win first prize.
She proudly won the first prize in the school art __________ with her beautiful painting.
Healthy __________ is actually very good for students because it makes them work much harder.

vocabularies.definition: The set of tools or things needed for a particular activity.
vocabularies.example:
You need the right equipment for scuba diving.
The gym has new sports equipment.
Don't forget to pack your sports equipment.
quizzes: You really need all the right __________ if you want to go scuba diving safely in the sea.
The local sports __________ has much brand new and modern training equipment for everyone.
Please don't forget to pack all your sports __________ in your bag before we go to the club.

vocabularies.definition: A room or building with equipment for doing physical exercise.
vocabularies.example:
I go to the gym three times a week.
The school has a very large gym.
He is working out at the gym.
quizzes: I try to go to the local sports __________ three times every week to stay fit and strong now.
Our school has an absolutely large and modern __________ for all our indoor sports lessons.
He is currently working out at the __________ to make his muscles much stronger for football.

vocabularies.definition: A sports game where two people or teams compete against each other.
vocabularies.example:
We watched an exciting football match.
The match ended in a draw.
Are you going to the match tonight?
quizzes: We all watched an extremely exciting football __________ yesterday afternoon at the stadium.
The basketball __________ unfortunately ended in a draw, so both teams got one point each.
Are you actually going to the important tennis __________ tonight to watch your sister play?

vocabularies.definition: A small flat piece of metal given as a prize in a competition.
vocabularies.example:
He won a gold medal in the race.
She showed her medals to her parents.
The medal is made of solid gold.
quizzes: He proudly won a shiny gold __________ in the very fast swimming race yesterday morning now.
She happily showed all her new sports __________ to her proud parents when she arrived home.
The winner's __________ is actually made of solid gold and it is worth a lot of money today.

vocabularies.definition: A person who takes part in a game or sport.
vocabularies.example:
He is the best player on the team.
There are eleven players in a football team.
She is a professional tennis player.
quizzes: He is definitely the very best __________ on our school basketball team this year today now.
There are exactly eleven __________ in every professional football team on the field now.
She is a truly hardworking and famous professional tennis __________ who has won many games.

vocabularies.definition: A competition to see who is the fastest.
vocabularies.example:
I ran in the 100-meter race.
Who won the race?
The horses are ready for the race.
quizzes: I proudly ran in the very fast one hundred meter __________ at the school sports day today.
Who exactly __________ the exciting bike race yesterday afternoon? I want to know his name.
The strong and fast brown horses are all ready for the exciting __________ to start right now.

vocabularies.definition: The number of points a person or team gets in a game.
vocabularies.example:
What's the final score?
He scored a goal in the last minute.
Keep track of the score.
quizzes: What exactly was the final __________ of the football match yesterday? Did our team win it?
He proudly __________ a great goal in the very last minute of the exciting match today now.
Please help me keep a very careful track of the __________ in our basketball game right now.

vocabularies.definition: A large open area with seats around it for watching sports.
vocabularies.example:
The stadium was full of fans.
They are building a new stadium in the city.
The stadium has sixty thousand seats.
quizzes: The large city __________ was absolutely full of happy fans cheering for their team today.
They are currently building a brand new and modern __________ in the center of our city now.
The huge __________ has exactly sixty thousand comfortable seats for the sports fans today.

vocabularies.definition: A prize, like a large silver cup, given to the winner of a competition.
vocabularies.example:
The team proudly held the trophy.
He has many trophies on his shelf.
Winning the trophy was his dream.
quizzes: The winners' team proudly held the shiny silver __________ high in the air for all to see.
He has many beautiful gold and silver __________ on his bedroom shelf from all his races.
Winning the national football __________ was his biggest and most secret dream since childhood.

vocabularies.definition: A sport played with rackets and a shuttlecock over a high net.
vocabularies.example:
We play badminton in the garden.
Badminton is a fun and fast sport.
Do you have a badminton racket?
quizzes: We truly love to play __________ in the garden together when the weather is warm and sunny.
__________ is an absolutely fun and very fast sport that requires very good hand-eye skills.
Do you actually have your own __________ racket, or do you need to borrow one from me today?

vocabularies.definition: A sport where two teams try to throw a ball through a high net.
vocabularies.example:
He is very tall, so he is good at basketball.
They play basketball every afternoon.
I love watching basketball games.
quizzes: He is truly very tall for his age, so he is naturally very good at playing __________ now.
They all try to play __________ every single afternoon at the school's outdoor sports court.
I truly love watching professional __________ games on the television with my dad on Sundays.

vocabularies.definition: The activity of riding a bicycle.
vocabularies.example:
Cycling is a great way to stay fit.
We went cycling in the country.
He is a professional cycling champion.
quizzes: __________ is an absolutely great way to stay fit and see many beautiful sights outdoors.
We all went __________ in the quiet countryside last weekend and had an absolutely great time.
He is a truly famous professional __________ champion who has won many important bike races.

vocabularies.definition: The activity of catching fish for sport or for food.
vocabularies.example:
My grandfather loves going fishing.
We caught three fish on our fishing trip.
Fishing is very relaxing.
quizzes: My kind grandfather truly loves going __________ at the quiet lake near his small village.
We luckily caught three large silver fish on our __________ trip to the river yesterday now.
__________ is an absolutely relaxing and peaceful hobby for people who love nature and water.

vocabularies.definition: A game where two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's goal.
vocabularies.example:
Football is the most popular sport in the world.
He plays football for his school.
Let's go and play football!
quizzes: __________ is definitely the most popular and famous sport in the whole world today now.
He proudly plays __________ for his school team and he has already scored many great goals.
The weather is absolutely perfect today, so let's go and play __________ in the large park!

vocabularies.definition: A game played on grass where you hit a small ball into holes with a club.
vocabularies.example:
My uncle plays golf every weekend.
Golf is played on a large green course.
He is learning how to play golf.
quizzes: My rich uncle truly loves to play __________ every single weekend at his favorite country club.
__________ is usually played on an absolutely large and beautiful green course with many holes.
He is currently learning how to play __________ and he bought some new clubs for his lessons.

vocabularies.definition: A game played on ice or grass with sticks and a puck or ball.
vocabularies.example:
She plays hockey for the national team.
Hockey is a fast and exciting sport.
Wear a helmet when playing hockey.
quizzes: She is an extremely talented player who plays __________ for our national team this year.
__________ is an absolutely fast and exciting sport that can be played on ice or on grass.
Please remember to wear your protective __________ when you are playing hockey in the match.

vocabularies.definition: A game played with an oval ball that can be kicked or carried.
vocabularies.example:
Rugby is a very tough sport.
They are watching a rugby match on TV.
He plays rugby for his college.
quizzes: __________ is a truly tough and physical sport where players carry an oval ball to score now.
They are all sitting on the sofa watching a very exciting __________ match on the television.
He proudly plays __________ for his college team and he is very strong and fast on the field.

vocabularies.definition: The sport or activity of traveling in a boat with sails.
vocabularies.example:
We went sailing on the lake.
Sailing can be very peaceful.
Do you know how to go sailing?
quizzes: We all went __________ on the large blue lake yesterday and it was an absolutely great day.
__________ on the clear water when there is a soft breeze can be an absolutely peaceful hobby.
Do you actually know how to go __________ , or do you need someone to teach you how to do it?

vocabularies.definition: The sport of moving over snow on long, flat pieces of wood or plastic.
vocabularies.example:
We go skiing in the mountains every winter.
Skiing is a fun winter sport.
Be careful when you go skiing.
quizzes: We all go __________ in the high snowy mountains every single winter during our school holidays.
__________ is an absolutely fun and exciting winter sport for anyone who loves the white snow.
Please try to be very careful when you go __________ down the high hill so you don't fall.

vocabularies.definition: The sport or activity of moving through water using your body.
vocabularies.example:
Swimming is good for your whole body.
She goes swimming every morning.
I love swimming in the ocean.
quizzes: __________ is an absolutely great exercise that is very good for your whole body and health.
She goes __________ in the local indoor pool every single morning before she goes to school.
I truly love __________ in the warm blue ocean during our summer holidays at the beach now.

vocabularies.definition: A game where two or four people hit a ball over a net with rackets.
vocabularies.example:
He is playing a tennis match today.
Tennis is a very popular sport.
She has a new tennis racket.
quizzes: He is currently playing an important __________ match against his best friend at the club now.
__________ is an absolutely popular sport that can be played by two or four people together.
She very proudly bought a brand new and expensive __________ racket for her lessons today now.

grammars.description: Cách dùng các động từ đi kèm với các môn thể thao khác nhau.
grammars.example: Play football, Go swimming, Do gymnastics.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling fear or being scared of something.
vocabularies.example:
I am afraid of the dark.
Don't be afraid!
She is afraid of making mistakes.
quizzes: I am truly __________ of the dark, so I always keep a small light on in my bedroom.
Please don't be __________! The big dog is actually very friendly and loves to play.
She is sometimes __________ of making mistakes when she is speaking English in class.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling strong negative emotions because something bad happened.
vocabularies.example:
He was very angry with me.
Don't make your mother angry.
What are you angry about?
quizzes: He was very __________ with me because I accidentally broke his favorite toy today.
Please don't make your mother __________; you should help her clean the living room now.
What exactly are you so __________ about? Tell me what happened so I can help you.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling unhappy because you have nothing interesting to do.
vocabularies.example:
I'm bored; let's do something fun.
He looks bored with his book.
Are you bored yet?
quizzes: I'm feeling quite __________; let's go outside and do something fun like playing football.
He looks a bit __________ with his school book; maybe he should take a short break now.
Are you __________ yet, or do you want to keep watching this long and slow movie?

vocabularies.definition: Giving a lot of attention to what you are doing to avoid danger.
vocabularies.example:
Be careful when you cross the road.
She is a very careful driver.
Carry that glass carefully.
quizzes: Please be very __________ when you cross the busy road; always look both ways first now.
She is a very __________ driver who always follows the rules and never goes too fast.
Please carry that thin glass extremely __________ so that you don't drop it on the floor.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling very happy and enthusiastic about something.
vocabularies.example:
I'm so excited about the holiday!
The children are excited for Christmas.
He gave an excited shout.
quizzes: I'm so __________ about our summer holiday to the beach! I can't wait to go swimming.
The little children are all very __________ for Christmas and the presents they will get.
He gave an __________ shout when he finally scored the winning goal in the match today.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling afraid or scared.
vocabularies.example:
She was frightened by the big dog.
I was frightened of the loud thunder.
Don't be frightened; I'm here.
quizzes: She was suddenly __________ by the big black dog that barked loudly at her today now.
I was very __________ of the loud thunder during the storm yesterday night in my bed.
Please don't be __________; I'm here with you and everything will be absolutely fine now.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling pleased or glad about something.
vocabularies.example:
I'm so happy today!
She has a happy family.
Happy birthday!
quizzes: I'm so __________ today because it's a sunny day and I'm going to the park now.
She is very lucky because she has a very loving and __________ family who cares for her.
__________ birthday to you! I hope you have an absolutely wonderful day with your friends.

vocabularies.definition: Wanting to know more about something.
vocabularies.example:
He is interested in ancient history.
Are you interested in photography?
I'm very interested in what you said.
quizzes: He is very __________ in ancient history and he loves reading books about old kings.
Are you actually __________ in photography? I can teach you how to take great photos.
I'm truly __________ in what you just said; can you tell me more about your adventure?

vocabularies.definition: Being caring, helpful, and friendly to others.
vocabularies.example:
He is a very kind man.
Thank you for your kind help.
Be kind to animals.
quizzes: He is a very __________ and helpful man who always helps his neighbors with their work.
Thank you so much for your truly __________ help; I couldn't have finished this without you.
Please always be __________ to animals; they are our friends and we must care for them.

vocabularies.definition: Having good things happen by chance.
vocabularies.example:
I am lucky to have such good friends.
You are very lucky to win the prize.
It was a lucky day.
quizzes: I am truly __________ to have such good and kind friends in my school class this year.
You are very __________ to win the silver prize in the competition! You worked very hard.
It was an absolutely __________ day for me today! I found a gold coin on the street now.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling unhappy or sorry.
vocabularies.example:
I feel a bit sad today.
It's a very sad movie.
Why do you look so sad?
quizzes: I feel a bit __________ today because my best friend is moving to another city now.
It's a very __________ movie about a lost puppy; I cried at the end when it was lonely.
Why exactly do you look so __________ today? Did something bad happen at school today?

vocabularies.definition: Feeling wonder or shock because something unexpected happened.
vocabularies.example:
I was very surprised to see him.
She looked surprised at the news.
We were surprised by the party.
quizzes: I was extremely __________ to see him at the park; I thought he was still on holiday.
She looked very __________ at the news that she won the school art competition today.
We were all very __________ by the surprise birthday party our friends organized now.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling that you need to rest or sleep.
vocabularies.example:
I'm very tired after the long walk.
You look tired; you should go to bed.
I'm tired of waiting.
quizzes: I'm feeling very __________ after that long walk in the countryside yesterday afternoon.
You look quite __________ tonight; I think you should go to bed and rest for a while.
I'm truly __________ of waiting for the bus; it's already twenty minutes late today now.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling unhappy because you are thinking about problems.
vocabularies.example:
I'm worried about my exams.
Don't be worried; everything will be fine.
She had a worried look on her face.
quizzes: I'm feeling a bit __________ about my math exams next week; I hope I can do well.
Please don't be __________! Everything will be absolutely fine and we will find the way.
She had a very __________ look on her face when she lost her favorite red school bag.

vocabularies.definition: Being ready to face danger or pain without fear.
vocabularies.example:
The firefighter was very brave.
She was brave to tell the truth.
He is a brave boy.
quizzes: The firefighter was extremely __________ when he ran into the burning building today.
She was very __________ to tell the whole truth, even though she was a bit afraid now.
He is a very __________ boy who isn't afraid of the dark or anything else in the house.

vocabularies.definition: Being quick at learning and understanding things.
vocabularies.example:
She is a very clever student.
He is clever at fixing things.
That was a clever idea.
quizzes: She is a very __________ student who always understands the difficult lessons very fast.
He is truly __________ at fixing things like old bicycles and broken toys in his garage.
That was an absolutely __________ idea to use a magnet to find the lost keys on the floor.

vocabularies.definition: Being known by many people.
vocabularies.example:
He is a famous actor.
She wants to be a famous singer.
This city is famous for its bridge.
quizzes: He is an extremely __________ actor who has been in many popular adventure movies today.
She truly wants to be a __________ singer and travel all over the world one day now.
This large city is very __________ for its beautiful and very long bridge over the river.

vocabularies.definition: Being kind and pleasant to other people.
vocabularies.example:
The local people are very friendly.
He has a friendly face.
It was a friendly game.
quizzes: The local people in this small village are all very __________ and helpful to everyone.
He has a very __________ face and a kind smile that makes everyone feel happy today.
It was just a __________ game of football in the park, so it didn't matter who won it.

vocabularies.definition: Having a big effect or being worth a lot of attention.
vocabularies.example:
It is important to study hard.
This is an important meeting.
Health is more important than money.
quizzes: It is extremely __________ to study hard if you want to get good grades at school today.
This is an absolutely __________ meeting about the school trip, so please don't be late.
Your health is much more __________ than having a lot of money or expensive toys now.

vocabularies.definition: Not wanting to work or make an effort.
vocabularies.example:
Don't be lazy; help me clean the room!
He is a lazy cat.
I feel a bit lazy today.
quizzes: Please don't be __________! You should help me clean the messy living room right now.
He is a very __________ cat who likes to sleep in the warm sun all day long today.
I feel a bit __________ today, so I think I'll just stay at home and watch television.

vocabularies.definition: Making a lot of loud, unpleasant noise.
vocabularies.example:
The neighbors are being very noisy.
It's a noisy street.
The machine is noisy.
quizzes: The neighbors are being very __________ tonight; I can hear their loud music and talking.
It's an extremely __________ street with many cars and buses going past all day long.
This old washing machine is very __________; it makes a lot of loud sounds when it works.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling very pleased about something you have done.
vocabularies.example:
I am very proud of you.
He is proud of his country.
She was proud to win the prize.
quizzes: I am truly __________ of you for working so hard on your science project this week.
He is very __________ of his country and he always tells everyone about its history today.
She was absolutely __________ to win the silver prize in the school swimming race today.

vocabularies.definition: Having a lot of money or valuable things.
vocabularies.example:
The king was very rich.
She lives in a rich neighborhood.
He wants to be rich and famous.
quizzes: The king in the story was extremely __________ and had a lot of gold in his large castle.
She lives in an extremely __________ neighborhood where all the houses are very big and old.
He truly wants to be __________ and famous like a professional football player one day.

vocabularies.definition: Unusual, surprising, or difficult to understand.
vocabularies.example:
That's a strange noise.
I had a strange dream.
He is a bit strange.
quizzes: That's an extremely __________ noise coming from the kitchen; let's go and see what it is.
I had a very __________ dream last night about a flying bicycle and a talking purple cat.
He is sometimes a bit __________ , but he is actually a very kind and helpful person today.

vocabularies.definition: Very good, pleasant, or enjoyable.
vocabularies.example:
We had a wonderful holiday.
The view is wonderful.
She is a wonderful person.
quizzes: We all had an absolutely __________ holiday at the beach this summer; it was so much fun!
The view from the top of the high hill is absolutely __________; you can see the whole city.
She is a truly __________ person who is always kind and helpful to everyone she meets today.

grammars.description: Một số tính từ chỉ cảm xúc đi kèm giới từ.
grammars.example: Afraid of, Excited about, Interested in.

vocabularies.definition: A structure built over a river or road so that people can cross it.
vocabularies.example:
We drove across the long bridge.
The bridge connects the two cities.
There is a wooden bridge over the river.
quizzes: We all drove slowly across the very long stone __________ that goes over the wide blue river.
That large iron __________ connects the two busy cities and many cars cross it every day now.
There is a small wooden __________ over the narrow river where children like to go fishing today.

vocabularies.definition: A large strong building with high walls, built in the past for safety.
vocabularies.example:
The king lives in a beautiful castle.
I saw an old castle on the hill.
The castle has very thick walls.
quizzes: The powerful king lives in an absolutely beautiful __________ with many high towers and flags.
I saw an extremely old and historical __________ on the top of the hill with my school friends.
The ancient __________ has very thick stone walls and a deep water ditch all around it today.

vocabularies.definition: The place where two roads or walls meet.
vocabularies.example:
The shop is on the corner of the street.
Wait for me at the corner.
He is sitting in the corner of the room.
quizzes: The small toy shop is on the __________ of the street, right next to the tall city library.
Please wait for me at the __________ of the road; I'll be there in just two minutes, I promise.
He is sitting quietly in the __________ of the living room and reading his new science book now.

vocabularies.definition: The place where someone or something is going.
vocabularies.example:
We reached our destination at midnight.
What is your final destination?
The beach is a popular holiday destination.
quizzes: We finally reached our holiday __________ at midnight after a very long and tiring journey today.
What exactly is your final __________? Are you going to London or to a small village in the country?
The golden beach is a very popular summer holiday __________ for many families with children now.

vocabularies.definition: A building where large amounts of goods are made by machines.
vocabularies.example:
My dad works in a car factory.
The factory produces thousands of toys.
Don't go near the factory.
quizzes: My dad works in a very large car __________ where they make hundreds of new cars every single day.
That busy city __________ produces thousands of colorful plastic toys for children all over the world.
Please don't go near the old __________; it's quite dangerous because there are many heavy machines.

vocabularies.definition: A building where people can look at interesting and old things.
vocabularies.example:
We visited the natural history museum.
The museum is closed on Mondays.
There are many interesting things in the museum.
quizzes: We visited the natural history __________ yesterday and saw many huge dinosaur bones and old tools.
The city __________ is always closed on Mondays, so you should go there on a different day today.
There are many extremely interesting and ancient things in the __________ that you can look at now.

vocabularies.definition: A room or building where people work at desks.
vocabularies.example:
She works in a busy office.
Where is your office located?
I have some work to do at the office.
quizzes: She works in an extremely busy __________ in the city center with many computers and telephones.
Excuse me, could you tell me where exactly your __________ is located? I have a letter for you.
I still have some important work to do at the __________ before I can go home and have dinner now.

vocabularies.definition: The office of the local police force.
vocabularies.example:
The police station is near the bank.
He went to the police station to report the loss.
There are many police cars at the station.
quizzes: The old __________ is right near the bank, on the other side of the busy main street today.
He went to the __________ to report the loss of his expensive watch and his red leather wallet.
There are many blue __________ cars at the station right now because there was a meeting this morning.

vocabularies.definition: A place where you can buy stamps and send letters.
vocabularies.example:
I need to go to the post office to sent a letter.
Where is the nearest post office?
The post office opens at 9 am.
quizzes: I really need to go to the __________ to send an important letter to my grandparents in the country.
Could you tell me where the nearest __________ is? I need to buy some stamps for these cards today.
The city __________ always opens at exactly nine o'clock every morning from Monday to Saturday.

vocabularies.definition: A place where trains stop for people to get on or off.
vocabularies.example:
We are meeting at the railway station.
The railway station was very crowded.
Which platform does the train leave from?
quizzes: We are all meeting at the __________ at half past seven to catch the morning train to the beach.
The central __________ was very crowded this morning with many people traveling for work or school.
Which platform exactly does the fast train leave from? I don't want to miss my journey today now.

vocabularies.definition: A very tall building in a city.
vocabularies.example:
The city has many tall skyscrapers.
Look at that huge skyscraper!
You can see the whole city from the skyscraper.
quizzes: The modern city has many extremely tall __________ with hundreds of glass windows that shine today.
Look at that absolutely huge __________ over there! It must be the tallest building in the country.
You can see the whole beautiful city from the top of the __________ if you go up in the lift now.

vocabularies.definition: A building where you can go to watch a play or a show.
vocabularies.example:
We went to the theatre to see a play.
The theatre was beautifully decorated.
I love going to the theatre.
quizzes: We all went to the city __________ yesterday evening to see a very famous and funny play together.
The old __________ was beautifully decorated with red velvet curtains and gold lights for the show.
I truly love going to the __________ with my family to watch musical shows and clever actors today.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people go to study for a degree after high school.
vocabularies.example:
She is studying medicine at university.
He wants to go to university after school.
The university has a large campus.
quizzes: She is currently studying medicine at the __________; she wants to be a kind doctor one day now.
He really wants to go to a famous __________ after he finishes school to study science and history.
The old __________ has an extremely large campus with many trees and old stone buildings today.

vocabularies.definition: A small group of houses in the countryside, smaller than a town.
vocabularies.example:
He lives in a small village in the country.
The village is very quiet and peaceful.
I love visiting my grandparents' village.
quizzes: He lives in an extremely small __________ in the middle of the country, far away from any big city.
The tiny __________ is very quiet and peaceful; you can only hear the sounds of birds and the wind.
I truly love visiting my grandparents' __________ because it's so different from life in the city.

vocabularies.definition: A road, path, or route to a particular place.
vocabularies.example:
Which way is the library?
This is the best way to learn English.
I don't know the way to the park.
quizzes: Excuse me, could you tell me which __________ the library is? I need to return these school books.
I truly believe that this is the absolute best __________ to learn English words and their meanings.
I'm very sorry, but I actually don't know the __________ to the city park; maybe ask that officer.

vocabularies.definition: A shop where bread and cakes are made and sold.
vocabularies.example:
The bread in this bakery is delicious.
I bought some cakes at the bakery.
The bakery smells wonderful.
quizzes: The fresh bread in this small __________ is absolutely delicious; my mother buys it every morning.
I bought some chocolate cakes and strawberry pies at the __________ for my sister's birthday party.
The __________ smells absolutely wonderful when they are baking fresh cookies and buns in the oven.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people keep their money safely.
vocabularies.example:
I need to go to the bank to get some money.
The bank is located in the city center.
He works for a big international bank.
quizzes: I really need to go to the global __________ right now to get some paper money for our trip today.
The city __________ is conveniently located right in the center, next to the large post office now.
He works as a manager for a very big international __________ in a tall skyscraper in New York.

vocabularies.definition: A shop where you can buy medicine and beauty products.
vocabularies.example:
Go to the chemist to buy some medicine.
Is there a chemist near here?
The chemist recommended this cream.
quizzes: Please go to the __________ to buy some medicine for your headache and your sore throat today.
Excuse me, is there a __________ near here? I need to buy some digital thermometers for my house.
The helpful __________ recommended this special cream to help with the dry skin on my hands now.

vocabularies.definition: A large shop divided into several parts selling different things.
vocabularies.example:
We went shopping at the department store.
The department store has ten floors.
You can buy anything in a department store.
quizzes: We all went shopping at the huge __________ yesterday to buy some new clothes and a new computer.
The central __________ has ten floors and sells everything from food to expensive furniture.
You can buy almost anything you need in a modern __________, from toys to kitchen tools now.

vocabularies.definition: A building where fire engines are kept and firefighters work.
vocabularies.example:
The fire station is just down the street.
I saw a big red fire engine at the station.
Firefighters work at the fire station.
quizzes: The local __________ is just down the street, next to the small park in our neighborhood today.
I saw a very big and loud red fire engine at the __________ this morning while I was walking now.
Many brave firefighters work at the __________; they are always ready to help people in danger.

vocabularies.definition: A place where cars are repaired or kept.
vocabularies.example:
I took my car to the garage for repair.
He works as a mechanic in a garage.
The garage is very busy today.
quizzes: I took my old car to the local __________ yesterday for a repair because the engine was noisy.
He works as a very clever mechanic in a __________; he can fix any kind of car or motorcycle now.
The __________ is extremely busy today with many cars waiting to be fixed by the mechanics today.

vocabularies.definition: A building where you pay to stay in a room and have meals.
vocabularies.example:
We are staying at a luxury hotel.
The hotel has a swimming pool.
Book a room in the hotel.
quizzes: We are all staying at a very expensive luxury __________ near the beach for our summer holiday.
The modern __________ has a large swimming pool and a very good restaurant on the top floor now.
Please remember to book a comfortable room in the __________ before we leave for our trip today.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people meet to buy and sell food and other goods.
vocabularies.example:
I buy fresh vegetables at the market.
The market is very noisy and crowded.
Let's go to the market together.
quizzes: I always buy fresh vegetables and sweet fruit at the local __________ every Saturday morning.
The outdoor __________ is very noisy and crowded today with many people buying and selling things.
Let's go to the city __________ together and see if we can find some interesting old things now.

vocabularies.definition: A place where you can buy and eat a meal.
vocabularies.example:
We had a delicious meal at the restaurant.
The restaurant is famous for its pizza.
I'd like to book a table at the restaurant.
quizzes: We had an absolutely delicious meal at the Italian __________ yesterday for my sister's birthday.
The small __________ on the corner is very famous for its spicy pizza and its fresh pasta now.
I'd like to book a table for four people at the __________ for tomorrow evening at seven o'clock.

vocabularies.definition: A large open area with seats around it for watching sports.
vocabularies.example:
The stadium was full of exciting fans.
They are playing football in the stadium.
The stadium is very impressive.
quizzes: The large national __________ was full of exciting fans who were cheering for their team today.
They are currently playing an important game of football in the __________ right in the city center.
The new sports __________ is very impressive with its modern design and its thousands of seats now.

grammars.structure: Giving directions: Turn left, Go straight
grammars.description: Cách chỉ đường cơ bản.
grammars.example:
Go straight then turn left at the corner.
The bank is opposite the park.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle used for taking sick or injured people to hospital.
vocabularies.example:
The ambulance took the patient to the hospital.
Wait for the ambulance.
You can hear the siren of the ambulance.

vocabularies.definition: A time that you have arranged to see someone, like a doctor.
vocabularies.example:
I have a dentist appointment at 3 pm.
Don't be late for your appointment.
She made an appointment to see the doctor.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body from your neck down to your waist.
vocabularies.example:
My back hurts after sitting too long.
He carried a heavy bag on his back.
I have a pain in my back.

vocabularies.definition: A long piece of cloth used for tying around an injury.
vocabularies.example:
The nurse put a bandage on my arm.
You need to change the bandage every day.
Keep the bandage clean.

vocabularies.definition: The red liquid that flows through the bodies of people and animals.
vocabularies.example:
Blood is red and carries oxygen.
He lost a lot of blood in the accident.
I need a blood test.

vocabularies.definition: The part in the middle of your arm where it bends.
vocabularies.example:
I hit my elbow on the table.
She rested her elbows on the desk.
His elbow is sore.
quizzes: I accidentally hit my __________ on the hard wooden table, and it really hurts a lot now today.
She rested both of her __________ on the desk while she was reading her favorite adventure book.
His __________ is very sore because he fell down while playing a friendly game of tennis today.

vocabularies.definition: Physical activity that you do to stay healthy and strong.
vocabularies.example:
Exercise is good for your health.
I do exercise every morning.
What kind of exercise do you like?
quizzes: Regular __________ is extremely good for your health and helps you stay very fit and strong.
I always do some __________ every morning before I have my breakfast and go to school today.
What exactly kind of __________ do you like the most? I enjoy going swimming and cycling now.

vocabularies.definition: One of the five long parts at the end of each hand.
vocabularies.example:
I cut my finger while cooking.
She has a ring on her finger.
Don't point your finger at people.
quizzes: I accidentally cut my __________ while I was helping my mother cook dinner in the kitchen now.
She has a beautiful silver ring on her __________ that her grandmother gave to her last year.
Please don't point your __________ at people; it's not very polite, you should be kind instead.

vocabularies.definition: A pain in your head.
vocabularies.example:
I have a terrible headache.
Loud music gives me a headache.
Take an aspirin for your headache.
quizzes: I have an absolutely terrible __________ today; I think I need to lie down in a quiet room now.
That extremely loud music always gives me a __________; can you please turn it down a little?
You should take a small pill for your __________ if it doesn't get better in an hour today now.

vocabularies.definition: The part in the middle of your leg where it bends.
vocabularies.example:
He fell and hurt his knee.
I have a pain in my knee.
She bent her knees.
quizzes: He fell down on the playground and hurt his __________ while he was running very fast today.
I have a quite bad pain in my left __________ whenever I try to go upstairs in my house now.
She bent her __________ and sat down on the soft grass to watch the colorful birds in the park.

vocabularies.definition: A substance used for treating an illness or injury.
vocabularies.example:
The doctor gave me some medicine.
Take your medicine three times a day.
Medicine helps you feel better.
quizzes: The kind doctor gave me some __________ to help with my bad cough and sore throat today now.
Please remember to take your __________ three times a day after you finish your meals every day.
This special __________ will surely help you feel much better very soon, so don't worry now.

vocabularies.definition: The part of the body that joins the head to the shoulders.
vocabularies.example:
A giraffe has a very long neck.
I have a stiff neck.
She wore a necklace around her neck.
quizzes: A giraffe has an extremely long __________ so it can reach the green leaves at the top of trees.
I have a very stiff __________ today because I slept in a bad position yesterday night in bed.
She wore a beautiful gold necklace around her __________ for the special birthday party today.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body where your arm joins your neck.
vocabularies.example:
He put his hand on my shoulder.
I have a pain in my shoulder.
Carry the bag on your shoulder.
quizzes: He put his hand kindly on my __________ and told me not to worry about the difficult exam now.
I have a sharp pain in my right __________ after carrying that heavy suitcase all day long today.
You should carry that heavy school bag on your __________ instead of holding it in your hand.

vocabularies.definition: A pain in your stomach.
vocabularies.example:
Eating too much candy gives me a stomach ache.
I have a bad stomach ache.
She felt unwell with a stomach ache.
quizzes: Eating way too much sweet candy usually gives me a bad __________ later in the evening now.
I have a quite bad __________ today; I think I shouldn't have eaten that very spicy food yesterday.
She felt quite unwell and stayed in bed with a painful __________ after the party last night.

vocabularies.definition: The front part of your neck that food and air go down.
vocabularies.example:
I have a sore throat.
It's hard to swallow with a sore throat.
Drink some honey for your throat.
quizzes: I have a very sore __________ today, and it hurts a lot whenever I try to speak or sing now.
It's extremely hard to swallow even small pieces of food when you have a painful sore __________.
You should drink some warm tea with honey for your __________; it will help you feel better soon.

vocabularies.definition: One of the five parts at the end of each foot.
vocabularies.example:
He stubbed his toe on the door.
A person has ten toes.
My toes are cold.
quizzes: He accidentally stubbed his big __________ on the heavy wooden door while he was walking today.
A person normally has ten __________ on their feet, just like they have ten fingers on their hands.
My __________ are feeling very cold today; I think I need to wear some warm wool socks now.

vocabularies.definition: A pain in one of your teeth.
vocabularies.example:
I have a terrible toothache.
You should see a dentist for your toothache.
Toothache can be very painful.
quizzes: I have an absolutely terrible __________ today; I cannot eat anything hard or cold right now.
You should really go and see a professional dentist for your __________ as soon as possible today.
A bad __________ can be extremely painful and make it very difficult to sleep at night now.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling sick or not healthy; ill.
vocabularies.example:
I feel unwell today.
He looks unwell; he should go home.
She has been feeling unwell recently.
quizzes: I feel quite __________ today, so I think I'll stay at home and rest instead of going to school.
He looks a bit __________; I think he should go home and sleep for a few hours today now.
She has been feeling quite __________ recently; maybe she should go and see a doctor very soon.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body between your hand and your arm.
vocabularies.example:
He wore a watch on his wrist.
I sprained my wrist while playing tennis.
Her wrist is very thin.
quizzes: He wore a beautiful silver watch on his left __________ so he could always tell the time today.
I unfortunately sprained my __________ while I was playing a fast game of tennis yesterday now.
Her __________ is very thin, so she needs a small bracelet that won't fall off her hand today.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people who are ill or injured are treated by doctors.
vocabularies.example:
He is in the hospital for a surgery.
I work at a local hospital.
Where is the nearest hospital?
quizzes: He is currently in the city __________ for a surgery; we hope he will be much better soon now.
I work at a large local __________ near the park; I help the doctors care for the sick people.
Excuse me, could you tell me where the nearest __________ is? There has been a small accident.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to care for sick or injured people.
vocabularies.example:
The nurse is taking my temperature.
She wants to be a nurse.
The nurse was very kind.
quizzes: The kind __________ is taking my temperature right now to see if I have a high fever today.
She truly wants to be a __________ when she grows up so she can help sick people feel better soon.
The __________ was extremely kind and helpful to all the patients in the hospital ward today.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is receiving medical treatment.
vocabularies.example:
The doctor is visiting the patient.
He is a very patient man.
The patients are waiting in the lobby.
quizzes: The kind doctor is visiting the __________ in their room to see how they are feeling today now.
He is a very __________ man who doesn't mind waiting for a long time without getting angry.
The __________ are all waiting quietly in the hospital lobby to see the doctors this morning now.

vocabularies.definition: A small solid piece of medicine that you swallow.
vocabularies.example:
Swallow the pill with some water.
I have to take two pills every day.
The pill is small and white.
quizzes: Please swallow the small __________ with some clean water; don't try to chew it in your mouth.
I have to take two small __________ every day after my breakfast to help me get better fast now.
The __________ is very small and white, so it is quite easy to swallow with a little bit of juice.

vocabularies.definition: An instrument used for measuring how hot or cold your body is.
vocabularies.example:
The nurse used a thermometer to check my fever.
What does the thermometer say?
We need a new thermometer.
quizzes: The nurse used a digital __________ to check my fever and see how hot my body is today now.
What exactly does the __________ say? I hope my temperature isn't too high this morning today.
We really need to buy a new __________ for the school clinic; the old one doesn't work well now.

grammars.description: Dùng để nói về các vấn đề sức khỏe.
grammars.example:
I have a headache.
He has a stomach ache.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of jewelry that you wear around your wrist.
vocabularies.example:
She is wearing a beautiful gold bracelet.
I bought this bracelet on holiday.
That's a lovely silver bracelet.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of clothing with sleeves, worn over other clothes for warmth.
vocabularies.example:
Put on your coat; it's cold outside.
He has a warm winter coat.
I love your long red coat.

vocabularies.definition: A set of clothes worn by someone to look like someone else.
vocabularies.example:
She wore a Spiderman costume to the party.
He has a scary Halloween costume.
The actors are in traditional costumes.

vocabularies.definition: Pieces of clothing that you wear on your hands to keep them warm.
vocabularies.example:
Wear gloves to keep your hands warm.
I lost one of my gloves.
She has a pair of white silk gloves.

vocabularies.definition: A small bag used by women for carrying personal things like keys.
vocabularies.example:
She keeps her keys in her handbag.
That's a very expensive leather handbag.
I need a new handbag.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of clothing that you wear on your head.
vocabularies.example:
He is wearing a funny hat.
Take your hat off in the house.
I like your straw hat.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of jewelry that you wear around your neck.
vocabularies.example:
She wore a diamond necklace to the wedding.
That's a beautiful pearl necklace.
I bought this necklace for my mom.

vocabularies.definition: A small bag-like part of a piece of clothing for keeping things in.
vocabularies.example:
He put the money in his pocket.
There is a hole in my pocket.
Keep your hands in your pockets.

vocabularies.definition: A small round piece of jewelry that you wear on your finger.
vocabularies.example:
She is wearing a gold ring.
He gave her a beautiful ring.
That's a very large diamond ring.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of cloth that you wear around your neck to keep warm.
vocabularies.example:
Wear a scarf to protect your neck from the wind.
She has a colorful wool scarf.
I'm knitting a scarf.

vocabularies.definition: Short trousers that end above your knees.
vocabularies.example:
I wear shorts in the summer.
He is wearing blue swimming shorts.
These shorts are too small.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of clothing for women that hangs from the waist.
vocabularies.example:
She is wearing a long flowery skirt.
I like your new skirt.
The skirt is made of denim.

vocabularies.definition: Pieces of clothing that you wear on your feet inside your shoes.
vocabularies.example:
I need a pair of clean socks.
His socks have holes in them.
Wear warm socks in winter.

vocabularies.definition: A large bag with a handle used for carrying clothes when traveling.
vocabularies.example:
I am packing my suitcase for the trip.
That's a very heavy suitcase.
Help me carry this suitcase.

vocabularies.definition: Dark glasses that you wear to protect your eyes from the sun.
vocabularies.example:
Wear sunglasses to protect your eyes from the sun.
I love your new sunglasses.
Don't lose your sunglasses.

vocabularies.definition: A warm piece of clothing with sleeves, made of wool or cotton.
vocabularies.example:
I'm wearing a warm wool sweater.
That's a beautiful blue sweater.
It's too hot for a sweater.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of clothing that covers a woman's legs and feet closely.
vocabularies.example:
She is wearing black tights under her skirt.
The tights are very thin.
I need a new pair of tights.

vocabularies.definition: Soft shoes used for sports or casual wear.
vocabularies.example:
I wear trainers when I go running.
Those are very cool trainers.
He bought a pair of expensive trainers.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of clothing with two legs that covers you from the waist.
vocabularies.example:
He is wearing grey trousers.
These trousers are too long.
I need to buy some new trousers.

vocabularies.definition: An object used for protecting you from the rain or sun.
vocabularies.example:
Don't forget your umbrella; it's raining.
I have a big blue umbrella.
Can I share your umbrella?

vocabularies.definition: A special set of clothes worn by people in the same organization.
vocabularies.example:
We have to wear a school uniform.
The police officer is in uniform.
I like the color of our uniform.

vocabularies.definition: A small flat case for carrying paper money and cards.
vocabularies.example:
He lost his wallet on the bus.
There is no money in my wallet.
Keep your wallet safe.

vocabularies.definition: A small clock that you wear on your wrist.
vocabularies.example:
What time is it by your watch?
He has a very expensive gold watch.
My watch has stopped.

vocabularies.definition: Strong shoes that cover your feet and part of your legs.
vocabularies.example:
Wear boots when it's raining.
She has a pair of stylish leather boots.
These boots are for hiking.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of jewelry that you wear on your ears.
vocabularies.example:
She is wearing beautiful silver earrings.
I love your new earrings.
He gave her a pair of diamond earrings.

grammars.description: Dùng để nói về trang phục hiện tại.
grammars.example:
She is wearing a red skirt.
He is wearing a school uniform.

vocabularies.definition: One of the seven main areas of land on the Earth, such as Africa or Asia.
vocabularies.example:
Asia is the largest continent.
How many continents are there?
Africa is a beautiful continent.
quizzes: Asia is the largest __________ in the world and has many different countries and cultures today.
How many __________ are there on Earth? I think there are seven, but let me check my book now.
Africa is a truly beautiful __________ with many wild animals and very high mountains to explore.

vocabularies.definition: An area of land that has its own government and rules.
vocabularies.example:
Which country are you from?
Vietnam is a beautiful country.
He travels to many countries.
quizzes: Which __________ exactly are you from? I am from Vietnam, which is in Southeast Asia today now.
Vietnam is an absolutely beautiful __________ with many green hills and long sandy beaches now.
He travels to many different __________ all over the world because he loves learning new languages.

vocabularies.definition: The planet that we live on; also the ground or soil.
vocabularies.example:
The earth goes around the sun.
We should take care of the earth.
The earth is round.
quizzes: The __________ goes around the sun once every year, and the moon goes around the planet too now.
We should all take very good care of the __________ so that it stays a beautiful place for everyone.
The __________ is actually round like a giant ball, even though it looks flat when we stand on it.

vocabularies.definition: An area of land that is completely surrounded by water.
vocabularies.example:
They live on a small island.
We went to an island for holiday.
The island is surrounded by water.
quizzes: They live on an extremely small __________ in the middle of the ocean, far from any large land now.
We all went to a beautiful tropical __________ for our summer holiday and went swimming every day.
An __________ is a piece of land that is completely surrounded by water on all of its sides today.

vocabularies.definition: A large area of water that is surrounded by land.
vocabularies.example:
The lake is very calm today.
We went fishing on the lake.
There is a small boat on the lake.
quizzes: The deep __________ is very calm and blue today; it looks like a giant mirror in the sun now.
We went fishing on the __________ yesterday and caught three small silver fish for our dinner today.
There is a small wooden boat on the __________ that people can use to cross to the other side now.

vocabularies.definition: The very large area of salt water that covers most of the Earth's surface.
vocabularies.example:
The ocean is vast and deep.
I love the smell of the ocean.
Whales live in the ocean.
quizzes: The __________ is extremely vast and deep, and it covers most of our planet's surface today now.
I truly love the fresh smell of the __________ when I am walking on the beach in the morning.
Huge blue whales and many other interesting sea animals live in the deep __________ all their lives.

vocabularies.definition: A very large round object in space that moves around the sun or another star.
vocabularies.example:
Mars is known as the red planet.
There are eight planets in our solar system.
Protect our planet.
quizzes: Mars is often known as the red __________ because of the color of its rocky surface and dust.
There are eight __________ in our solar system that all move around the sun at different speeds.
We must work together to protect our beautiful __________ and keep the air and water clean now.

vocabularies.definition: A natural wide flow of water that goes across land into the sea or a lake.
vocabularies.example:
The river flows through the city.
We walked along the river.
The river is very wide here.
quizzes: The long blue __________ flows right through the middle of the city and under many stone bridges.
We walked slowly along the __________ yesterday evening and watched the small boats going past now.
The __________ is very wide here near the sea, so it's quite difficult to swim across it today.

vocabularies.definition: The area outside the Earth's atmosphere where the stars and planets are.
vocabularies.example:
Rockets travel into space.
There is no air in space.
He wants to explore space.
quizzes: Fast rockets travel into outer __________ to visit the moon and other planets in our solar system.
There is absolutely no air in __________ , so astronauts must wear special suits to breathe now.
He truly wants to explore __________ one day and be the first person to walk on a distant planet.

vocabularies.definition: A bright object in the night sky that looks like a small point of light.
vocabularies.example:
The stars are shining brightly.
Look at the stars tonight.
The sun is a star.
quizzes: The tiny white __________ are shining very brightly in the dark night sky above our house today.
Look at the beautiful __________ tonight! There are so many of them that I cannot count them all.
The sun is actually a very large and hot __________ that is much closer to us than the others.

vocabularies.definition: The space above the Earth that looks blue during the day and black at night.
vocabularies.example:
The sky is clear and blue.
I saw a plane in the sky.
The sun is high in the sky.
quizzes: The __________ is perfectly clear and bright blue today, without a single white cloud.
I saw a very large silver plane flying high in the __________ above the mountains today now.
The hot yellow sun is high in the __________ right now, and it's a very warm afternoon today.

vocabularies.definition: A very high hill, often with rocks or snow on top.
vocabularies.example:
They climbed the high mountain.
Mount Everest is the highest mountain.
The view from the mountain is great.
quizzes: They all climbed the very high and rocky __________ yesterday to see the view from the top.
Mount Everest is the highest __________ in the world, and it is covered in snow all year now.
The view from the top of the __________ is absolutely great; you can see for many miles today.

vocabularies.definition: An area of low land between hills or mountains.
vocabularies.example:
The village is located in the valley.
The valley is very green.
A river flows through the valley.
quizzes: The small peaceful village is located deep in the __________, between two very high hills now.
The __________ is very green and full of colorful flowers and tall trees during the spring today.
A wide blue river flows slowly through the middle of the __________, going towards the ocean.

vocabularies.definition: A stream of water that falls from a high place, like a cliff.
vocabularies.example:
The waterfall is very loud.
We saw a beautiful waterfall.
Waterfall is a natural wonder.
quizzes: The white __________ is very loud as the water falls from the high cliff into the pool below.
We saw an absolutely beautiful __________ in the forest yesterday; the water was very clear today.
A high __________ is a truly amazing natural wonder that people love to visit and take photos of.

vocabularies.definition: A large area of salt water that is part of an ocean or is surrounded by land.
vocabularies.example:
I love swimming in the sea.
The sea is very rough today.
He lives near the sea.
quizzes: I truly love swimming in the salt __________ when the weather is warm and the sun is shining.
The __________ is very rough and the waves are huge today, so we should stay on the beach now.
He is very lucky because he lives near the __________ and can hear the sound of the water today.

vocabularies.definition: The land along the edge of an ocean, sea, lake, or river.
vocabularies.example:
We walked along the shore.
The waves crashed against the shore.
Boats are kept at the shore.
quizzes: We walked slowly along the sandy __________ and looked for interesting shells in the morning.
The large waves crashed loudly against the rocky __________ during the storm yesterday night now.
Many small wooden boats are kept safely at the __________ when the fishermen are not working today.

vocabularies.definition: The solid surface of the Earth that we stand on.
vocabularies.example:
Don't sit on the cold ground.
He fell to the ground.
The ground is wet.
quizzes: Please don't sit on the cold and hard __________; you should find a chair or a bench instead.
He unfortunately fell to the __________ while he was running, but he didn't hurt himself badly.
The __________ is very wet and muddy after the heavy rain we had earlier this morning today now.

vocabularies.definition: An area of land on a farm used for growing crops or keeping animals.
vocabularies.example:
The cows are grazing in the field.
He is working in the field.
The field is full of flowers.
quizzes: The large brown cows are grazing peacefully in the green __________ near the farm today now.
He is working very hard in the __________ today, planting seeds for the next crop of vegetables.
The open __________ is full of beautiful colorful flowers and long green grass in the summer now.

vocabularies.definition: A track or way made for people to walk on.
vocabularies.example:
Follow the path.
The path is very narrow.
It's a beautiful forest path.
quizzes: Please follow the small stone __________ that leads from the house down to the edge of the lake.
The __________ is very narrow here, so we have to walk one behind the other very carefully today.
It's an absolutely beautiful __________ through the forest; you can see many tall trees and birds.

vocabularies.definition: An area of land that is higher than the land around it, but lower than a mountain.
vocabularies.example:
The house is on the hill.
We ran up the hill.
It's a small green hill.
quizzes: The small white house is on the top of the __________, with a great view of the valley below.
We all ran quickly up the steep __________ to see who could reach the old tree first today now.
It's a small green __________ with many sheep grazing and some small yellow flowers in the grass.

vocabularies.definition: A large natural hole in the side of a hill or cliff, or under the ground.
vocabularies.example:
The cave is dark and cold.
Bats live in the cave.
Explorers found an old cave.
quizzes: The deep stone __________ is very dark and cold inside, even on a warm and sunny day today.
Many black bats live in the __________ and they fly out to find food when the sun goes down now.
Some brave explorers found an extremely old __________ with drawings on the walls yesterday now.

vocabularies.definition: A large area of land where it is always very dry and there are few plants.
vocabularies.example:
The desert is very hot.
Camels live in the desert.
It is dry in the desert.
quizzes: The sandy __________ is extremely hot during the day and can be quite cold during the night today.
Large camels live in the dry __________ and they can walk for a long time without needing water.
It is always very dry in the __________ because it almost never rains there during the whole year.

vocabularies.definition: A thick forest in a hot and tropical part of the world.
vocabularies.example:
Wild animals live in the jungle.
The jungle is very thick.
He explored the jungle.
quizzes: Many different wild animals like tigers and monkeys live in the deep green __________ today now.
The tropical __________ is very thick and it's quite difficult to walk through the tall plants.
He carefully explored the __________ last month and found many rare birds and colorful flowers now.

vocabularies.definition: A large area of land covered with many trees.
vocabularies.example:
We walked in the forest.
The forest has many trees.
Don't get lost in the forest.
quizzes: We walked for a long time in the quiet __________ and listened to the sounds of the wind and birds.
The large __________ has thousands of very tall trees and it is home to many small wild animals.
Please stay on the path and don't get lost in the __________; it's very big and all looks the same.

grammars.structure: Prepositions of place: Near, Far from
grammars.description: Mô tả khoảng cách vị trí tự nhiên.
grammars.example:
The house is near the sea.
The city is far from the mountains.

vocabularies.definition: To start to be seen or to be present in a place.
vocabularies.example:
A ghost appeared in the mirror.
He appeared to be happy.
The sun appeared after the rain.
quizzes: A scary white ghost suddenly __________ in the large mirror when I turned off all the lights.
He truly __________ to be very happy today because he just won the school science prize.
The bright yellow sun finally __________ after the heavy rain stopped and the clouds went away.

vocabularies.definition: To go away suddenly so that you cannot be seen or found.
vocabularies.example:
The rabbit disappeared into the hole.
My keys have disappeared!
Wait until the clouds disappear.
quizzes: The small brown rabbit quickly __________ into the deep hole when it heard the loud noise.
I can't find them anywhere; my silver keys have completely __________ from the kitchen table!
We should wait until the dark clouds __________ before we go for a walk in the park today now.

vocabularies.definition: To find something for the first time or to learn something new.
vocabularies.example:
He discovered a new star.
We discovered a hidden path.
Scientists discovered a cure.
quizzes: The clever astronomer __________ a bright new star in the night sky using his powerful telescope.
We all __________ a hidden path behind the old waterfall while we were exploring the forest today.
The hardworking scientists finally __________ a powerful cure for the illness after years of study.

vocabularies.definition: To travel through an unknown area to learn about it.
vocabularies.example:
They explored the old castle.
I want to explore the world.
Explore the cave safely.
quizzes: They carefully __________ the old stone castle and found many secret rooms and ancient stairs.
I truly want to __________ the whole world one day and visit many different and far-off countries.
Please remember to __________ the deep sea cave very safely and always stay with your group now.

vocabularies.definition: To design or create something that has never been made before.
vocabularies.example:
Who invented the telephone?
He likes to invent things.
Thomas Edison invented the light bulb.
quizzes: Do you know exactly who __________ the telephone? It's used by everyone all over the world today.
He truly likes to __________ useful things in his garage using old plastic and metal parts now.
Thomas Edison is the famous man who __________ the first electric light bulb a long time ago.

vocabularies.definition: To be in control of an office, shop, or team of people.
vocabularies.example:
He manages a large hotel.
How did you manage to fix it?
She manages her time well.
quizzes: He successfully __________ a large luxury hotel near the beach and looks after all the guests.
How exactly did you __________ to fix the broken computer? I tried for hours but I couldn't do it.
She always __________ her time very well so she can finish her homework and still play sports.

vocabularies.definition: To plan or arrange something, such as an event or a meeting.
vocabularies.example:
We are organizing a party.
She likes to organize her desk.
Can you help me organize the event?
quizzes: We are all __________ a surprise birthday party for our favorite teacher next Friday afternoon.
She truly likes to __________ her desk every evening so that everything is in its correct place.
Excuse me, can you help me __________ the school sports event? There is a lot of work to do now.

vocabularies.definition: To get something ready for use or to get ready for something.
vocabularies.example:
I am preparing dinner.
Prepare for the exam carefully.
She is preparing for her trip.
quizzes: I am currently __________ an Italian dinner with fresh pasta and spicy tomato sauce for us today.
Please remember to __________ for the science exam very carefully and read all your notes tonight.
She is busy __________ for her long trip to the mountains by packing her boots and warm coat.

vocabularies.definition: To get or be given something.
vocabularies.example:
I received a letter today.
Did you receive my email?
He received a gold medal.
quizzes: I finally __________ a long handwritten letter from my grandparents in the country this morning.
Excuse me, did you actually __________ my email? I sent it to you at half past ten yesterday now.
He proudly __________ a shiny gold medal after he won the fast swimming race at the stadium today.

vocabularies.definition: To say or do something again.
vocabularies.example:
Could you repeat that, please?
Don't repeat the same mistake.
Repeat after me.
quizzes: I'm very sorry, but could you __________ that last sentence, please? I didn't hear you clearly.
Please try hard not to __________ the same mistake in your math homework today; be very careful.
Please __________ after me: 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog' to practice English.

vocabularies.definition: To find the answer to a problem or mystery.
vocabularies.example:
I solved the math problem.
Can you solve this puzzle?
We need to solve this issue.
quizzes: I finally __________ the difficult math problem after thinking about it for half an hour today.
Can you actually __________ this complicated wooden puzzle? It seems very difficult to me now.
We really need to __________ this important issue before the meeting starts tomorrow morning today.

vocabularies.definition: To go from one place to another, especially over a long distance.
vocabularies.example:
I love to travel to different countries.
He travels by train.
Travel around the world.
quizzes: I truly love to __________ to different countries and learn about their music and their food today.
He always __________ by fast train when he goes to the city center because it's very easy to do.
It would be an amazing adventure to __________ around the whole world for an entire year now.

vocabularies.definition: To go to see a person or a place for a short time.
vocabularies.example:
I am visiting my grandparents.
We visited the museum yesterday.
Would you like to visit Paris?
quizzes: I am currently __________ my grandparents in their small village in the middle of the country.
We all __________ the history museum yesterday and saw many interesting and ancient things now.
Would you truly like to __________ Paris one day and see the Eiffel Tower and the wide river?

vocabularies.definition: To stay in one place until something happens or someone arrives.
vocabularies.example:
Wait for me at the bus stop.
I've been waiting for an hour.
Wait a minute, please.
quizzes: Please __________ for me at the bus stop on the corner; I'll be there in just two minutes now.
I've been __________ for an hour for the train to arrive, and it is still not here yet today.
Please __________ a minute! I need to find my red umbrella and my gloves before we leave today.

vocabularies.definition: To want something to happen even though it is unlikely.
vocabularies.example:
I wish I could fly.
Make a wish!
I wish you a happy birthday.
quizzes: I truly __________ I could fly like a colorful bird and see the whole world from high above now.
Please close your eyes and make a special __________ before you blow out the candles on your cake.
I truly __________ you a very happy birthday and I hope you have a wonderful day with friends.

vocabularies.definition: To reach a place at the end of a journey.
vocabularies.example:
The train arrives at six.
When did you arrive?
He arrived late.
quizzes: The evening train usually __________ at exactly six o'clock at the central railway station today.
When exactly did you __________ at the hotel yesterday? Was it before or after the sun went down?
He unfortunately __________ late for the important school meeting because the bus was very slow.

vocabularies.definition: To think that something is true or that someone is telling the truth.
vocabularies.example:
I believe you.
Do you believe in magic?
I can't believe it.
quizzes: I truly __________ you; I know that you are telling the truth about what happened at school now.
Do you actually __________ in magic and flying dragons, or do you think they are only in books?
I simply can't __________ it! We won the first prize in the national sports competition today!

vocabularies.definition: To decide which one you want from a group of people or things.
vocabularies.example:
Choose a color.
I can't choose between these two.
Choose wisely.
quizzes: Please __________ a color for your new bedroom: do you want it to be light blue or soft green?
I simply can't __________ between these two pairs of shoes; they both look very comfortable today.
Please __________ very wisely which book you want to read first, as they are both very interesting.
