vocabularies.definition: To look at two or more things to see how they are similar or different.
vocabularies.example:
Compare these two pictures.
Don't compare yourself to others.
Compare the prices.
quizzes: Please __________ these two pictures very carefully and tell me if you can find five differences.
You should not __________ yourself to others, because everyone is special in their own way today.
Always __________ the prices in different shops before you decide to buy an expensive computer.

vocabularies.definition: To make a choice about something after thinking about it.
vocabularies.example:
I can't decide yet.
He decided to go home.
Have you decided?
quizzes: I'm sorry, but I simply can't __________ yet; I need more time to think about your suggestion.
He successfully __________ to go home after the long meeting and have a quiet dinner with family.
Have you finally __________ which subject you want to study at university next year today now?

vocabularies.definition: To make something clear or easy to understand by describing it.
vocabularies.example:
Explain the rules.
Can you explain why?
He explained it clearly.
quizzes: Please __________ the rules of the board game to us very carefully before we start playing today.
Can you actually __________ why you were late for the school meeting this morning? We were waiting.
He successfully __________ exactly how the new machine works, so now we can use it to make things.

vocabularies.definition: To form a picture or idea in your mind of something that is not real.
vocabularies.example:
Imagine living in space.
I can't imagine that.
Imagine you are a king.
quizzes: Just __________ living in outer space and floating around in a dark room with no gravity at all!
I simply can't __________ that! It seems so strange and impossible to happen in real life today.
Please __________ you are a powerful king living in a beautiful castle with many high towers now.

vocabularies.definition: To make something better or to become better.
vocabularies.example:
I want to improve my English.
His health is improving.
The weather is improving.
quizzes: I truly want to __________ my English words and my grammar by reading many interesting books.
His health is successfully __________ now because he is eating healthy food and sleeping enough.
The weather is finally __________; it's not raining anymore and the sun is starting to shine today.

vocabularies.definition: To speak or write about something in a few words.
vocabularies.example:
He didn't mention it.
Did I mention the party?
Don't mention it.
quizzes: He unfortunately didn't __________ the important news during the meeting, so nobody knew about it.
Excuse me, did I actually __________ the surprise party next week? I'm not sure if I told you yet.
Don't __________ it! I was very happy to help you with your heavy shopping bags this afternoon.

vocabularies.definition: To say that you admire someone or something highly.
vocabularies.example:
The teacher praised the students.
He deserves praise for his work.
Everyone praised the hero.
quizzes: The kind teacher __________ all the students because they worked very hard on their science project.
He truly deserves a lot of __________ for his excellent work on the new computer program today.
Everyone loudly __________ the brave hero after he saved the small cat from the tall tree now.

grammars.description: Dùng để nói về các hành động đã xảy ra.
grammars.example:
I visited Paris last year.
I have visited Paris three times.

vocabularies.definition: Always busy doing things, especially physical activities.
vocabularies.example:
He is a very active person.
Stay active to be healthy.
She is active in sports.
quizzes: He is an extremely __________ person who loves running and swimming every single day of the week.
You should try to stay __________ to be healthy and strong; don't just sit in front of the TV all day.
She is very __________ in many different sports at school, like basketball, football, and tennis.

vocabularies.definition: Good at thinking of new ideas or making new things.
vocabularies.example:
She has a creative mind.
He is very creative with art.
That's a creative solution.
quizzes: She has an extremely __________ mind and she's always coming up with wonderful new ideas today.
He is very __________ with art; he can turn old plastic bottles into beautiful and useful toys now.
That is a truly __________ solution to the problem! I never would have thought of that by myself.

vocabularies.definition: Having a strong desire to do something and not letting anything stop you.
vocabularies.example:
He is determined to win.
She is a determined young woman.
Be determined in your work.
quizzes: He is extremely __________ to win the swimming race today, so he is practicing very hard now.
She is a very __________ young woman who never gives up, even when things are very difficult.
Please try to be more __________ in your work; if you keep trying, you will surely succeed today.

vocabularies.definition: Having a lot of power and enthusiasm to do things.
vocabularies.example:
Children are always so energetic.
He gave an energetic performance.
I feel energetic today.
quizzes: All the small children are always so __________; they never seem to get tired of playing games.
He gave a truly __________ performance on the stage today, dancing and singing for almost an hour.
I feel surprisingly __________ today, so I think I'll go for a long walk in the park this afternoon.

vocabularies.definition: Willing to give money, help, or presents more than is usual.
vocabularies.example:
He is very generous with his money.
Thank you for your generous gift.
She has a generous heart.
quizzes: He is extremely __________ with his money and often buys beautiful presents for all his friends.
Thank you very much for your __________ gift! It's exactly what I wanted for my birthday today.
She has a truly __________ heart and she is always willing to share whatever she has with others.

vocabularies.definition: Telling the truth and not hiding things from others.
vocabularies.example:
He is an honest man.
Be honest with me.
Honesty is the best policy.
quizzes: He is an extremely __________ man who always tells the truth, even when it is difficult to do so.
Please be completely __________ with me: did you actually finish all your school homework today?
My mother always says that __________ is the best policy; you should never tell lies to anyone.

vocabularies.definition: Able to learn and understand things quickly and easily.
vocabularies.example:
She is an intelligent student.
That's an intelligent question.
He is highly intelligent.
quizzes: She is a very __________ student who always understands the science lessons very quickly now.
That is a truly __________ question! It shows that you have been thinking carefully about the topic.
He is highly __________ and can solve complicated math problems in just a few minutes today now.

vocabularies.definition: Always supporting your friends, family, or country.
vocabularies.example:
Dogs are very loyal animals.
He is a loyal friend.
She is loyal to her family.
quizzes: Dogs are known to be very __________ animals that always stay close to their masters and help them.
He is an extremely __________ friend who will always be there for you when you need some help now.
She is very __________ to her family and she always puts their needs before her own every day.

vocabularies.definition: Able to wait for a long time without getting angry.
vocabularies.example:
Be patient; wait for your turn.
He is very patient with children.
She is a patient teacher.
quizzes: Please try to be _________ and wait quietly for your turn to play with the new computer today.
He is extremely __________ with small children and never gets angry when they ask many questions.
She is a very __________ teacher who will explain things many times until everyone understands.

vocabularies.definition: Able to be trusted to do what is expected or promised.
vocabularies.example:
He is a reliable worker.
Is this information reliable?
She is my most reliable friend.
quizzes: He is a truly __________ worker who always arrives on time and does his job very carefully today.
Are you absolutely sure that this information is __________? We need to know the true facts now.
She is my most __________ friend; I know I can always depend on her to do what she promises.

vocabularies.definition: Caring only about yourself and not about other people.
vocabularies.example:
Don't be selfish; share your toys.
He is a bit selfish.
It was a selfish decision.
quizzes: Please don't be ___________; you should share your new toys with your younger brother today.
He is unfortunately a bit __________ and he doesn't like to give his things to other people now.
It was a truly __________ decision to eat the whole chocolate cake and not leave any for us today.

vocabularies.definition: Having good judgment and being practical.
vocabularies.example:
That's a sensible idea.
He is a sensible boy.
Wear sensible shoes for walking.
quizzes: That is an extremely __________ idea! It's the most practical way to solve the problem today.
He is a very __________ boy who always thinks carefully before he does something dangerous now.
Please remember to wear __________ shoes for our long walking trip in the mountains tomorrow.

vocabularies.definition: Not willing to change your mind or your ideas, even when you should.
vocabularies.example:
He is as stubborn as a mule.
Don't be so stubborn.
She is too stubborn to change her mind.
quizzes: He is as __________ as a mule and he simply refuses to listen to any of my suggestions today.
Please don't be so __________! You should try to understand that there are other ways to do it.
She is unfortunately too __________ to change her mind, even though she knows she is wrong now.

vocabularies.definition: Having a natural ability to do something very well.
vocabularies.example:
She is a talented musician.
He is a talented young artist.
You are very talented.
quizzes: She is an extremely __________ musician who can play the piano and the violin beautifully today.
He is a very __________ young artist whose colorful paintings are already famous in the city.
I truly think that you are very __________; you should keep practicing your singing every day now.

vocabularies.definition: Not being kind or helpful to other people.
vocabularies.example:
The neighbor is a bit unfriendly.
Don't be unfriendly to newcomers.
He gave an unfriendly look.
quizzes: The new neighbor is unfortunately a bit __________ and doesn't want to talk to anyone in the street.
Please don't be __________ to the newcomers at school; you should try to make them feel welcome.
He gave me a very __________ look when I asked him if I could borrow his science book today.

vocabularies.definition: Willing to try new and often dangerous things.
vocabularies.example:
He is an adventurous traveler.
Are you adventurous?
It was an adventurous trip.
quizzes: He is an extremely __________ traveler who loves exploring deep jungles and high mountains today.
Are you actually __________ enough to go camping in the forest during a stormy night now?
It was a truly __________ trip to the desert where we saw many amazing animals and vast sands.

vocabularies.definition: Having a strong desire to be successful or powerful.
vocabularies.example:
She is very ambitious about her career.
He is an ambitious student.
An ambitious plan.
quizzes: She is extremely __________ about her career and hopes to be the manager of the company one day.
He is an __________ student who wants to get the highest marks in all of his school exams today.
That is an extremely __________ plan to build a new bridge in just two months! It will be hard.

vocabularies.definition: Peaceful and quiet; not excited or upset.
vocabularies.example:
Please stay calm.
The sea is calm today.
He has a calm voice.
quizzes: Please try to stay __________ even if you are feeling a bit scared about the science test today.
The blue sea is surprisingly __________ today, so it's a perfect afternoon for a boat trip now.
He has an extremely __________ voice that always makes me feel peaceful when he is speaking now.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling sure about your own ability to do things well.
vocabularies.example:
He is confident about the test.
She is a confident speaker.
Be confident in yourself.
quizzes: He is feeling extremely __________ about the math test because he practiced for many hours today.
She is a very __________ speaker who can talk to a large group of people without being nervous.
You should truly be more __________ in yourself; I know that you can do a great job on the project.

vocabularies.definition: Willing to help other people.
vocabularies.example:
The staff are very helpful.
He is a helpful boy.
That's a helpful suggestion.
quizzes: All the staff at the hotel are very __________ and they will answer any questions you have today.
He is a truly __________ boy who always helps his mother with the shopping and the cleaning now.
That is a very __________ suggestion! It will definitely make our work much easier to finish today.

vocabularies.definition: Funny and making you laugh.
vocabularies.example:
He is a very humorous man.
She told a humorous story.
He has a humorous personality.
quizzes: He is a truly __________ man who always makes everyone laugh with his funny jokes and stories.
She told an extremely __________ story about her cat that made us all laugh for a long time today.
He has a very __________ personality and he always sees the funny side of every situation now.

vocabularies.definition: Not talking too much about your own abilities or success.
vocabularies.example:
He is very modest about his success.
She is a modest person.
A modest house.
quizzes: He is extremely __________ about his success and never talks about how intelligent he actually is.
She is a truly __________ person who doesn't like to be the center of attention during a party.
They live in a small and __________ house in a quiet street, far from the busy city center today.

vocabularies.definition: Friendly and energetic and liking to meet new people.
vocabularies.example:
She is an outgoing girl.
He is very outgoing and friendly.
Are you outgoing?
quizzes: She is an extremely __________ girl who loves meeting new people and making many new friends today.
He is very __________ and friendly, and he is always the first person to talk to everyone now.
Are you actually very __________ , or do you prefer to spend time alone reading your favorite books?

vocabularies.definition: Having good manners and being respectful to others.
vocabularies.example:
He is always polite to adults.
She has polite manners.
Be polite, please.
quizzes: He is always extremely __________ to adults and always remembers to say 'please' and 'thank you'.
She has very __________ manners and she always knows the correct way to behave in public today.
Please try to be __________ to the staff at the restaurant; they are working very hard for us now.

vocabularies.definition: Thinking carefully about things and not laughing very much.
vocabularies.example:
The situation is very serious.
He is a serious student.
Don't be so serious!
quizzes: The situation is becoming very __________ and we need to find a way to solve the problem now today.
He is a truly __________ student who spends all his free time studying for the important exams.
Please don't be so __________! It's a party and you should try to have some fun with your friends.

grammars.description: Cách sử dụng tính từ mô tả tính cách con người.
grammars.example:
He is very generous.
She is an intelligent student.

vocabularies.definition: Extremely surprising or very good.
vocabularies.example:
The view is absolutely amazing.
She is an amazing singer.
That's an amazing story.
quizzes: The view from the top of the skyscraper is absolutely __________ ; you can see the whole city!
She is an __________ singer with a very powerful voice that everyone loves to listen to today.
That is a truly __________ story! I can't believe that all those things actually happened to you.

vocabularies.definition: Very bad or unpleasant.
vocabularies.example:
The weather was awful yesterday.
I have an awful headache.
That's an awful thing to say.
quizzes: The weather was absolutely __________ yesterday with heavy rain and very strong cold winds now.
I have a truly __________ headache today and I think I need to lie down and rest for a while.
That is an __________ thing to say to your friend! You should apologize and be more kind today.

vocabularies.definition: Extremely large in size or amount.
vocabularies.example:
The elephant is an enormous animal.
They live in an enormous house.
An enormous amount of work.
quizzes: The grey elephant is an __________ animal that can weigh as much as six large cars today now.
They live in an absolutely __________ house with fifteen rooms and a very large swimming pool.
There is an __________ amount of work to finish before the school holidays start next Friday now.

vocabularies.definition: Extremely good or of very high quality.
vocabularies.example:
She is an excellent student.
The food was excellent.
That's an excellent idea.
quizzes: She is an __________ student who always gets the highest marks in all her science and math tests.
The Italian food we had at the restaurant was __________ ; I truly loved the spicy tomato pasta.
That is an __________ idea! I think it will help us to finish our group project much faster today.

vocabularies.definition: Very unpleasant or making you feel scared or sick.
vocabularies.example:
The coffee tastes horrible.
I had a horrible dream.
What a horrible noise!
quizzes: This black coffee tastes absolutely __________; it's much too bitter and I can't drink it at all.
I had a truly __________ dream last night about a huge green monster that was chasing me now.
What an absolutely __________ noise! It sounds like someone is breaking a thousand glass bottles!

vocabularies.definition: Very beautiful or very pleasant.
vocabularies.example:
She has a lovely smile.
What a lovely day!
Thank you for the lovely flowers.
quizzes: She has an absolutely __________ smile that makes everyone feel happy when they see her today.
What an absolutely __________ day! The sun is shining and the birds are singing in the garden.
Thank you very much for the __________ pink flowers; they smell wonderful in the living room now.

vocabularies.definition: Different from what is usual and often better or more important.
vocabularies.example:
Today is a very special day.
Do you have any special plans?
She is someone special.
quizzes: Today is a very __________ day because it's my birthday and all my friends are visiting me.
Do you have any __________ plans for the school holidays, or are you just staying at home now?
She is someone truly __________ to me; she always helps me when I am feeling sad or tired today.

vocabularies.definition: Helping you to do or achieve something.
vocabularies.example:
This tool is very useful.
Thank you for your useful advice.
Dictionaries are useful for learning.
quizzes: This small metal tool is very __________ for fixing broken toys and opening tight plastic boxes.
Thank you very much for your __________ advice; it helped me to decide which course to study now.
Electronic dictionaries are extremely __________ for learning new English words and their meanings.

vocabularies.definition: Worth a lot of money or very important.
vocabularies.example:
Health is very valuable.
That's a valuable ring.
He gave me some valuable information.
quizzes: Your health is extremely __________ , so you should eat good food and exercise every day now.
That's a very __________ gold ring with a shiny blue stone; please be very careful with it today.
He gave me some extremely __________ information about the missing money and the open window.

vocabularies.definition: Extremely small.
vocabularies.example:
A tiny insect landed on my arm.
They live in a tiny house.
The writing is tiny.
quizzes: An absolutely __________ green insect suddenly landed on my arm while I was in the garden.
They live in a very __________ house near the beach that only has one small room today.
The writing in this old yellow book is so __________ that I can't read it without glasses now.

vocabularies.definition: Extremely large; enormous.
vocabularies.example:
The whale is huge.
A huge building.
He made a huge mistake.
quizzes: The blue whale is a truly __________ animal that lives in the deep oceans of the world today.
They are building an absolutely __________ new glass building in the center of the city now.
He unfortunately made a __________ mistake in his bank work and lost a lot of important money.

vocabularies.definition: Measuring only a small distance from one side to the other.
vocabularies.example:
The bridge is very narrow.
A narrow street.
He has a narrow mind.
quizzes: The old wooden bridge is very __________ , so only one small car can cross it at a time today.
We walked down a very __________ stone street with many small shops on both sides of us now.
My grandfather says that you shouldn't have a __________ mind; you should always learn new things.

vocabularies.definition: Measuring a large distance from one side to the other.
vocabularies.example:
The river is very wide.
Open your mouth wide.
A wide range of products.
quizzes: The blue river is very __________ at this point, so we need a large boat to get across it now.
Please open your mouth very __________ for the dentist so he can check all your teeth today.
This large supermarket has a very __________ range of products from many different countries now.

vocabularies.definition: Going a long way down from the top or surface.
vocabularies.example:
The water is deep here.
A deep lake.
He has deep feelings.
quizzes: The blue water is extremely __________ here, so please be very careful when you go swimming.
They are fishing in a very __________ lake in the middle of the dark forest this afternoon now.
He has very __________ feelings for his family and he always wants to help them every single day.

vocabularies.definition: Not deep; having only a short distance from the top to the bottom.
vocabularies.example:
The river is shallow here.
A shallow bowl.
Don't be so shallow.
quizzes: The river is surprisingly __________ here, so we can easily walk across it to the other side now.
Please put the colorful fruit in a __________ bowl on the kitchen table for everyone today.
You shouldn't be so __________ ; try to think about the important things in life more often now.

vocabularies.definition: Not smooth; having an uneven surface.
vocabularies.example:
The road is very rough.
A rough surface.
The sea is rough today.
quizzes: The old mountain road is very __________ and full of large stones, so the car moved slowly now.
The grey stone wall has an extremely __________ surface that can hurt your hands if you touch it.
The blue sea is very __________ today because of the strong winds, so the boats are staying home.

vocabularies.definition: Having a flat, even surface without any lumps or holes.
vocabularies.example:
The skin feels smooth.
A smooth surface.
Everything went smooth.
quizzes: The baby's soft skin feels very __________ and warm against my hand in the morning today now.
This new wooden desk has a very __________ surface that is perfect for writing your homework.
I am very happy that everything went __________ during the school play and everyone loved it now.

vocabularies.definition: Measuring a large distance between two opposite surfaces.
vocabularies.example:
A thick book.
Thick walls.
He has thick hair.
quizzes: I am currently reading a very __________ book about the history of the world and ancient kings.
The old stone castle has extremely __________ walls that keep the rooms cool in the summer today.
He has very __________ black hair that he needs to cut every three weeks at the barbershop now.

vocabularies.definition: Measuring only a small distance between two opposite surfaces.
vocabularies.example:
A thin slice of cake.
He is very thin.
Thin paper.
quizzes: Would you like a __________ slice of the chocolate cake, or would you prefer a much larger piece?
He is very __________ because he runs for ten kilometers every single morning before breakfast now.
Please be very careful with this __________ paper; it is easy to tear if you are not gentle today.

vocabularies.definition: Moving or happening very quickly.
vocabularies.example:
A fast car.
He runs fast.
Fast food.
quizzes: The red racing car is extremely __________ and can travel at three hundred kilometers per hour now.
He always runs very __________ during the school sports competition and usually wins the gold medal.
I truly love eating __________ food like burgers and chips, but I know it isn't very healthy today.

vocabularies.definition: Moving or happening at a speed that is lower than usual.
vocabularies.example:
A slow train.
You are so slow!
Slow down, please.
quizzes: The old steam train is very __________ and it takes two hours to reach the next small station now.
You are so __________ ! We need to leave the house in five minutes or we will miss the bus today.
Please __________ down! You are walking much too quickly and I can't keep up with you anymore.

vocabularies.definition: Not pleasant to look at; not beautiful.
vocabularies.example:
An ugly building.
The ugly duckling.
That was an ugly look.
quizzes: They are planning to pull down that __________ old building and build a beautiful new park today.
Have you ever read the famous story about the __________ duckling that became a beautiful swan?
He gave me a very __________ look when I accidentally dropped his favorite red pen on the floor.

vocabularies.definition: Very pleasant to look at or to listen to.
vocabularies.example:
A beautiful girl.
Beautiful weather.
What a beautiful day!
quizzes: She is a truly __________ girl with long golden hair and bright blue eyes like the summer sky.
We are having absolutely __________ weather this week, so let's go for a picnic in the garden now.
What an absolutely __________ day! I'm so happy the sun is shining for our school sports day today.

vocabularies.definition: As good as it is possible to be; without any mistakes.
vocabularies.example:
A perfect day.
Nobody is perfect.
It's a perfect match.
quizzes: It was an absolutely __________ day for our trip to the beach: sunny, warm, and very peaceful today.
Please don't worry about making mistakes; my teacher always says that nobody is truly __________ now.
This blue tie and your white shirt are a __________ match! You look very smart for the party today.

vocabularies.definition: Happening or done most of the time; normal.
vocabularies.example:
The usual time.
As usual, he was late.
Is this the usual way?
quizzes: We will meet at the __________ time, which is exactly half past eight in front of the school today.
As ___________ , he was late for the football practice because he couldn't find his sports shoes.
Is this truly the __________ way that you make an apple cake? It smells absolutely wonderful now.

grammars.description: Cách hình thành trạng từ từ tính từ.
grammars.example: Beautifully, Carefully, Quickly.

vocabularies.definition: A period of one hundred years.
vocabularies.example:
We are in the twenty-first century.
A century is a hundred years.
This building is two centuries old.
quizzes: We are currently living in the twenty-first __________ , which is a time of many amazing inventions.
Did you know that a long __________ is exactly one hundred years? That is a very long time indeed.
This ancient stone building is more than two __________ old and it is still very strong today now.

vocabularies.definition: The time that will come after the present.
vocabularies.example:
What do you want to be in the future?
No one knows the future.
I'm thinking about my future.
quizzes: What do you truly want to be in the ___________? I want to be a famous scientist and travel to Mars!
My grandmother always says that no one truly knows the __________ , so you should enjoy today now.
I am currently thinking about my __________ and which interesting subjects I want to study at school.

vocabularies.definition: The time that has already happened.
vocabularies.example:
In the past, people traveled by horse.
Forget the past.
It is ten past five.
quizzes: In the distant __________ , people traveled by horse and carriage because there were no cars at all.
You should try to forget the __________ and focus on making your life better today and tomorrow now.
Excuse me, do you have the time? Yes, it is exactly ten __________ five on my new digital watch.

vocabularies.definition: The time that is happening now.
vocabularies.example:
Live in the present.
Thank you for the present!
He is present today.
quizzes: My teacher always says it is important to live in the __________ and enjoy the things we have now.
Thank you very much for the birthday __________ ! I truly love this new set of colorful pens today.
He is surprisingly __________ today; I thought he was still on holiday with his family in the city.

vocabularies.definition: A period of ten years.
vocabularies.example:
He has lived here for a decade.
A decade is ten years.
The project took a decade to finish.
quizzes: He has successfully lived in this beautiful city for more than a __________ now today.
Did you know that a long __________ is exactly ten years? That is quite a long time.
The building project took almost a whole __________ to finish due to the difficult weather now.

vocabularies.definition: A very short period of time.
vocabularies.example:
Wait a moment, please.
It was a special moment.
At that moment, the phone rang.
quizzes: Please wait a __________ ! I need to find my house keys and my wallet before we leave today.
It was a truly __________ moment when he won the first prize in the national sports competition.
At that exact __________ , the black phone rang loudly and woke up the small sleeping cat.

vocabularies.definition: A particular length of time.
vocabularies.example:
A difficult period in my life.
The first period is math.
A long period of time.
quizzes: That was a very difficult __________ in my life, but I learned a lot of important things then.
The first __________ on my school timetable today is math, and I have to be there by eight.
It took a very long __________ of time to build the ancient pyramids in the hot sandy desert.

vocabularies.definition: A plan that lists the times when things will happen.
vocabularies.example:
What's your school schedule?
I have a busy schedule.
Check the bus schedule.
quizzes: What is your school __________ for next Friday afternoon? Do you have art or science class?
I have a very busy __________ today with three meetings and a long train trip to the city center.
Please check the bus __________ on the corner to see when the next one is arriving today now.

vocabularies.definition: A line that shows the order in which past events happened.
vocabularies.example:
Create a timeline for the history project.
The timeline shows the main events.
Follow the project timeline.
quizzes: Please create a detailed __________ for your history project showing the dates of the major wars.
The colorful __________ shows all the main events that happened during the twentieth century now.
We should carefully follow the project __________ to ensure that we finish all the work on time.

vocabularies.definition: Twelve o'clock at night.
vocabularies.example:
The party ended at midnight.
I was asleep at midnight.
Midnight is 12 am.
quizzes: The surprise birthday party finally ended at __________ and everyone went home to sleep today.
I was already fast asleep at __________ , so I didn't hear the loud thunder and the heavy rain.
Did you know that __________ is exactly twelve o'clock at night? It is also called twelve a.m.

vocabularies.definition: Twelve o'clock in the middle of the day; noon.
vocabularies.example:
We have lunch at midday.
The sun is high at midday.
Midday is 12 pm.
quizzes: We usually have a delicious lunch at __________ in the school canteen with all our friends now.
The bright yellow sun is very high in the sky at __________ , so please wear your sun hat today.
In English, __________ is exactly twelve o'clock in the middle of the day, or twelve p.m.

vocabularies.definition: Twelve o'clock in the middle of the day.
vocabularies.example:
See you at noon.
It's noon already!
Let's meet at noon.
quizzes: See you at __________ tomorrow in front of the library; don't be late for our group meeting.
I can't believe it's __________ already! The morning has gone by very quickly while we were playing.
Let's meet at exactly __________ in the park to have our picnic under the large oak tree today.

vocabularies.definition: The time in the morning when the sun first appears.
vocabularies.example:
We watched the sunrise.
The sunrise was beautiful.
He wakes up at sunrise.
quizzes: We all sat on the beach and watched the beautiful orange __________ early this morning now.
The golden __________ was absolutely beautiful, with pink and purple clouds in the sky today.
He always wakes up at __________ so he can go for a long run while the air is still cool now.

vocabularies.definition: The time in the evening when the sun disappears.
vocabularies.example:
I love watching the sunset.
The sunset was red and orange.
We went home after sunset.
quizzes: I truly love watching the colorful __________ from my balcony every single evening today.
The __________ was bright red and orange as the sun disappeared behind the high mountains.
We finally went home after the __________ when the first stars started to appear in the sky now.

vocabularies.definition: The time of day when light first appears.
vocabularies.example:
The birds sing at dawn.
We started our journey at dawn.
Dawn is the beginning of the day.
quizzes: The colorful birds always start to sing at __________ when the first light of day appears now.
We started our long journey into the mountains at __________ while everyone else was asleep.
The early __________ is the beautiful beginning of the day, before the sun is truly high today.

vocabularies.definition: The time of day when it just begins to get dark.
vocabularies.example:
It gets cold at dusk.
Dusk is the time between day and night.
We returned at dusk.
quizzes: It always starts to get a bit cold at __________ , so please remember to wear your warm coat now.
The quiet __________ is the time between day and night when the sky becomes a dark blue today.
We all returned to the campsite at __________ after a long and very exciting day of exploring now.

vocabularies.definition: The season between summer and winter when leaves fall from trees.
vocabularies.example:
The leaves fall in autumn.
Autumn is my favorite season.
It's cool in autumn.
quizzes: The golden and red leaves always fall from the trees in ___________ when the weather becomes cooler.
I think that ___________ is truly my favorite season because of the beautiful colors in the forest.
It is usually very cool in ___________ , so it's the perfect time to go for long walks in the park now.

vocabularies.definition: The season between winter and summer when plants grow.
vocabularies.example:
Flowers bloom in spring.
Spring is a beautiful season.
I love spring.
quizzes: The colorful flowers start to bloom everywhere in __________ and the small birds begin to sing now.
I believe that __________ is a truly beautiful season because everything becomes green and fresh again.
I truly love __________ because I can finally go outside and play with my friends in the warm sun.

vocabularies.definition: The hottest season of the year, between spring and autumn.
vocabularies.example:
We go to the beach in summer.
It's very hot in summer.
I love summer holidays.
quizzes: We always go to the sandy beach in __________ to swim in the cool blue ocean and eat ice cream.
It is usually very hot in __________ , so please remember to drink lots of water and stay in the shade.
I truly love the long __________ holidays because I can go on exciting trips with my whole family now.

vocabularies.definition: The coldest season of the year, between autumn and spring.
vocabularies.example:
It snows in winter.
Winter is cold.
I wear a thick coat in winter.
quizzes: It often snows in __________ here, and the whole garden becomes white and very beautiful today now.
The cold __________ is finally here, so we should stay warm inside and drink some hot chocolate instead.
I always wear my very thick coat and a warm scarf in __________ to protect myself from the freezing wind.

vocabularies.definition: A chart showing the days, weeks, and months of a year.
vocabularies.example:
Check the date on the calendar.
I have a new calendar for the year.
Write the appointment on the calendar.
quizzes: Please check the exact date on the __________ to see when the school holidays start this month now.
I have a beautiful new __________ for the year with pictures of amazing animals from around the world.
You should write the dentist appointment on the __________ so you don't forget it next Monday today.

vocabularies.definition: A particular day of the month or year.
vocabularies.example:
What's the date today?
Save the date!
He has a date tonight.
quizzes: Excuse me, do you know what the __________ is today? I think it is the twenty-fifth of February now.
Please save the __________ ! My big brother's wedding is going to be on the tenth of June next year.
He is very excited because he has a __________ tonight with the girl he really likes from his school.

vocabularies.definition: A book in which you write about what you did each day.
vocabularies.example:
She writes in her diary every night.
Don't read my diary!
I keep a travel diary.
quizzes: She carefully writes in her private __________ every single night before she goes to sleep in her bed.
Please don't read my __________ ! It contains all my secret thoughts and my smallest feelings today.
I always keep a detailed travel __________ when I go on holiday so I can remember all the fun things.

vocabularies.definition: The study of things that happened in the past.
vocabularies.example:
I love learning about history.
History is my favorite subject.
That's ancient history.

vocabularies.definition: Designed or made using the most recent ideas or methods.
vocabularies.example:
We live in a modern house.
Modern technology is amazing.
The city has many modern buildings.

grammars.description: Dùng để nói về dự đoán hoặc quyết định trong tương lai.
grammars.example:
I will visit you tomorrow.
It will rain tonight.

vocabularies.definition: Nearly, but not completely.
vocabularies.example:
It's almost time to go.
I've almost finished my work.
She is almost as tall as me.
quizzes: It is __________ time to go to school, so please finish your breakfast and get your bag today now.
I have __________ finished all my difficult math homework, and I only have one small problem left.
She is surprisingly __________ as tall as me now, even though she is two years younger than I am.

vocabularies.definition: As much as is necessary; the right amount.
vocabularies.example:
Do you have enough money?
I've had enough to eat.
That's enough!
quizzes: Do you actually have __________ money to buy that large Lego set, or do you need to save more today?
Thank you, but I have already had __________ to eat and I simply can't have any more cake right now.
That is __________ ! Please stop making such a loud noise while I am trying to study for my test now.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something is fairly or completely true.
vocabularies.example:
The movie was quite good.
I'm quite sure about it.
It's quite a long way.
quizzes: The new adventure movie was __________ good, and I truly loved the part with the flying dragon now.
I am __________ sure about exactly where I left my red umbrella, so let me go and check the hall.
It is __________ a long way from here to the station, so we should start walking right now today.

vocabularies.definition: To a certain level or degree; quite.
vocabularies.example:
It's rather cold today.
I'd rather stay at home.
She is rather shy.
quizzes: It is __________ cold today, so you should definitely wear your thick coat and your warm gloves now.
I would truly __________ stay at home and read a book than go out in this heavy pouring rain today.
She is unfortunately __________ shy and she doesn't like to speak in front of a lot of people yet.

vocabularies.definition: In addition; also.
vocabularies.example:
This tea is too hot to drink.
I'm tired too.
It's too far to walk.
quizzes: This green tea is much __________ hot to drink right now, so let's wait a few minutes for it to cool.
I am feeling very tired __________ , because I spent the whole afternoon playing football in the park.
It is unfortunately much __________ far to walk to the museum today, so we should take the bus now.

vocabularies.definition: Used to make an adjective or adverb stronger.
vocabularies.example:
Thank you very much.
She is a very kind person.
It's very important.
quizzes: Thank you __________ much for the beautiful gift! I truly love the colorful drawing you made today.
She is a truly __________ kind person who always helps everyone in our class when they have a problem.
It is __________ important to remember to bring your passport when you travel to another country now.

vocabularies.definition: A small amount of something.
vocabularies.example:
I'm a bit tired.
Can I have a bit of cake?
Wait a bit.
quizzes: I am feeling a __________ tired after the long walk in the sun, so I will sit down for a minute.
Can I please have a small __________ of the delicious chocolate cake that you made this morning?
Please wait a __________ ! I need to tie my shoelaces before we start walking to the town center.

vocabularies.definition: A large amount or number of people or things.
vocabularies.example:
I have a lot of books.
Thanks a lot.
She travels a lot.
quizzes: I have a __________ of interesting books about space and stars in my large wooden bookcase now.
Thanks a __________ for helping me with my heavy bags! You are truly a very kind and helpful person.
She travels a __________ during the summer holidays because she loves to see many new places today.

vocabularies.definition: A larger amount or number of something.
vocabularies.example:
I need more time.
Would you like more coffee?
Read more books.
quizzes: I need a bit __________ time to finish this difficult puzzle; it is much harder than I first thought.
Would you like __________ hot coffee, or would you prefer a glass of cold water instead right now?
You should try to read __________ books in English if you want to improve your vocabulary quickly.

vocabularies.definition: The largest amount or number of something.
vocabularies.example:
Most people like music.
She is the most beautiful girl.
What do you like most?
quizzes: __________ people like listening to music while they are traveling on the bus or the train now.
I think she is the __________ beautiful girl in our school with her long hair and her kind smile.
What do you actually like __________ about living in the city? Is it the shops or the parks today?

vocabularies.definition: A smaller amount or number of something.
vocabularies.example:
Eat less sugar.
It takes less than an hour.
I have less money than you.
quizzes: My doctor says that I should try to eat __________ sugar if I want to stay healthy and strong today.
It usually takes __________ than an hour to fly from here to the capital city in a fast plane now.
I unfortunately have __________ money than you right now, so I can only buy a small ice cream today.

vocabularies.definition: The smallest amount or number of something.
vocabularies.example:
At least we tried.
That's the least I can do.
Which is the least expensive?
quizzes: At __________ we tried our best during the football match, even though we didn't win the gold medal.
That is truly the __________ I can do to help you after everything you have done for me this week.
Can you tell me which of these three colorful bags is the __________ expensive? I want to save money.

vocabularies.definition: A large amount of something (used with uncountable nouns).
vocabularies.example:
How much does it cost?
I don't have much time.
Thank you so much.
quizzes: Excuse me, how __________ does this beautiful red sweater cost? I can't find the price tag on it.
I am sorry, but I don't have __________ time to talk right now because I have to catch the bus today.
Thank you so __________ for the lovely flowers! They smell absolutely wonderful in my room now.

vocabularies.definition: A large number of people or things (used with countable nouns).
vocabularies.example:
How many siblings do you have?
There are many stars in the sky.
I have many friends.
quizzes: How __________ siblings do you actually have? I have two elder brothers and one younger sister now.
There are __________ bright stars in the night sky when you look at it from the top of the mountain.
I am very lucky because I have __________ good friends who are always there for me when I need them.

vocabularies.definition: A small number of people or things.
vocabularies.example:
I have a few friends.
Few people know the secret.
Wait for a few minutes.
quizzes: I have a __________ close friends in my neighborhood, and we often play football together on Sundays.
Only a __________ people in the world truly know the secret of the ancient golden dragon's treasure.
Please wait for a __________ minutes while I pack my school bag and put on my shoes for the trip.

vocabularies.definition: A small amount of something.
vocabularies.example:
A little bit of sugar.
He is a little boy.
I know a little English.
quizzes: Would you like a __________ bit of white sugar in your tea, or do you prefer it without any today?
He is a very energetic __________ boy who loves running around the garden all day with his dog now.
I only know a __________ English right now, but I am studying very hard every day to learn more.

vocabularies.definition: The whole amount or number of something.
vocabularies.example:
All the children are here.
I like all kinds of music.
That's all.
quizzes: __________ the children in the school are here today for the special assembly in the large hall.
I truly like __________ kinds of music, from classical piano to modern rock and pop songs now.
That is __________! I have finished all my work and now I can go outside and play with my friends.

vocabularies.definition: Every one of a group of things or people.
vocabularies.example:
Each student has a book.
They cost ten dollars each.
Each of us is special.
quizzes: __________ student in my class now has a new blue book to write their interesting stories in today.
These colorful red apples cost exactly ten dollars __________ , because they are imported from Japan.
My teacher says that __________ of us is truly special and has something wonderful to share now.

vocabularies.definition: Used to talk about all the people or things in a group.
vocabularies.example:
I go to school every day.
Every child deserves love.
I've seen every movie here.
quizzes: I go to school __________ day of the week except for Saturday and Sunday when I stay at home.
I truly believe that __________ child in the world deserves love, care, and a happy family today.
I have __________ almost every single movie in this cinema, and my favorite is the one about dragons.

vocabularies.definition: An amount or number of something that is not known.
vocabularies.example:
I need some help.
Some people are very kind.
Would you like some water?
quizzes: I need __________ help with this very heavy wooden box; can you please help me move it now?
__________ people are truly very kind and they always help others who are in trouble today.
Would you like __________ cold water to drink after your long walk in the hot sun today?

vocabularies.definition: Some, or every one of a group (used in questions and negatives).
vocabularies.example:
Do you have any questions?
I don't have any money.
Any time is fine.
quizzes: Do you have __________ questions about the history project? Please ask me right now if you do.
I don't have __________ money left in my wallet today, so I can't buy that new video game.
__________ time is fine for our meeting tomorrow; I will be at home all day long today now.

vocabularies.definition: Not any of a group of people or things.
vocabularies.example:
None of them knew the answer.
None of my friends are here.
I have none left.
quizzes: __________ of them knew the correct answer to the teacher's difficult question in the class today.
__________ of my friends are here at the park today, so I am playing by myself right now.
I wanted to eat another cookie, but I have __________ left in the blue jar on the kitchen table.

vocabularies.definition: Two people or things together.
vocabularies.example:
Both of them were happy.
I like both of these.
They are both tall.
quizzes: __________ of them were extremely happy when they heard the good news about the school trip now.
I like __________ of these colorful shirts, but I can only buy one of them today because of the cost.
They are __________ very tall and they are the best players in our school's basketball team now.

vocabularies.definition: One or the other of two people or things.
vocabularies.example:
Either one is fine.
I don't like it either.
You can take either path.
quizzes: __________ one is fine with me; I don't really mind which color you choose for the new room today.
I don't like eating spicy food __________ , so let's order something mild for our dinner tonight.
You can take __________ path to reach the top of the mountain; they are both very beautiful now.

vocabularies.definition: Not one and not the other of two people or things.
vocabularies.example:
Neither of them spoke.
I like neither red nor blue.
Neither plan worked.
quizzes: __________ of them spoke a single word while they were waiting for the principal in the office now.
I like __________ red nor blue; I think that green is a much more beautiful color for a dress.
__________ of the two plans worked, so we had to think of a completely new idea for the party.

grammars.description: Cách dùng 'too' và 'enough' với tính từ.
grammars.example:
It's too cold to swim.
It's not warm enough to swim.

vocabularies.definition: A company that provides regular flights for passengers or goods.
vocabularies.example:
Which airline are you flying with?
The airline lost my suitcase.
He works for a major airline.
quizzes: Which __________ are you actually flying with to London? Is it British Airways or Vietnam Airlines?
The __________ unfortunately lost my large suitcase, and I had to wait for three days to get it back.
He works for a major international __________ and he gets to travel to many different countries now.

vocabularies.definition: A place where planes land and take off, with buildings for passengers.
vocabularies.example:
We arrived at the airport early.
The airport is very busy.
I'll meet you at the airport.
quizzes: We arrived at the large __________ very early this morning to make sure we didn't miss our flight.
The __________ is extremely busy today with thousands of people traveling for the summer holidays.
I will meet you at the __________ near the information desk as soon as your plane lands now.

vocabularies.definition: A card that you must show to get on a plane.
vocabularies.example:
Don't lose your boarding pass.
Show your boarding pass at the gate.
I have my boarding pass ready.
quizzes: Please don't lose your __________ ! You need it to get on the plane and find your seat today.
You must show your __________ and your passport at the gate before you can enter the aircraft.
I have my __________ ready in my hand so I don't have to look for it when we reach the front now.

vocabularies.definition: The place where you show your ticket and leave your heavy bags.
vocabularies.example:
Go to the check-in desk.
What time is check-in?
Check-in was very fast.
quizzes: Please go directly to the __________ desk to show your ticket and leave your heavy suitcases today.
What time is __________ for our flight to Paris? I want to make sure we have enough time now.
The __________ process was surprisingly fast today, so we have plenty of time to look at the shops.

vocabularies.definition: The act of leaving a place at the beginning of a journey.
vocabularies.example:
The departure time is 10 am.
Check the departure board.
Wait in the departure lounge.
quizzes: The exact __________ time is ten a.m., so we should be at the airport at least two hours before.
Please check the large __________ board to see if our flight is on time or if it has been delayed.
We can wait in the comfortable __________ lounge and have a drink before they call us to the gate.

vocabularies.definition: A journey in a plane.
vocabularies.example:
The flight was delayed.
How was your flight?
I have a flight to London.
quizzes: I am very sorry, but your __________ to London has been delayed by two hours due to the fog now.
How was your long __________ over the ocean? Did you enjoy the food and the movies on the plane?
I have a very early __________ to Paris tomorrow morning, so I need to go to sleep right now today.

vocabularies.definition: The part of an airport where you go to get on your plane.
vocabularies.example:
Go to gate number five.
The gate is closing soon.
Wait at the gate.
quizzes: Please go directly to __________ number five; your plane is boarding and will leave in twenty minutes.
Hurry up! The __________ is closing soon and you don't want to miss your flight to the city today.
We should wait at the __________ for our flight so we can be the first people to get on the plane now.

vocabularies.definition: The bags that you carry with you when you are traveling.
vocabularies.example:
We have too much luggage.
Don't leave your luggage unattended.
Where can I get my luggage?
quizzes: We have far too much __________ for this short trip; we should try to leave some bags at home now.
Please do not leave your __________ unattended in the airport; always keep it close to you today.
Excuse me, can you tell me exactly where I can get my __________ after I get off the plane now?

vocabularies.definition: An official book that you need to visit other countries.
vocabularies.example:
Show your passport, please.
I forgot my passport at home!
Keep your passport safe.
quizzes: Please show your __________ and your boarding pass to the officer before you enter the departure area.
Oh no! I think I forgot my __________ at home on the kitchen table! I can't travel without it today.
You should definitely keep your __________ safe in a small bag that you carry with you at all times now.

vocabularies.definition: A person who flies a plane as their job.
vocabularies.example:
He wants to be a pilot.
The pilot spoke to the passengers.
She is a commercial pilot.
quizzes: He truly wants to be a __________ when he grows up so he can fly large planes all around the world.
The __________ spoke To the passengers over the speakers and told us about the weather in London today.
She is a very experienced commercial __________ who has been flying for more than fifteen years now.

vocabularies.definition: Things that are done to keep a place and people safe.
vocabularies.example:
Go through airport security.
Security is very strict.
The security guard checked my bag.
quizzes: We have to go through airport __________ now to make sure that no one is carrying anything dangerous.
The __________ at this international airport is very strict, so please follow all the rules carefully.
The __________ guard carefully checked my small bag and my pockets before letting me through today.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of paper that shows you have paid for a journey or an event.
vocabularies.example:
Do you have your flight ticket?
I bought a return ticket.
Show your ticket at the entrance.
quizzes: Do you already have your flight __________ for the trip to New York, or do you need to buy it now?
I bought a __________ ticket to the city center because it is much cheaper than buying two single ones.
Please show your __________ at the entrance of the museum so the guard can let you inside today.

vocabularies.definition: Someone who visits a place for pleasure and not for work.
vocabularies.example:
The city is full of tourists.
He is a foreign tourist.
A tourist information center.
quizzes: The whole city is full of __________ this summer because everyone wants to see the famous ancient castle.
He is a foreign __________ who has come from Japan to learn about the history and culture of our country.
There is a __________ information center near the station where you can get free maps and advice today.

vocabularies.definition: A person or company that helps people to plan and book holidays.
vocabularies.example:
The travel agent booked our trip.
Ask the travel agent for advice.
We went to the travel agent's office.
quizzes: The __________ agent successfully booked our whole trip to Africa, including the flights and the hotels.
You should ask the __________ agent for some advice on which places are the best to visit in Thailand.
We all went to the __________ agent's office yesterday to pick up our tickets and our travel brochures.

vocabularies.definition: A journey to a place and back again.
vocabularies.example:
Have a safe trip!
We went on a school trip.
I enjoyed the trip to the zoo.
quizzes: Have a very safe __________ to the mountains! I hope you have a wonderful time and see many birds.
We all went on a long school __________ to the science museum yesterday and we learned many new things.
I truly enjoyed the __________ to the large zoo with my family; my favorite animals were the tigers.

vocabularies.definition: A period of time when you have to wait because something is late.
vocabularies.example:
The flight had a long delay.
Don't delay your departure.
Sorry for the delay.
quizzes: Our flight to Paris had a very long __________ because of the heavy snow at the airport today now.
Please do not __________ your departure any longer; we need to leave the house right now for the bus.
I am very sorry for the __________ ! I couldn't find my car keys and I had to look for them everywhere.

vocabularies.definition: Someone who is traveling in a vehicle but is not the driver.
vocabularies.example:
The passengers are boarding the bus.
He is a regular passenger.
All passengers should remain seated.
quizzes: The excited __________ are now boarding the large blue bus for their trip to the beautiful seaside.
He is a regular __________ on the eight o'clock train to the city center every single morning today.
All __________ should definitely remain seated until the plane has come to a complete stop now today.

vocabularies.definition: The area in a railway station where you get on or off a train.
vocabularies.example:
The train leaves from platform three.
Wait on the platform.
Don't cross the platform.
quizzes: The fast train to London always leaves from __________ three, which is just down these stone stairs.
Please wait on the __________ for the next train; it should be arriving in exactly five minutes now.
You must never __________ the yellow line on the platform for your own safety while you are waiting.

vocabularies.definition: The act of traveling from one place to another.
vocabularies.example:
It was a long journey by train.
Enjoy your journey.
The journey took three days.
quizzes: It was a very long and exciting __________ by train across the high mountains and through the forests.
Enjoy your __________ to the seaside! I hope the weather is beautiful and the ocean is very warm today.
The __________ across the hot sandy desert took exactly three days and two nights with the camels.

vocabularies.definition: To go and get someone or something.
vocabularies.example:
Collect your bags at the carousel.
I'll collect you from the airport.
He collects stamps.
quizzes: Please remember to __________ all your heavy bags at the carousel after you get off the plane now.
I will definitely __________ you from the airport at six o'clock; I will be waiting in the car park.
My younger brother __________ colorful stamps from many different countries all around the world today.

vocabularies.definition: To hold something and move it from one place to another.
vocabularies.example:
He carried the heavy suitcase.
Can I help you carry that?
She carries a handbag.
quizzes: He carefully __________ the very heavy brown suitcase up the stairs to his room on the second floor.
Can I please help you __________ that large box to the car? It looks much too heavy for you today.
She always __________ a small black handbag with her which contains her phone and her house keys now.

vocabularies.definition: To move behind someone or something in the same direction.
vocabularies.example:
Follow the signs to the exit.
Follow me, please.
Follow the rules.
quizzes: Please __________ the bright yellow signs to the exit of the airport so you can find the taxi stand.
Please __________ me to the conference room; I will show you where you need to sit for the meeting.
It is very important to __________ all the school rules if you want to be a good and successful student.

vocabularies.definition: To move or do something more quickly than usual.
vocabularies.example:
Hurry up, we're late!
There's no hurry.
He hurried to the station.
quizzes: __________ up, please! We are already late for the movie and it starts in exactly five minutes today.
There is absolutely no __________ ; we have plenty of time before the bus arrives at the corner now.
He __________ to the station as fast as he could because he didn't want to miss the last train home.

vocabularies.definition: To stay in one place until something happens or someone arrives.
vocabularies.example:
Wait in the lounge.
Don't keep me waiting.
I've been waiting for hours.
quizzes: Please __________ in the comfortable lounge for a few minutes; the doctor will see you very soon.
Please do not keep me __________ any longer; I have been standing here for more than twenty minutes.
I have been __________ for hours for the rain to stop so I can finally go for a walk in the garden.

grammars.structure: Going to vs Will for travel
grammars.description: Dùng 'going to' cho kế hoạch và 'will' cho quyết định ngay lúc đó.
grammars.example:
I am going to visit Paris next month.
I will book the flight now.

vocabularies.definition: To cook food in an oven using dry heat.
vocabularies.example:
I'm baking a chocolate cake.
The bread is baking in the oven.
She likes to bake cookies.
quizzes: I am __________ a delicious chocolate cake for my best friend's birthday party today.
The fresh white bread is carefully __________ in the hot oven and it smells absolutely wonderful.
She truly likes to __________ sweet cookies with her grandmother on rainy Sunday afternoons now.

vocabularies.definition: To cook food in water that is very hot and has bubbles.
vocabularies.example:
Boil the water for tea.
I'm boiling some eggs.
The kettle is boiling.
quizzes: Please __________ the water for the tea; it needs to be very hot to make a good cup now.
I am __________ some white eggs in the small silver pot for our breakfast this morning today.
Listen! The blue kettle is finally __________ in the kitchen, so we can make the coffee now.

vocabularies.definition: To prepare food by heating it.
vocabularies.example:
My mom is a great cook.
He is cooking dinner tonight.
I love to cook Italian food.
quizzes: My mom is a truly great __________ who can make many different types of delicious meals today.
He is __________ a healthy dinner tonight for his whole family with lots of fresh vegetables.
I truly love to __________ Italian food, especially pasta with a very rich red tomato sauce now.

vocabularies.definition: To cook food in hot oil or fat.
vocabularies.example:
Fry the fish in a little oil.
He loves fried chicken.
Don't fry it for too long.
quizzes: Please __________ the fresh fish in a little olive oil until it becomes golden and very crispy.
He truly loves eating __________ chicken which is served with some hot and salty French fries now.
Please do not __________ the onions for too long, or they will become much too dark and bitter today.

vocabularies.definition: To cook food over or under strong, direct heat.
vocabularies.example:
We are grilling burgers in the garden.
Grill the meat on both sides.
He bought a new grill.
quizzes: We are all __________ juicy burgers in the garden for our exciting summer afternoon party now.
You should __________ the meat on both sides until it is perfectly cooked and very tasty today.
He bought a large new __________ for the kitchen so he can cook healthy fish and vegetables easily.

vocabularies.definition: To put different things together so that they become one.
vocabularies.example:
Mix the flour and sugar together.
Don't mix these two colors.
Mix it well.
quizzes: Please __________ the white flour and the sweet sugar together in the large glass bowl now.
Please do not __________ these two colors of paint, or you will get a very strange and dark result today.
You must __________ the cake batter very well to make sure there are no lumps in it before baking.

vocabularies.definition: To cook meat or vegetables in an oven or over a fire.
vocabularies.example:
We have roast chicken on Sundays.
Roast the vegetables in the oven.
I love roast beef.
quizzes: We always have delicious __________ chicken on Sundays with my whole family at the large table.
You should __________ the colorful vegetables in the hot oven with some herbs and a bit of oil.
I truly love eating __________ beef with some hot gravy and some soft boiled potatoes today now.

vocabularies.definition: A round container that is open at the top and used for food.
vocabularies.example:
Put the fruit in a bowl.
He ate a bowl of cereal.
I need a clean bowl.
quizzes: Please put all the fresh __________ in a large wooden bowl and place it on the kitchen table now.
He ate a large __________ of chocolate cereal for his breakfast before he went to school today.
I truly need a clean __________ to mix the eggs in; can you please find one for me in the cupboard?

vocabularies.definition: A small container with a handle used for drinking tea or coffee.
vocabularies.example:
Would you like a cup of tea?
He won a gold cup.
Show me your favorite cup.
quizzes: Would you like a hot __________ of green tea, or would you prefer a glass of cold fruit juice now?
He was very proud because he won a large gold __________ in the school's annual swimming race today.
Please show me your favorite __________ ! I want to see the beautiful picture that is painted on it.

vocabularies.definition: An object with three or four points used for picking up food.
vocabularies.example:
Eat your salad with a fork.
Where are the forks?
I dropped my fork.
quizzes: You should definitely eat your fresh green salad with a __________ instead of using your fingers now.
Excuse me, do you know exactly where the __________ are? I need one for my piece of chocolate cake.
Oh no! I accidentally dropped my __________ on the floor; can you please bring me a clean one today?

vocabularies.definition: A container made of glass used for drinking cold liquids.
vocabularies.example:
Can I have a glass of water?
The windows are made of glass.
Be careful, it's glass.
quizzes: Can I please have a cold __________ of water? I am very thirsty after playing football in the sun.
All the large windows in our new house are made of very thick and clear __________ to let in light.
Please be very careful with that expensive vase; it's made of fragile __________ and it might break.

vocabularies.definition: An object with a sharp blade and a handle used for cutting food.
vocabularies.example:
Use a knife to cut the bread.
Be careful with that sharp knife.
I'll sharpen the knife.
quizzes: You should use a sharp __________ to cut the fresh bread into even slices for the sandwiches now.
Please be extremely careful with that sharp __________ ! It is for adults only and not for children.
I will __________ the knife for you so you can cut the meat more easily for our dinner tonight today.

vocabularies.definition: A flat round dish that you put food on to eat.
vocabularies.example:
Put the sandwiches on a plate.
He licked the plate clean!
I need a large plate.
quizzes: Please put all the delicious __________ on a large white plate and bring them to the living room.
He was so hungry that he actually licked his entire __________ clean after eating the chocolate cake!
I need a very large __________ for the big roast chicken that I just took out of the hot oven now.

vocabularies.definition: An object with a small round bowl and a handle used for eating.
vocabularies.example:
Eat the soup with a spoon.
A teaspoon of sugar.
I need another spoon.
quizzes: You should eat your hot tomato soup with a __________ so you don't make a mess on your shirt now.
Please add exactly one small __________ of white sugar to my cup of coffee and stir it well today.
I truly need another __________ because this one is a bit too small for eating my cereal right now.

vocabularies.definition: A large metal box that is kept cold for storing food.
vocabularies.example:
Put the milk in the fridge.
The fridge is empty.
Is there any juice in the fridge?
quizzes: Please put the fresh __________ in the cold fridge so it stays good for our breakfast tomorrow morning.
Oh no! The __________ is completely empty, so we need to go to the supermarket and buy some food.
Is there any cold orange __________ in the fridge? I would really like a glass of it right now today.

vocabularies.definition: A large piece of equipment used for baking or roasting food.
vocabularies.example:
The pizza is in the oven.
Turn off the oven, please.
Wait for the oven to heat up.
quizzes: The delicious cheese __________ is in the hot oven and it will be ready to eat in ten minutes now.
Please remember to turn off the large __________ as soon as you have finished baking the cookies today.
You must wait for the __________ to heat up to the correct temperature before you put the cake inside.

vocabularies.definition: A flat board used for keeping things on.
vocabularies.example:
Put the books on the shelf.
The shelf is full of jars.
He is building a new shelf.
quizzes: Please put all your colorful school __________ on the wooden shelf in your room before you go out.
The high __________ in the kitchen is completely full of glass jars with different kinds of spices.
He is busy building a new __________ for his large collection of model planes in the garage today.

vocabularies.definition: A sweet substance that comes from plants and is used in food.
vocabularies.example:
Do you take sugar in your tea?
Don't eat too much sugar.
I need some sugar for the cake.
quizzes: Do you actually take any white __________ in your hot tea, or do you prefer it without any today?
My dentist says that I shouldn't eat too much __________ if I want to keep my teeth healthy and white.
I truly need some __________ for the large chocolate cake I am making for the special party tonight.

vocabularies.definition: A white substance from the sea used for making food taste better.
vocabularies.example:
Pass the salt, please.
Sea water contains salt.
The soup needs a bit more salt.
quizzes: Could you please pass the __________ ? My soup is a bit tasteless and it needs some more flavor today.
Sea water always contains a lot of __________ , which is why you can't drink it when you are thirsty.
I think this vegetable __________ needs a bit more salt and perhaps a little bit of black pepper too.

vocabularies.definition: A black or white spice that makes food taste strong and spicy.
vocabularies.example:
I like black pepper on my eggs.
Add some green pepper to the salad.
Pepper can be spicy.
quizzes: I truly like to have a bit of black __________ on my fried eggs every single morning for breakfast.
You should add some local green __________ to the fresh salad to make it more colorful and healthy now.
Please be careful because this black __________ can be very spicy if you add too much to your food.

vocabularies.definition: A white or brown powder made from grain, used for making bread.
vocabularies.example:
A bag of white flour.
Mix the flour with water.
He has flour on his face.
quizzes: Could you please buy a large bag of white __________ from the shop? I want to bake some bread today.
You should carefully __________ the white flour with some warm water and yeast to make the dough now.
He was baking cookies and now he has some white __________ all over his face and his new blue shirt.

vocabularies.definition: A smooth liquid that comes from plants and is used in cooking.
vocabularies.example:
Use olive oil for the salad.
Heat the oil in the pan.
The engine needs oil.
quizzes: You should always use high-quality olive __________ for the fresh salad to make it taste much better.
Please __________ the vegetable oil in the large pan for a few minutes before you add the fresh fish.
The old car __________ definitely needs some more oil, otherwise it will stop working very soon today.

vocabularies.definition: A pale yellow solid food made from cream, used on bread.
vocabularies.example:
Spread some butter on the toast.
I love the taste of butter.
Keep the butter in the fridge.
quizzes: Please spread a little bit of yellow __________ on the warm toast while it is still hot from the toaster.
I truly love the rich taste of __________ on fresh bread, especially when it is still warm and soft now.
You must definitely keep the __________ in the cold fridge, otherwise it will melt in this hot weather.

vocabularies.definition: A sweet, sticky food made by bees.
vocabularies.example:
Bees make honey.
Honey is very sweet.
I like honey on my pancakes.
quizzes: Do you know that busy yellow __________ make honey from the flowers that grow in the green fields?
This golden __________ is very sweet and it is much healthier for you than eating white sugar today.
I truly like to have some sweet __________ on my pancakes on Sunday mornings when I have more time.

vocabularies.definition: A sweet, soft food made by boiling fruit with sugar.
vocabularies.example:
Strawberry jam is delicious.
Put some jam on your bread.
He is in a traffic jam.
quizzes: This red __________ jam is absolutely delicious; did you make it with the fruit from our own garden?
You should put some sweet __________ on your fresh bread for a quick and very tasty snack right now.
He is unfortunately stuck in a very long traffic __________ and he won't be home for another hour today.

grammars.description: Dùng để mô tả các bước nấu ăn.
grammars.example: First, boil the water. Then, add the tea. Finally, add some honey.

vocabularies.definition: Used to introduce a fact that makes the rest of the sentence surprising.
vocabularies.example:
Although it was raining, we went out.
He is happy although he is poor.
Although she was tired, she finished the work.
quizzes: ___________ it was raining very heavily, we all decided to go out and play football in the park.
He is always very happy __________ he doesn't have much money or a large house to live in today.
___________ she was extremely tired, she still finished all her difficult math homework before bed.

vocabularies.definition: Used to give a reason for something.
vocabularies.example:
I'm happy because I passed the test.
He stayed home because he was sick.
Just because I'm small doesn't mean I'm weak.
quizzes: I am extremely happy __________ I finally passed my difficult English test with a very high mark.
He stayed at home today __________ he was feeling very sick and had a high temperature now.
Just __________ I am small doesn't mean I am weak; I can carry those heavy bags for you today.

vocabularies.definition: At an earlier time; earlier than another event.
vocabularies.example:
Wash your hands before eating.
I've seen this movie before.
Think before you speak.
quizzes: You must always wash your hands __________ eating your lunch to keep yourself healthy and clean.
I am sure that I have seen this interesting movie __________ , but I can't remember the ending now.
It is always a very good idea to think carefully __________ you speak to anyone in an angry way.

vocabularies.definition: At a later time; later than another event.
vocabularies.example:
We went home after the party.
He arrived after I left.
After you!
quizzes: We all went home together __________ the wonderful birthday party finished late last night today.
He unfortunately arrived at the station just __________ my train had left for the city center now.
Please go __________ you! I am not in a hurry at all and I can wait for the next bus to come.

vocabularies.definition: From the beginning to the end of a period of time.
vocabularies.example:
It rained during the night.
Don't talk during the exam.
We stayed inside during the storm.
quizzes: It rained very heavily __________ the night, and today the garden is completely wet and green.
Please remember that you must not talk __________ the exam, or the teacher will take your paper away.
We all stayed safely inside the house __________ the big storm while the wind was blowing outside.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something will happen only after another thing happens.
vocabularies.example:
If it rains, we won't go.
I'll help you if you want.
Ask me if you need anything.
quizzes: __________ it rains tomorrow, we won't be able to go for a picnic in the mountains today now.
I will definitely help you with your project __________ you truly want me to; just let me know today.
Please don't hesitate to ask me __________ you need anything at all while I am at the shop now.

vocabularies.definition: In place of something else.
vocabularies.example:
I'll have tea instead of coffee.
He didn't go; I went instead.
Use a pencil instead of a pen.
quizzes: I think I will have a hot cup of tea __________ of coffee this morning, thank you very much.
He didn't go to the museum because he was busy; so I went __________ to see the new exhibition.
You should use a sharp pencil __________ of a pen to draw the map, so you can erase any mistakes.

vocabularies.definition: From a particular time in the past until now; because.
vocabularies.example:
I haven't seen him since Monday.
Since you're here, help me.
It has been long since we met.
quizzes: I haven't seen my best friend __________ last Monday, and I really miss playing football with him.
___________ you are already here in the kitchen, could you please help me wash these dirty dishes?
It has been a very long time __________ we last met; you have grown much taller than before today.

vocabularies.definition: Used to give a result; therefore.
vocabularies.example:
I was tired, so I went to bed.
He is so tall!
Is that so?
quizzes: I was extremely tired after the long trip, __________ I went to bed very early last night today.
Wow, he is __________ tall that his head almost touches the top of the door when he comes in!
Is that really __________ ? I can't believe that she actually won the first prize in the competition.

vocabularies.definition: Used when comparing two things or people.
vocabularies.example:
He is taller than me.
Better late than never.
I have more than enough.
quizzes: He is much taller __________ me now, even though I am two years older than he is in our school.
Well, it is better to arrive late __________ to never arrive at all; the party is still going on.
