I have much more __________ enough money to buy that small toy; I will also buy some sweets now.

vocabularies.definition: Used to mean 'although' or 'however'.
vocabularies.example:
He's very strong, though he's small.
It's hard work, though.
Even though it was late, they stayed.
quizzes: He is a very strong and brave boy, __________ he is much smaller than all the other children today.
It is a lot of hard work, __________ , I truly enjoy every minute of my time in the garden now.
Even __________ it was already very late at night, they all stayed until the end of the movie.

vocabularies.definition: Up to a particular time.
vocabularies.example:
Wait until I come back.
It didn't rain until midnight.
Stay until the end.
quizzes: Please wait here __________ I come back from the shop; I won't be more than ten minutes now today.
It didn't start to rain __________ almost midnight, when everyone was already sleeping in their beds.
We should definitely stay __________ the very end of the concert to see the final song tonight.

vocabularies.definition: At what time; at the time that something happens.
vocabularies.example:
When will you arrive?
He was happy when he saw me.
When it's cold, wear a coat.
quizzes: __________ will you actually arrive at the station tomorrow? I will come to meet you in my car.
He was extremely happy __________ he finally saw his parents after the long school trip today now.
__________ it is very cold outside, you should always wear your thick coat and a warm scarf today.

vocabularies.definition: During the time that something is happening.
vocabularies.example:
I read a book while waiting.
While you were out, someone called.
It's worth a while.
quizzes: I read an interesting book __________ I was waiting for the bus to arrive at the station today.
__________ you were out at the supermarket, someone called to leave a message for your father now.
It is definitely worth __________ to visit that old museum if you have some free time this afternoon.

vocabularies.definition: Except if; used to say that something will only happen in one case.
vocabularies.example:
You won't pass unless you study.
Don't go unless I tell you.
Unless it rains, we will go.
quizzes: You won't pass your final English test __________ you study hard and practice every single day now.
Please do not go outside __________ I tell you that it is safe; the storm is still very strong.
__________ it rains very heavily, we will still go for a walk in the forest tomorrow morning today.

vocabularies.definition: Used to talk about a choice between two possibilities.
vocabularies.example:
I don't know whether he'll come.
He asked whether I was ready.
Whether you like it or not.
quizzes: I truly don't know __________ he will come to the party tonight or if he has too much work today.
He asked __________ I was ready to start the journey to the mountains yet, and I said that I was.
__________ you like it or not, you must finish your green vegetables before you can have any cake.

vocabularies.definition: Used to connect different choices or possibilities.
vocabularies.example:
Tea or coffee?
Is it true or false?
Either now or never.
quizzes: Would you like a hot cup of tea __________ coffee this afternoon? Both are already made for you.
Is it really true __________ false that the ancient Romans built this beautiful stone bridge here?
It's either now __________ never! We have to make a decision right away if we want to catch the bus.

vocabularies.definition: Used to connect two ideas that are different or opposite.
vocabularies.example:
I'm small but strong.
Everyone went but me.
I'm sorry, but I can't.
quizzes: I am quite small __________ I am very strong, and I can lift this heavy wooden box easily today.
Everyone went to the cinema to see the new movie __________ me; I had to stay home and study today.
I am extremely sorry, __________ I simply can't come to your football match this Saturday afternoon.

vocabularies.definition: Used to connect two words or parts of a sentence.
vocabularies.example:
Bread and butter.
You and I.
Go and see.
quizzes: I love eating fresh bread __________ butter for my breakfast every morning while the bread is hot.
You __________ I should Definitely go to the beach together this weekend if the weather is beautiful.
Please go __________ see who is knocking at the front door; it might be the postman with my parcel.

vocabularies.definition: Until now; used to something that has not happened.
vocabularies.example:
Is it ready yet?
I haven't finished yet.
He is small, yet brave.
quizzes: Is the delicious chocolate cake ready __________ ? I am very hungry and I can smell it from here.
I haven't quite finished all my difficult math homework __________ , so I need ten more minutes now.
He is a very small boy, __________ he is extremely brave and he isn't afraid of the dark at all.

vocabularies.definition: In addition to something else; too.
vocabularies.example:
He is also a teacher.
I also like chocolate.
She also sang a song.
quizzes: He is not only a very good doctor but he is __________ a teacher at the local university today.
I __________ like chocolate ice cream very much, especially when it is served with some fresh fruit.
She didn't just play the piano amazingly, she __________ sang a beautiful song for all of us now.

vocabularies.definition: In addition; also.
vocabularies.example:
Me too!
It's too hot.
I'm tired too.
quizzes: Oh, me __________ ! I would truly love to have a large glass of cold orange juice right now today.
It is much __________ hot to go for a walk in the afternoon sun, so let's stay inside and read.
I am feeling quite tired __________ after our long hike in the mountains, so I will sit down now.

vocabularies.definition: Before the present time or earlier than expected.
vocabularies.example:
I've already eaten.
Is it ten o'clock already?
He has already left.
quizzes: Thank you, but I have __________ eaten a very big lunch, so I can't have any more cake right now.
Is it ten o'clock __________ ? Time passes so quickly when you are having fun with your friends!
He has __________ left the house to catch the early bus to the city center, so you missed him.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something is possible; perhaps.
vocabularies.example:
Maybe it will rain.
Maybe he's right.
Can you help? Maybe.
quizzes: __________ it will rain this afternoon, so we should take our umbrellas with us when we go out.
__________ he is actually right about the answer to this difficult question; let's check the book.
Can you help me with this heavy bag? __________ , if I finish my own work first, I will help.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something is possible; maybe.
vocabularies.example:
Perhaps he will come later.
Perhaps it's for the best.
Give her some flowers, perhaps.
quizzes: __________ he will come later this evening when he has finished all his work at the office now.
__________ it is for the best that we didn't go to the beach today, because it is starting to rain.
You should give her some colorful flowers, __________ , to say thank you for her help this morning.

grammars.description: Cách nối các mệnh đề trong câu.
grammars.example:
I went out although it was raining.
I stayed home because I was sick.

vocabularies.definition: Used for getting someone's attention politely.
vocabularies.example:
Excuse me, where is the station?
Excuse me, can I pass?
Excuse me, is this seat taken?
quizzes: ___________ , could you please tell me where the nearest train station is located around here?
___________ , can I please pass through here? I need to get to the other side of the crowded bus.
___________ , is this seat taken by anyone? I would really like to sit down and rest for a while.

vocabularies.definition: Used for asking someone to repeat what they just said.
vocabularies.example:
Pardon? Could you repeat that?
I beg your pardon.
Pardon me for being late.
quizzes: ___________ ? I'm afraid I didn't hear you because the music in the cafe was very loud today.
I beg your ___________ , but could you please speak a little more slowly so I can understand you?
___________ me for being late to the meeting; I got stuck in a very long traffic jam on the way.

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying that something is not difficult or annoying.
vocabularies.example:
Thank you! - No problem.
No problem, I can help you.
It's no problem at all.
quizzes: Thank you very much for your help! - Oh, ___________ at all! I was very happy to help you today.
___________ , I can definitely help you carry those heavy shopping bags to your car right now.
It is ___________ at all; I have plenty of free time this afternoon to show you the way to the park.

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying 'yes' in a very sure way.
vocabularies.example:
Can I help? - Of course!
Of course she is coming.
Of course I remember you.
quizzes: Can I help you with your math project? - ___________ ! That would be extremely kind of you today.
___________ she is coming to your birthday party tonight; she has already bought you a present!
___________ I remember you! We went to the same primary school together many years ago now.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone not to worry about something.
vocabularies.example:
I forgot it. - Never mind.
Never mind the mess.
Never mind, it doesn't matter.
quizzes: I'm sorry, I forgot to bring your book. - Oh, ___________ , you can just bring it to school tomorrow.
___________ the mess in the living room; I am going to clean it up as soon as I finish my dinner.
___________ , it really doesn't matter at all; we can still play the game without the extra pieces.

vocabularies.meaning: không có gì (đáp lại cám ơn)
vocabularies.definition: Used as a polite answer when someone thanks you.
vocabularies.example:
Thanks! - You're welcome.
You're always welcome here.
You're very welcome.
quizzes: Thanks for the delicious cake! - ___________ ; I am very glad that you enjoyed it this afternoon!
You are always ___________ here at my house; please come and visit me whenever you have some time.
___________ very welcome! It was a pleasure to show you around our beautiful city today now.

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying goodbye to someone in a friendly way.
vocabularies.example:
Goodbye, take care!
Take care of yourself.
Take care on the road.
quizzes: Goodbye, my friend, ___________ ! I hope you have a very safe and wonderful journey home today.
Please ___________ of yourself while you are away at summer camp; don't forget to wear a hat!
You should definitely ___________ on the road because it is starting to rain and it might be slippery.

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying goodbye when you expect to see them again.
vocabularies.example:
Bye! See you later.
See you later at the party.
See you later, alligator!
quizzes: Bye for now! ___________ later at the park when we meet to play our football match today.
___________ later at the party; I am really looking forward to seeing everyone there tonight!
___________ later, alligator! - After a while, crocodile! That is a funny way to say goodbye now.

vocabularies.definition: Used for wishing someone success in something they are doing.
vocabularies.example:
Good luck with your exams!
I wish you good luck.
Good luck to everyone.
quizzes: ___________ with your difficult English exams tomorrow! I am sure that you will do very well now.
I truly wish you ___________ with your new job in the city; I hope you enjoy working there today.
___________ to everyone who is taking part in the school's annual swimming race this afternoon!

vocabularies.definition: Used for praising someone who has done something well.
vocabularies.example:
Well done on passing the test!
Well done, everyone!
That was well done.
quizzes: ___________ on passing your difficult driving test! Now you can drive your father's car today.
___________ , everyone! You all worked extremely hard to make this school play a great success now.
That was ___________ ; your painting of the mountains looks exactly like a real photograph today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to hurry or to be more reasonable.
vocabularies.example:
Come on, we're late!
Come on, you can do it!
Oh, come on, don't be sad.
quizzes: ___________ , we are very late already! We must hurry up if we want to catch the last train home.
___________ , you can definitely do it! Just take a deep breath and keep trying your best today.
Oh, ___________ , please don't be sad! Everything will be fine once we find your lost toy today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for warning someone that they are in danger.
vocabularies.example:
Look out! There's a car.
Look out for the wet floor.
Look out behind you!
quizzes: ___________ ! There is a fast car coming down the road, so stay on the pavement right now today.
Please ___________ for the wet floor in the kitchen; I just washed it and it is very slippery now.
___________ behind you! There is a large low branch on the tree that you might hit your head on.

vocabularies.definition: Used for warning someone of a danger or problem.
vocabularies.example:
Watch out for the big dog!
Watch out, it's hot.
Watch out for pickpockets.
quizzes: ___________ for the big angry dog that is barking behind the garden fence right now today!
___________ ! The soup is still extremely hot, so please wait a few minutes before you eat it.
You should definitely ___________ for pickpockets when you are walking in the crowded market today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to avoid danger or mistakes.
vocabularies.example:
Be careful with that knife.
Be careful when you cross the road.
Be careful what you say.
quizzes: Please ___________ with that sharp knife; it is for cutting the meat and it is not a toy today.
You must always ___________ when you cross the busy road; look both ways for cars first now.
Please ___________ what you say to your sister today; she is already feeling a little bit sad.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone that they should not be upset.
vocabularies.example:
Don't worry, be happy.
Don't worry about me.
Don't worry, everything is fine.
quizzes: ___________ , be happy! We still have plenty of time to finish our school project before Monday.
Please ___________ about me while I am away on my trip; I will call you every single evening.
___________ , everything is going to be perfectly fine as long as we all work together today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to go or act more quickly.
vocabularies.example:
Hurry up or we'll miss the train.
Hurry up and get ready.
Hurry up, please.
quizzes: Please ___________ or we will miss the last train home and we'll have to wait for a long time.
___________ and get ready for the party now; all your friends are already waiting for you today.
___________ , please! We need to leave the house in five minutes if we want to be on time now.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to move or work more slowly.
vocabularies.example:
Slow down, you're driving too fast.
Slow down and think.
Can you slow down, please?
quizzes: Please ___________ , you are driving much too fast for this small and narrow country road today.
Just ___________ and think carefully about the answer before you write it down on your paper now.
Can you please ___________ ? I am finding it quite difficult to keep up with you when we walk.

vocabularies.definition: Used for wishing someone a quick recovery from being sick.
vocabularies.example:
I heard you're sick. Get well soon!
Get well soon, my friend.
We all hope you get well soon.
quizzes: I heard that you are feeling sick today. ___________ ! We all miss playing with you in the park.
___________ , my dear friend; I am sending you some beautiful flowers to make you feel better.
We all truly hope that you ___________ after your operation; we hope to see you back at school.

vocabularies.definition: Used for wishing someone a happy day on their birthday.
vocabularies.example:
Happy birthday to you!
Have a very happy birthday.
I sent her a happy birthday card.
quizzes: ___________ to you! I hope you have a truly wonderful day with all your family and friends today.
I hope you have a very ___________ and receive many exciting presents for your special day now.
I sent her a very colorful ___________ card with a picture of a large chocolate cake on the front.

vocabularies.definition: Used for wishing someone a happy Christmas.
vocabularies.example:
Merry Christmas to all!
I wish you a Merry Christmas.
We had a Merry Christmas together.
quizzes: ___________ to all! I hope you have a peaceful and happy holiday with your whole family now.
I truly wish you a very ___________ and a happy new year filled with lots of joy and success.
We all had a very ___________ together, eating delicious food and playing many funny games today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for making a suggestion.
vocabularies.example:
How about going to the park?
How about a cup of tea?
How about tomorrow?
quizzes: ___________ going to the park this afternoon to play football while the sun is still shining?
___________ a hot cup of tea and some sweet cookies before we start our homework together now?
___________ meeting at the library tomorrow morning instead? I am a bit too busy to go today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for asking someone what is wrong.
vocabularies.example:
You look sad. What's the matter?
What's the matter with your arm?
Is anything the matter?
quizzes: You look quite sad today. ___________ ? Is there anything I can do to make you feel better now?
___________ with your arm? It looks very red and sore; did you fall down while playing outside?
Is anything ___________ ? You haven't said a single word since we arrived at the shopping center.

vocabularies.definition: Used for offering something to someone politely.
vocabularies.example:
Would you like some cake?
Would you like to come with us?
What would you like to drink?
quizzes: ___________ some more chocolate cake? I baked it myself this morning and it's very delicious.
___________ to come with us to the cinema tonight to see the new movie about the space journey?
What ___________ to drink? We have fresh orange juice, cold milk, and hot chocolate for you today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying that you believe something is true.
vocabularies.example:
Is he coming? - I think so.
I think so, but I'm not sure.
Yes, I think so too.
quizzes: Is he coming to play football with us? - ___________ , but he might be a little bit late today.
___________ , but I'm not entirely sure; we should probably check the bus timetable again now.
Yes, ___________ too! It is much too cold to go for a swim in the sea on a day like today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying that you want something NOT to happen.
vocabularies.example:
Will it rain? - I hope not.
I hope not for your sake.
Is he angry? - I hope not.
quizzes: Will it rain during our picnic tomorrow? - Oh, ___________ ! I really want us to have a good time.
___________ for your sake, because if it does, you won't be able to finish your garden project.
Is the teacher angry because we were late? - ___________ ; let's go and apologize to her now.

grammars.description: Các cách diễn đạt thông dụng trong giao tiếp hàng ngày.
grammars.example: You're welcome. See you later. Good luck.

vocabularies.definition: Any person; it does not matter who.
vocabularies.example:
Does anyone know the answer?
Anyone can do it.
I didn't tell anyone.
quizzes: Does ___________ in this large classroom know the correct answer to this difficult question?
___________ can do it if they just try their best and practice every single day today now.
I didn't tell ___________ my secret because I wanted to keep it a surprise for your birthday.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is not known or named.
vocabularies.example:
Someone is knocking at the door.
I need someone to help me.
Someone told me a secret.
quizzes: Listen! ___________ is knocking loudly at the front door; can you go and see who it is today?
I truly need ___________ to help me with this heavy wooden box; it is much too heavy for me.
___________ told me a very interesting secret about the new teacher, but I won't tell you yet.

vocabularies.definition: Not any person; nobody.
vocabularies.example:
No one knows where he is.
No one was home.
I saw no one.
quizzes: Absolutely ___________ knows exactly where he is hiding; we have looked everywhere in the house.
I knocked on the door but ___________ was home today; I think they have all gone to the beach.
I saw ___________ in the park this morning because it was raining very heavily and it was cold.

vocabularies.definition: All the people in a group.
vocabularies.example:
Everyone is happy.
Hello, everyone!
Everyone needs a friend.
quizzes: ___________ is very happy today because the school holidays are finally starting tomorrow now.
Hello, ___________ ! I am so glad that you could all come to my birthday party this afternoon.
___________ truly needs a good friend to talk to when they are feeling a little bit sad today.

vocabularies.definition: Any object, event, or situation.
vocabularies.example:
Do you need anything?
I can't see anything.
Anything is possible.
quizzes: Do you need ___________ else from the supermarket today? I am going there in my car right now.
It is so dark in this room that I actually can't see ___________ at all; where is the light?
My father always says that ___________ is possible if you work hard enough and never give up.

vocabularies.definition: A thing that is not known or named.
vocabularies.example:
There is something in my eye.
I have something for you.
Something is wrong.
quizzes: Ouch! There is ___________ small and sharp in my eye; can you please help me take it out now?
I have ___________ very special for you in this small blue box; I hope you like it today!
___________ is definitely wrong with the car because it is making a very strange and loud noise.

vocabularies.definition: Not anything; no single thing.
vocabularies.example:
There is nothing in the box.
I have nothing to say.
Nothing is free.
quizzes: I opened the large wooden box but there was ___________ inside it; it was completely empty today.
I have absolutely ___________ more to say about this matter; I have already told you the whole truth.
My grandmother always told me that ___________ is truly free in this world; you must work for it.

vocabularies.definition: All things in a particular situation.
vocabularies.example:
Everything is ready.
I like everything here.
Tell me everything.
quizzes: ___________ is finally ready for the party; the cake is baked and the balloons are all up now.
I truly like ___________ about this beautiful new house, especially the large and green garden.
Please tell me ___________ that happened at school today; I want to know every single detail.

vocabularies.definition: Any place; in, at, or to any place.
vocabularies.example:
I can't find it anywhere.
Go anywhere you like.
It's not anywhere here.
quizzes: I have looked in every single room but I can't find my lost toy ___________ in the house today.
You can go ___________ you like during the school holidays, as long as you tell me where you are.
It is definitely not ___________ here; I have checked under the bed and behind the large cupboard.

vocabularies.definition: In, at, or to a place that is not named.
vocabularies.example:
He lives somewhere in London.
Let's go somewhere quiet.
It must be somewhere.
quizzes: He lives ___________ in the middle of London, but I don't know the name of the street today now.
Let's go ___________ quiet to read our books, away from the loud music and the noisy children.
My blue pen must be ___________ in this room; I was just using it to write my English homework.

vocabularies.definition: Not in, at, or to any place.
vocabularies.example:
There is nowhere to hide.
The path led to nowhere.
I have nowhere to go.
quizzes: In this small and empty room, there is absolutely ___________ to hide from my big brother today.
The narrow path through the dark forest seemed to lead to ___________ and we stayed lost for hours.
I have ___________ else to go this afternoon, so I think I will just stay at home and watch TV.

vocabularies.definition: In, at, or to every place.
vocabularies.example:
I've looked everywhere.
There were flowers everywhere.
Love is everywhere.
quizzes: I have looked ___________ for my lost keys, but I still can't find them anywhere in the garden.
There were beautiful and colorful flowers ___________ in the green park this spring morning today.
My mother always says that love is ___________ if you just look for it with a kind and open heart.

vocabularies.definition: Any person; anyone.
vocabularies.example:
Is there anybody there?
Anybody can join.
I don't know anybody.
quizzes: Is there ___________ there in the dark hallway? I think I heard a strange noise just now.
___________ can join the school's exciting chess club today, even if they have never played before.
I don't know ___________ at this large party, so I am feeling a little bit shy and quiet now.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is not named; someone.
vocabularies.example:
Somebody help me!
Somebody stole my bag.
I saw somebody in the garden.
quizzes: ___________ help me, please! I have fallen and I can't get up by myself today now.
Oh no! ___________ stole my expensive blue bag while I was busy looking at the shop window.
I am sure that I saw ___________ standing in the dark garden late last night, but I was afraid.

vocabularies.definition: Not any person; no one.
vocabularies.example:
Nobody likes me.
There was nobody in the room.
Nobody knows the truth.
quizzes: He feels very sad today because he thinks that ___________ likes him, but that isn't true at all.
There was ___________ in the large room when I entered; it was completely silent and empty now.
Absolutely ___________ knows the whole truth about what happened at the museum last week today.

vocabularies.definition: All the people in a group; everyone.
vocabularies.example:
Everybody likes ice cream.
Is everybody ready?
Everybody knows that.
quizzes: ___________ likes eating cold chocolate ice cream on a very hot summer afternoon like today.
Is ___________ ready to go to the beach yet? The big bus is waiting for us outside right now.
___________ knows that the sun rises in the east every morning; it's a very simple fact now.

vocabularies.definition: Used for changing the subject or finishing a talk.
vocabularies.example:
I'll do it anyway.
It's too late anyway.
Anyway, let's go.
quizzes: I know it is difficult, but I will do it ___________ because I want to finish my work today.
It is probably too late to go to the cinema now ___________ , so let's just stay home and watch TV.
___________ , let's go to the park now; the sun is finally shining and it's not raining anymore.

vocabularies.definition: In addition to someone or something else.
vocabularies.example:
What else do you need?
Someone else called.
Is there anything else?
quizzes: What ___________ do you need from the shop besides bread and milk? I am going there now today.
Someone ___________ called you while you were in the shower, but they didn't leave a message for you.
Is there ___________ else I can help you with before I go home this evening? Just ask me now.

vocabularies.definition: At any time; at any time in the past.
vocabularies.example:
Have you ever been to Paris?
Nothing ever happens.
Forever and ever.
quizzes: Have you ___________ been to Paris to see the famous Eiffel Tower? I would truly love to go there.
Nothing ___________ interesting happens in this very quiet and small village where I live now today.
I will be your best friend forever and ___________ , and I will always help you when you need me.

vocabularies.definition: Not at any time; not once.
vocabularies.example:
I never drink coffee.
Never say never.
He never late.
quizzes: I ___________ drink hot coffee in the evening because I won't be able to sleep well tonight now.
You should ___________ say never because things can change very quickly in life, you'll see today.
He is ___________ late for school; he always arrives exactly ten minutes before the bell rings.

vocabularies.definition: Many times or at regular intervals.
vocabularies.example:
I often go to the library.
How often do you swim?
We often meet at the park.
quizzes: I ___________ go to the local library on Saturday mornings to find some interesting new books now.
How ___________ do you go for a swim in the sea when you are on holiday with your family today?
We ___________ meet at the green park to play football after we finish our school homework now.

vocabularies.definition: Not often; rarely.
vocabularies.example:
He seldom watches TV.
It seldom rains in the desert.
We seldom see them.
quizzes: He actually ___________ watches TV because he prefers to spend his free time reading or playing music.
It ___________ rains in the dry desert, so the plants there have to be very good at saving water.
We ___________ see our cousins because they live very far away in another country across the sea.

vocabularies.definition: At all times; every time.
vocabularies.example:
Always brush your teeth.
I'll always love you.
You are always late!
quizzes: You must ___________ brush your teeth before you go to bed and after you have your breakfast today.
I will ___________ love you and be there for you, no matter what happens in the future, my friend.
Oh no, you are ___________ late for our meetings! We have been waiting for you for twenty minutes.

vocabularies.definition: In the way that most often happens; normally.
vocabularies.example:
I usually wake up at six.
What do you usually do?
She usually wears a dress.
quizzes: I ___________ wake up at six o'clock every morning, but on Sundays I stay in bed a bit longer now.
What do you ___________ do after you finish your school homework? Do you play games or read books?
She ___________ wears a colorful dress when she goes to a party, but today she is wearing trousers.

vocabularies.definition: Occasionally; some of the time.
vocabularies.example:
Sometimes I feel lonely.
Sometimes it snows in April.
Come and see us sometimes.
quizzes: ___________ I feel a little bit lonely when my best friend goes away on holiday without me today.
___________ it actually snows in April, even though it is already spring and the flowers are out.
Please come and see us ___________ ! We would truly love to have a long talk and a cup of tea now.

grammars.description: Cách dùng các đại từ bất định như Someone, Anything, Nowhere.
grammars.example:
Is there anyone home?
I have something for you.
